modName: I wish I was in Dixie (Co-Op Defense)
description:
re are trading posts. Unfortunately, keeping them is not so easy, and after being burned by the natives, trading posts cannot be returned.

  • Redesigned resource system
    In the scenario, you don't have access to your home city. Why is experience necessary then? To train the colonial militia! It's simple: train the militia -> kill the enemy -> gain experience -> train more militia. By the way, you can train her directly from the estates.
    Excess food can be invested in Fusiliers - relatively fast and good shooting fighters. If they hadn't died from a couple of enemy arrows ...
    For gold, you hire bandits, and if you add a little more wood, then you can make a couple of useful improvements ## ### ######## Well, or improve the fortifications.
  • A set of small tasks to make it easier to complete
    Yes, there are full-fledged quests here. For example, if things get really bad, we will be offered to accompany the workers to the signal fire. If you ignore the request, the poor fellows will be cut by the savages who have arrived in time. But if you still cover them from enemy arrows and axes, then the whole team will receive a nice bonus in the form of a discount on the colonial militia.
    And if, despite all your efforts, the fort falls, you will have another chance to win - to run to the arriving boat and leave these mortal lands.

  • The world is in detail
    The fort is inhabited by special characters that you should sometimes talk to. Explore your surroundings, complete errands for your allies, fend off wild animals, collect treasures, find food caches, or simply enjoy the beautiful sunrise. Perhaps one day it will help you wait for reinforcements.

  • 5 full levels of difficulty
    Doesn't playing sandbox make you sweat anymore? Then turn on the "expert" and understand that now it has become impossible to win.

  • Half an Hour Heroic Defense&

Compared to previous versions of this mod, more limited changes vs default UI. Only adds red idle villager flag, transparent postgame, always-transparent ageup UI independent of new built-in transparency option.

Compilation of several other mods tweaked to my liking, plus mine:

  • Unlimited buildings: houses, outposts, castles and more.
  • Changed Pop cost: Depending of number of displayed units, ex. Horses 1 Pop, Falconet 1 Pop, mortar 4 pop.
  • Mercenary and Consulate armies: 0 pop cost, since they are foreigns.
  • Factory Card: 1 of the 2 factory cards now is unlimited, however now build limit is 4.
  • Livestock Pen: Added (shrine trickle) to Livestock Pen, it now produces Food/Gold (togeable) when animals are stocked, it also fatens them and they dont move after reaching limit, for balancing max limit is 4.
  • Spahi: is now trainable in Stable and Fort, Age 3. Can be upgraded to Guard and Imperial in stable.

Known Issues: 

Ai builds a Outposts too close of each other, due pop cost change will produce more canons, seems like doesnt use more than 2 factories, and doesnt use the new trickle mechanic of the livestock pens. Due balance issues Town Centers remain the same, bound to cards in order to create more, aswell as other buildings.

Future Plans:

  • Farm/Rice Paddy: Add (shrine trickle) to American natives and Asians.
  • Japanese Hunting: Remove hunting restriction and add trainable livestock.
  • Musketeers: Add them to Germans and Dutch, other mod does this already.
  • Outposts/Castles/Warhuts: Increase attack and cost.
  • Pop Limit: Add this mod too.
  • Merc Cards: tweak them to work like sweedish, to be able to train them in barracks/stable.
  • Ottoman Models: Change some models, settler to indian settler, use indian architecture in some buildings, change hussar model, add dragoon type of unit to stable.

You can now train Regulars and Volunteers when you revolt as the United States! They will replace the default gunpowder units for the civs that can revolt as the United States. For those who don't know, these units are locked behind a United States civ that's unobtainable other than using it in the Scenario Editor. I thought the US needed to be spiced up a little bit since it seemed a little basic compared to the other countries you could revolt to, so I added these units to make it more interesting!

The Regulars are the musketeer equivalent that have a ranged and melee bonus against cavalry, while the Volunteers are Skirmisher type units with longer range and a strong ranged bonus vs. heavy infantry and light ranged cavalry. They will replace certain units for each of the civs that can revolt to the United States.

British

  • Musketeers -> Regulars
  • Longbowman -> Volunteers

French

  • Musketeers-> Regulars
  • Skirmishers -> Volunteers

Swedes

  • Caroleans -> Regulars
  • Volunteers are simply added in

Dutch

  • Regulars are simply added in
  • Skirmishers -> Volunteers

I haven't tested this with multiplayer yet, but it should work with multiplayer if people have the same mod. This mod will likely need a restart of the game before it works. Let me know if there are any issues that need fixing!

This mod isn't compatible with most other mods. If you're having weird issues like stuff is inexplicably missing or just outright broken, disable your other mods! This should be less of a problem with the latest additive mods update, but who knows.

Overview

This mod adds a fully playable version of Hungary, and Romania, and The Commonwealth. Hope you enjoy playing with the new civs!

Hungary - Based on the 1848-49 Hungarian Revolution.

Romania - Based on 17th-19th century Romania

The Commonwealth - Based on 16th-19th century Poland/Lithuania
This mod allows you and your enemies to build unlimited population of all kind

v1.1 [READ - IMPORTANT]

Settlers cost 1 pop each. If you set settlers to be worth 0 pop; bots will not play and dont gather resources.

All other units cost 0 pop.

Build houses in order to increase your settler count. Technically the pop-limit is 200 settlers; which you shouldn't need.

I've increased the amount of resources that settlers gather by x2.5. This balances needing half the settlers to do the same work.

 

Tested on v 100.12.5025.0.

 

CHANGES

- Sets outpost population to 999
- All units cost 0 population (except settlers)
- Certain stats have been buffed for longer fights
- Mines, farms can have more settlers on each.
- Settlers gather 2.5x the normal speed

 

MANUAL DOWNLOAD

If you wish to manually download and install this mod; you may download it at:

https://mega.nz/file/yxRAHBBB#bpjZtLNnamytQQBp9NUFvBOzGI4vEiZRPF4dlAPlfXs

 

NOTES

Once subscribed, ensure you restart your game before starting a match. Also, ensure you aren't using other mods that modify population, etc (data mods). Mine and theirs may conflict.

This mod seeks to improve the AI in various areas and is a work in progress. All players in multiplayer need to have the mod enabled in order to play with one another. Do not run with other AI mods. If the file 'aiLoaderStandard' fails to load, try disabling other mods even if they are not AI-related. Typically, the game requires a restart after installing mods for the mods to work. This mod is for use in non-Campaign games. For more details regarding the mod, click here if you are viewing this on a web browser. Feel free to report any issues in the comments. A singleplayer and coop scenario of the 3rd Crusade. Up to 3 players.

Play as the English lead by Richard the Lionheart (Player 1), the French (Player 2) or what remains of Barbarossa's German expedition (Player 3).

Take the coastal cities of Acre, Caesarea, Arsouf and Jaffa. And force Salah ad-Din to negotiate with a trade monopoly.

You can play the scenario in the Load menu (main menu), in skirmish or in multiplayer (custom scenario).

NB: It's a large map with lots of detail. Be careful if you have a computer with low performance, especially in multiplayer. There is a different version of the scenario for cooperative games.

English

A singleplayer and coop scenario about the american civil war and inspired by the french comic "Les tuniques bleues" ("Bluecoats" in English).

Lead the Union army to the south, take the bridge, the fort, the train station and Atlanta city.

To defeat the confederates you must build three trading posts and start the trade monopoly.

-You can play the scenario in the Load menu (main menu) or in multiplayer.

Pour battre les confédérés, vous devez construire trois comptoirs et lancez le monopole commercial.

-Vous pouvez jouer le scénario depuis le menu Charger (menu principal) ou en multijoueur.

A singleplayer and coop (for 2 players) scenario about the start of the french revolution: the Storming of the Bastille.

July 12, 1789, the dismissal of the minister Necker, loved by the people, creates unrest in the Tuileries. The royal cavalry charges the crowd...

This attack amplified the people's anger. Looking for something to fight the king's men, they find weapons at the Invalides. But gunpowder was in short supply. They say there's some at the Bastille...

July 14, 1789, now armed and supported by soldiers loyal to the revolution, the Parisians begin their assault on the Bastille. The castle's garrison refused to give in and fired on the crowd. The ensuing escalation leads to the storming of the Bastille, a key date in the French Revolution.

NB: You can play the scenario in the "Load" menu (main menu) or in the skirmish map options.
No need to move any file, the scenario is automatically installed when you subscribe to the mod (it's placed at the end of the list in the in game scenario browser).

modName: [Broken] Aztec Ascension - aztec rework - by Neilug
description:
Aztec's situation is kind of bad with AoE3 right now, it's not a surprise. I think they lack variety after what they commit, and it's often either it works, either it fails. If you predict well their attack by scouting, or by looking at the cards, it becomes easier to counter, and then counter attack. So unlike most of other civs, they're not very playable competitively.

I offer here some ideas I've been thinking, solving all those problems, trying to give them more options in mid/lategame, and more strategy around a new building : the Aztec Temple.

--

Here some of the features you can expect : 

- new Building : Temple. You can train Skullknights and Warrior Priests.

Temple will offer new strategy where you may want to keep them alive at all cost, and where enemies will try to focus that first.

- more Midgame/Lategame options with new techs.

- new type of tech : choice. Choose between A or B, depending of the situation ofthe game.

- new shipments, updated ones, such as the Puma shipment down to 500 coins in age 2, delivering 6 Pumas Lancers.

 

--

WARNING : Strings and text aren't properly working right now, so all new techs have the same placeholder name and description.
You can read full workthrough here

A mini-campaign telling the story of the Nam-eun, a people who were forced to flee their homeland for a new world. Based on the setting of "The Red-Haired Maiden".

However, the natives of this land are willing to set aside their differences to drive the Nam-eun from their home. The Nam-eun must find a way to stake their claim, or they will never find a place in this new land.

This scenario features a unique technology tree and "home city" shipments to represent the Nam-eun and the "Nambu" (Majin) natives. This mod changes several data files controlling units and technologies. As of v4, it should not break with game updates any ####### ### this is still being tested.

This mod is for players dont want to run out of trees. Now each trees can provide 30 times more wood than original, thus 9000 per normal tree, 4500 per bamboo or small tree.

Somehow due to some bugs you have to check this mod in mod tool every time you restart the game, here is the solution:

1. go to C:\Users\(yourUserName)\Games\Age of Empires 3 DE\76561198193942981\mods

2. in local folder, create a new folder, name it as MoreWood (or any name you want);

3. in the folder you created, make a new folder called "Data";

4. go back to mods folder and go to subscribed -> 20333_30 Times More Wood -> data, and copy the protoy.xml file into the Data folder you created in step 3

5. in your mod tool uncheck the mod "30 Times More Wood" and check the mod you just created;

6. restart the game you should be good to go. 

also tested in multiplayer, just make sure all player did the same thing.

All civilizations have access to new powerful mercenary units: infantry, cavalry, artillery,  the captain (who acts like Daimyo) and several unique units, including STEAM TANK!

- You can buy new mercenary units in special building (Mercenary Company Office), and also purchase many powerful upgrades there.
- Every Explorer/Monk/Watchief can construct this new building, also they can train NEW Air Balloon, which moves VERY FAST and will help you to explore map and monitor enemies (it can auto-scout aswell).
- All new units cost gold only.
- All new units cost less at start, but will cost more after further upgrades (the stronger the units, the more you pay).
- All new units DO NOT auto-attack buildings untill you order them to (for better battlefield control), but they still respond to towers/TC attacks, etc. 
- All new units will not "run away" just to chase a random unit across the map. For that just MANUALLY select a tactic plan (Stagger/Volley or Bombard for artillery) and they will stop useless scattering.
- All new units have no penalties or bonuses against any unit class, so you can always know how much damage they can do.
- New Mercenary artillery is always in bombard mode, so it is always ready to fire and always moves at maximum speed.

  • Draugur AI for The Asian Dynasties 2008

What works right now:

  • A very different way of the AI making unit composition, complicated but effective 
  • Personality traits and specific behavoirs attached to those
  • AI will build proper forward bases
  • It will micromanage its units way better
  • Will build walls ... Well yes they used to. Now however that is also a personality trait which means there is a chance for specific countries to build one
  • It will defend its base way better then before and not just that also its allies bases including you the player. 
  • It will be much more aggressive
  • A different approach to unit movement and micromanagement. Also uses artillery and I am still working on this. But I am very happy with the progress
  • It will build its buildings competetively and also has code in it which enables unlimited population mods this if the modders change the maximum villagercount
  • Multiplayer compatible, only the host needs the mod
  • The rest is for you to find out 

Report any behavior that you think could be improved. If you got alot of knowledge regarding a civilization and you think the AI does this wrong? Let me know.

A new update will follow in the upcoming weeks as I managed to not only get some systems I was working on to work. On top of that I managed to improve on other things to. 

Works with 300 population mod, it will not use the 40 cards though. https://www.ageofempires.com/mods/details/297585/

Normal Pop Cap = 230 (250 for Chinese);
All Infantry = 1 pop;
Most Cavalry = 1 pop (Cuirassiers cost 2.);
Most Artillery cost a bit less pop;
Every Civ got buffed, some more than others;
More gold from mines and wood from trees. (Trees now have 500 wood and common mines 3000.);
Factories a little bit buffed. (5.5 gather rate to 6.5);
Stronger Walls and they cost a bit more. (5 to 7 Wood. 1000 more HP, even more after research bastion);
Defense buildings are stronger and have higher limits. (Usually from 7 to 10. Attack speed and damage buffed.);
Most merc units cost 1 pop;
All Imperial Units a little bit stronger.
Lots of changes in cards from all civilizations (NOT all cards.)
Also, making changes to the asian consulates.

 

Feel Free to give a recomendation for the mod as it is still a work in progress.

This is a simple mod that replaces the Definitive Edition UI flags for the Lakota (Sioux) and Haudenosaunee (Iroquois) with the more modern symbols of each tribe. In-Game flags are still kept blank as it was in Original and Definitive.

The Haudenosaunee flag "is the flag used to represent the six nations of the Haudenosaunee. It is a purple flag with four connected white squares and an eastern white pine tree in the center." -Wikipedia

The Lakota flag is a combination of the Definitive Flag and the Oglala Tribe Flag of Pine River Reservation.

I made this more as a test of flag creation and for personal use, but it's made public for those who want it.

Zombie Survival - LastZing!
Made by Drandel


Survive 10 waves of 'zombies' to beat the game!

You begin with a single (european) explorer. Each kill will grant you 2 coins which can be used to buy units. There are also upgrades available for your units. 

The scenario can be played alone, or in multiplayer. If you play solo, I recommend starting the game using the multiplayer lobby.

 

Set up:

Players 1-7 are humans, and Player 8 are the zombies.
P1-7 slots can be players, or bots. P8 slot should be any random bot and NOT a player.

The age should be set to 'Exploration Age' and nothing else.

Difficulty:

  • Easy/Standard - Unit Prices -25%
  • Moderate - Normal Prices, Zombies gain +1hp per wave
  • Hard -  Prices +10%, Zombies gain +1hp per wave, Zombies gain +0.01 speed/30 secs
  • Hardest/Extreme - Prices +25%, Zombies starting hp +10%, Zombies gain +1hp per wave

 

IF YOU CAN NOT FIND SCENARIO IN GAME
1. Go to: C:/Users/[YourName]/Games/Age of Empires 3 DE/[Numbers]/mods/subscribed/41929_zombie survival - lastzing/
2. Copy and paste the file into C:/Users/[YourName]/Games/Age of Empires 3 DE/[Numbers]/Scenario/

The mods folder is always correct, so just copy and paste the same file in .../Scenario/ for any future updates.


If there are any issues, please leave a comment in the discussion.
Alternatively, you may join the discord for feedback and/or updates on new scenarios!

This mods adds Argentina (United Provinces of the Río de la Plata) as a playable civilization both on single and multiplayer skirmish matches. For now, here's a list of the unique characteristics of Argentina:

  • You can train both Gauchos and Mounted Grenadiers and upgrade them in the stables.
  • Argentina has Urban Batallions, Patricios, Brown Corps, and Infernales as unique infantry units, all with custom icons, portraits, fully voiced and upgradable from the barracks:
    • Urban Batallions are extremely cheap but very weak units, similar to the strelets. Trainables in a batch of 10.
    • Patricios are militia musketeers, they performs exactly like the vanilla musketeer.
    • Brodébiles, similar a los Strelets, se pueden entrenar de a 10.
    • Los patricios son mosqueteros de milicia, se desempeñan exactamente igual que un mosquetero.
    • Los Cuerpos de Morenos son esclavos liberados que se unieron a la milicia. Se desempeñan exactamente igual que un guerrillero.
    • Los Infernales son poderosas unidades similares a un fusilero Nizam. Solo estan disponibles a partir de IV Edad.
  • Argentina comienza con menos cajas de recursos, pero con dos vacas gratis y una carreta de corral.
  • Argentina tiene algunas tecnologías y mejoras únicas.
  • Argentina puede reclutar nativos mapuches e integrarlos a su ejército. Tiene siempre disponible una embajada nativa para construir, desde donde puede entrenar una cantidad casi ilimitada de Garrotes de hierro mapuches y ##### ####### y puede mejorarlos, aunque cada unidad cuesta 1 de población. Argentina también puede entrenar agricultores mapuches, aldeanos únicos que son excepcionales trabajando en los molinos y las haciendas.
  • Argentina tiene nuevas cartas únicas en la metrópoli.

Tanto los lenguajes de Español como Inglés son compatibles. Posiblemente se añada el portugués en algún punto.

MAS CAMBIOS Y ADICIONES EN PROCESO.

Take to the seas in this expansive swash-buckling mod! Completely revamp your experience with new maps, new natives, and new units - all fully integrated with Assertive Seawall Naval AI for a complete single-player experience.

New Maps

Age of Pirates comes equipped with all-new random maps to explore, including land and water-based maps as well as new historical maps. The mod brings you to completely new regions like Australia and Oceania with unique fauna, resources, natives, treasures, and mercenaries. The current pool includes: 

Standard Maps

- Pirates of the Caribbean

- Corsairs of Mediterranean

- Burma Monasteries

- Aztec Gold

- The Dead Sea

- The First Cold War

- Pirate Wars

- Treasure Island

- Adriatic Sea

- Malta

- Mediterranean Islands

- Kuril Islands

- Green Valley

- Mississippi River

- The Canyon

- Australia

- New Zealand

- Hawaii

- Blue Mountains

- Eyre Basin

- Riverina

- Torres Strait

- Tasmania

- Polynesia

- Melanesia

- Micronesia

- New Guinea

- Cook Islands

- Great Barrier Reef

- Labrador Coast

- Unknown

Historical Maps

- Storming of the Bastille

- March to Versailles

- Fall of Venice

- Caribbean Wars

- King of Bohemia

- Crownlands

- Siege of Florence

- The Blood of Tenochtitlan

- Civil War

Seasonal maps

- Silent Night

- Grinch Mountain

You can find them by selecting "Custom Maps" from the drop-down menu as you set up a Skirmish.

New Natives

Native settlements in the Age of Pirates are greatly expanded on, transforming them into sub-civs with truly advantageous and unique bonuses and units. Whether it be the Pirate Flagships, an Academy, or Nemo's Submarine Fleet, you'll be turning the tide with your strategic choices.

Special Natives

Allow you to train ships directly in your trading post or use other special abilities

- Pirates

- Wokou

- Inventors

- Venetians

Other new Natives

- Maltese Order

- Jewish

- Orthodox Monastery

- Frontier Town (Western Town)

- Penal Colony

- Inuits (adopted from Age of the World mod)

- Maori (using Wars of Liberty voiceover)

- Aboriginal Australians (using Wars of Liberty voiceover)

- Korowai

- House of Auditore

- Hussites

- Prince-Electors

Extended Natives and native variations

- Aztec city

- Maya City

- Zen Great Buddha Monastery / Zen Mountain Monastery

- Sufi Great Mosque / Sufi Blue Mosque

- Jesuit Monastery (using assets from Archipáin’s Jesuit Alliance mod)

Seasonal and Historical maps only natives

- Christmas Village

- Grinch Mountain

- Sansculottes

New Naval AI

Age of Pirates comes fully equipped with Assertive Seawall Naval AI and custom support for added maps and settlements. From the re-imagined Caribbean to the coasts of Burma, all your favorite personalities will be there to hoist the sails and man the guns. 

Not only are new maps supported, but previously unplayable maps are supported as well! Both Archipelago and Ceylon-style maps are now playable in an alpha and beta state, respectively.  All the new historical maps are also fully supported by the AI.

How to play

This mod is not compatible with other data mods or AI mods. We recommend you disable other mods while this mod is enabled.

For Spanish translation, you can download a separate compatible mod.

To play the new maps, select Custom Maps from the drop-down on the Select Map screen while starting a skirmish. 

Custom scenarios (Map Editor)

It is possible to use the new natives (including all their special features like native politicians and ship training) in your custom scenarios. Check our Discord for more information about making the map for the Age of Pirates mod.

Team

- BaltazarGreat

- AssertiveWall20

- AlistairJah 

- Phoenixbursche

- InnateJaguar501

- vividlyplain

- Superleihwerker

Open positions

- Scenario creator (custom campaign)

- Translation into more languages (Spanish, French, and others…)

- Tester for all new features

- AI Developer (or tester)

- 3D Modelling and animations

- XML editor

Interested in joining the mod and contributing or testing the newest features in the pre-release? Contact a team member to join our Discord.

  • All other players (Players 1-7) should be on Team 1

modName: Population Buildlimit 4x Resources Fast Gather more
description:
Thought of uploading my modded ptotoy and techtree. What does it do?


-All Units cost 1 Population
-Buildlimit of all Units and Buildings removed, except the town center, set that one to 10.
-all resources multiplied by 4
-All Settlers Gather 5xfaster and gather from crates roughly twice as fast. this is a tech however and can be researched at the house from the respective Civ you play.
-all Military units are almost istant to train, this one is also a tech and can be researched at houses aswell.

The Techtree is modded as a Techtreemods.xml file, so it should be compatible with all other Techtree mods as it only adds 2 Techs. the Proto file however will replace all other proto mods you have installed, as it was a hassle to do everything as a protomods file. So you really can't have the one without the other, but if enough people want me to do this as an additive modded ptrotomods file I'll consider doing it.

The word #### ########## is normally assembled but the age of empire website censors the word if I assemble it for some reason.

[FR]

La Bataille de La #### ########## fut une action militaire de la guerre d'indépendance du Mexique qui eut lieu du 19 avril au 28 mai 1813, dans un village de la ville de Tonalá, dans l'État du Chiapas. Les insurgés dirigés par le général Mariano Matamoros y vainquirent les forces royalistes commandées par le général Miguel Dambrini.

Matamoros avait moins de 1 000 soldats pour affronter les forces royalistes de Miguel Dambrini . Dambrini, au courant de l'offensive des insurgés, entame une retraite vers le Guatemala . Lorsque les deux armées se rencontrèrent enfin à 6 kilomètres de Tonalá , Chiapas , les premières escarmouches eurent lieu se soldant favorablement par les forces rebelles.

[UK]

The Battle of the #### ########## was abattle of the War of Mexican Independence that occurred from 19 April through 28 May 1813 at a ranch at Tonalá, Chiapas. The battle was fought between the royalist forces loyal tothe Spanish crown, commanded by General Miguel Dambrini, and the Mexican rebels fighting for independence from the Spanish Empire, commanded by Mariano Matamoros y Guridi.

The battle resulted in a victory for the Mexican rebels. Matamoros had fewer than 1 000 soldiers with which to confront the royalist forces under Miguel Dambrini. Dambrini, knowing of the insurgent offensive began a retreat towards Guatemala. When the two armies finally met 6 kilometers from Tonalá, Chiapas, the first skirmishes took place resulting favorably for the rebel forces.
Large Armies and Natural Resources last longer

This mod allows for Large Armies by reducing the population cost for most units to 1. Some mercenary units cost no population giving you unlimited population. It also increases the number of native units that can be trained from each native post. Natural Resources last longer by providing more resources (e.g. silver mine now provides more gold. Unzip the file and then Copy this mod to C:\Users\user-name\Games\Age of Empires 3 DE\76561198004803521\mods\local. Then start the game, go to Tools, then Mods, and select Large-Armies as your only mod. This mod modifies the most current protoy.xml file so it will continue to work with new released patches and even with new DLC's.

modName: Battle of Kurikara The Fall of Heike(1183)
description:
After a practically three-year-long famine in Honshu, the main island of Japan, grand lord Taira no Kiyomori resolved to eradicate the pressing menace of their most indomitable enemy across decades——the clan of Minamoto——in Kurikara Toge. He sent his grandson, Taira no Koremori, the direct devisee of the deceased Shigemori, who had preferred liturgical dance to be a commander upon warfare, to bear commandership of the campaign. At this very critical moment, Minamoto no Yoshinaka acknowledged the importance of the imminent war between the two families and accepted the urgent call to lead an army against Koremori. 

Having been informed that the multitude of Heike's cavalries surpassed them astronomically, Yoshinaka, upon an immediate hour, formed a stratagem with the Sohei Clan in the eastern mountains. Will this subtle maneuver inflict upon the Clan of Heike a prodigious buffet? Will the battle of Kurikara terminate Heike's decade-long domination of Japan?

Note: If the Sohei Master failed to garrison the ship, feel free to adjust his position.

This is an older mod/custom map from 2005 some of you may remember.  I've taken some time to revive Fort Wars, so it now works on the Definitive Edition.  This also marks the 16th year since the first official release on January 28, 2006 and is a nice anniversary/send-off for the map.  Thanks everyone for their support over the years.  This (and the original Norse Wars game) made up a significant part of my childhood growing up, and there have been many good memories.

For those unfamiliar, the gameplay is based on Matei's Norse Wars from Age of Mythology, sort of the opposite side of the coin to games like DotA back in the day that existed as mods before League of Legends.  Fort Wars is the AOE3 adaptation of Matei's game but arguably is unique in its own right as well given years of development and refinement.

HOW TO INSTALL: Click Subscribe first.  Then in the game, go to Tools > Mods, find Fort Wars 5.0, and install it.  Fort Wars will show under Maps > Custom Maps.  Also clarification on Asian/DLC civs - the map won't break or put you at disadvantage, you just will be normal without any bonuses but also no weaknesses.

LIVE-STREAMED EVENT: Saturday Jan.29 2022 2:00PM PST

We'll also be having a kick-off event Saturday Jan.29 2022 2:00PM PST on live-stream to get everyone playing again and will hopefully make that a regular occurrence. Check out NorseWars.com and follow @NorseWars on Twitter for updates.  I'll probably be hosting games and hanging around online even before then, so feel free to add me.  It helps that AOE3 is old enough now that it is cheap (only $7) to download and install - if only just to play Fort Wars. :P The ladder doesn't work on AOE3 DE yet, so DE can be treated as unranked while formal ranked matches can be played on the older Fort Wars 4.1 for AOE3 (2007)/Complete on Steam (yes it still works!).

STANDALONE GAME:

Lastly, I will be working on turning this into a standalone game starting this year, so be on the lookout for that too.  Feel free to follow the progress at NorseWars.com and @NorseWars on Twitter, and I welcome all the help I can get to get that off the ground.

Welcome to Glory of Freedom - custom MOBA scenario for AOE 3 DE!

You still looking for an interesting scenario that can be played in multiplayer with your friends? You have already found it!

 THE MAIN FEATURES:

A complete MOBA game!

2 sides of the conflict, 3 lines, 1 standart explorer hero and thousands of possible variations for the development of your army. Pick your side and destroy enemy command center!

(Player 1-3-5 - British Colonists, Player 2-4-6 - Native Americans)

Redesigned resource system

In the scenario, you don't have access to #### ##me city. Why is experience necessary then? To train the army! It's simple: train the army-> kill the enemy -> gain experience -> train more army! You also can have up to three settlers to collect other resources neededfor important upgrades.

Unit type selection as part of your strategy

What about the army? You have only 20 population and 7 types of modified units. Your choise may be diferent - cheap pikemen or expensive janissaries? Fast rodeleros or slow free militia? Do you want to play with cavalry? Just train the pikeman and upgrade him to lancer in the stable! Play the way you see fit!

The world is in detail

The map is inhabited by special boss-characters that you should sometimes visit to. Explore your surroundings, complete errands for your allies, fend off wild animals, collect treasures, find food caches, or simply enjoy the beautiful landscape. Perhaps one day it will help you to win. Use hints to advance to the next age, capture a trading post to hire healers and arm your settlers into militia

 

How to install?

Extract GloryOfFreedomVX-X.age3Yscn to C:\Users\%Username%\Games\Age of Empires 3 DE\.........................\Scenario folder. Host the online game, choose the scenario mode and pick the map.

Paste bots players into the empty slots to start the game.

Beware the patrols are very dangerous, the map is available in three different difficulties. I took a lot of time to test the card to look for all the bugs, I fixed all those encountered, the card is playable and there are no worries but if there are any to send a message for me to fix. I tried to explain as best I can for the player

Biography :

Jack Powers, or John Power (1827 - 26 October 1860 in Calabasas, Arizona Territory), was an Irish-American bandit from southern and central California at the time of the gold rush.

In the 1850s, the robberies and murders committed by him and his gang made the part of El Camino Real, the historic route of the Spanish missions of California, located between San Luis Obispo and Santa Barbara the most dangerous road of California. His gang had the small town of Santa Barbara almost entirely under his control. He was finally expelled from the city but only after resisting the sheriff and a detachment of 200 men.

Grant some Experience upon AgeUp:

  • Colonialize = 150 XP
  • Fortressize = 300 XP
  • Industrialize = 600 XP
  • Imperialize = 1200 XP

Napoleonic Artillery Tactics.

This scenario focuses on artillery control. You will be given a set amount of resources, men, and cannons, so look after them well! Use your culverins to defeat the French Cannon to cross the river and take on the Fort.

To save this scenario, open the directory on the mod page, copy the file in the folder and paste it into your scenario folder in your game files.

This is my first published scenario, so hope it works well :)

How to install custom scenario : https://youtu.be/Dv0qkpEg8jY

My youtube channel : https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=84R2zH7cKnw

modName: Rio Grande do Sul - South America
description:
Summary

A map created by a native of the state of Rio Rrande do Sul - Brazil, the map includes regions of Uruguay and part of the Argentine province of Misiones.

About the map

The scenario tries to be as immersive as possible in relation to the local biomes.

The north is dense semi-temperate forests.

The northeast is a mountain range with Araucarias forest.

The east is a coastal region, and in the center south and west plains known as the pampa.

The map has Jesuit missions to the northwest, to the south it has indigenous villages.

The map has two railroads, one that comes from the north and goes to Argentina and another that comes from the north and runs along the entire coast.

A little bit of the history of Rio Grande do Sul

The region of Rio Grandedo Sul was the scene of many disputes between the Portuguese and the Spaniards, the Portuguese needed to have control of this region to be able to reach the silver river and the Spaniards, on the other hand, wanted to keep this region under control to prevent the Portuguese arrived at the Silver River.

Before these disputes the Jesuits settled in the region and catechized the indigenous people and founded indigenous cities governed by the Jesuit law, they were responsible for introducing cows and horses in the region, the first Jesuit settlements were attacked by bandeirantes (Portuguese pioneers) and because of this the cows and horses dispersed over the plains and lived wild and reproduced on a large scale, due to this the region was coveted by herds of horses and cows, the gauchos prosperedin this region due to these herds, the gauchos were Iberian cowboys and often mixed with indigenous people, they are considered the South American cowboys. 

 

Observation

I would like your feedback, together we can make this map more fun.

My discord - Nicktom#8814
Extreme Fortification

This mod transforms a variety of Home city cards that are weaker or underused into Techs that can be researched (if the civ has access to the card). The original cards are not removed because this would be highly incompatible with other mods and break the AI, and to not double the effects of the cards with the techs, all cards will give the corresponding tech for free. This mod is still being developed, as other techs could be added or removed. The cards transformed to techs included in the mod are:

  • The market cards (Advanced Market, Advanced Livestock Market, the asian market cards (they no longer ship a market builder), San Marcos Fair).
  • Some of the livestock cards (Stockyards, Ranchings, Barbacoa). Incas can research Llama lifestyle; Indians,The Mystical Syllable; Japanese, the Seven Lucky Gods and Africans, the Draft Oxen and Kilish Jerky.
  • Pioneers, Florentine Football, Alpaca Wool, Dambe Wrestling, Shiftas.
  • Improved Buildings, Stonemasons, Land Reforms, Monumental Architecture, Palace of Iturbide
  • Master Surgeons, Florence Nightingale, Agents, Assassins, Medicine Wheels, Autarky
  • Advanced Scouts, Advanced Wonders, Advanced Agriculture, Advanced Port, Advanced Dock
  • Germantown Farmers
  • Hegemony and Mass levies are also added, so Africans have around the same amount of researchable techs as other civs.
  • Steel bolts (I don't think it's a weak/underused card, but I wanted to make it available to other civs)

Any feedback would be appreciated (my discord is Zaolat#6901).

Introduction to OligarchyMod by bendeguz200 and VitezVaddiszno

Requires ALL expansions - US, Mexico, African civs AND Mediterranean civs to work!

Make sure to update the mod regularly - we do! The Change List is no longer updated. Fresh patchnotes are posted on reddit.com/r/OligarchyMod instead!

Description:

OligarchyMod is chiefly a work of satire in video game form, not a video game mod with satirical themes. Therefore, balance has been relegated to the background to leave room to the message. Play the mod with this in mind. This does not mean we refuse to balance anything, we just don't let tryhardness get in the way of fun. Also we've never touched Ranked in our lives and instead delight in team games against the bot.

This mod adds an entirely new civilization into the game named Oligarchy. Oligarchy is not like other civilizations, in more ways than you'd think. Itmakes use of Influence, but unlike its more advanced African siblings, Oligarchy uses it to play petty and sociopathic political games with it that often lead to its citizens drawing the short straw.

In real life, Hungary held a national election on the 3rd of April, 2022, with a landslide Fidesz-KDNP victory. In light of this completely irrelevant fact, OligarchyMod's first version was published on the 2nd of April, 2022. And in Oligarchy, you decide who wins the elections!

OligarchyMod contains many, many inside jokes that only Oligarchians will understand, but hopefully that won't take away gameplay enjoyment from other people who are living outside the borders of our wondrous utopia.

OligarchyMod does not shy away from sharply jabbing anything and everything related to Oligarchian culture. We, however, wear the fact that we jab every entity equally as a badge of honor. Except the Two-Tailed Dog Party. We can't satirize somethingthat's already satirical, so they got off easy.


Civilization features:

  • Uses Influence
  • Starts with a Parliament, a special Capitol that is available at all ages and trickles Influence.
  • Starts with an Ivory Tower, a special building which trickles Experience.
  • Every Election (age-up) grants you 2 HC cards and 2 new techs at the Parliament and the Ivory Tower each, but the deck is limited to 21 cards.
  • Many default HC Cards like 700 Wood are disabled or replaced with a weaker version.
  • You choose from 5 different Parties in every Election. A party may be chosen again in subsequent Elections. Parties grant thematical bonuses.
  • Starts with no crates, but 1 Orange Orchard Wagon and 1 Alcoholic.
  • The Hero unit is the Oligarch General, who'sa cross between anExplorer and an American General - he builds Town Centers and Trading Posts, and cannot use Inspiring Flags, but has American-style Charged abilities and increased combat capabilities.
  • Can train Ships but no military units - they are available through Elections.
  • Map-specific Outlaws are disabled. The only military unit available at first is the Alcoholic - a cheap and weak hand infantry Outlaw from the Tavern, who is good at mugging Villagers, bad at fighting Guardians, can gather Crates and Treasures, and be upgraded in many ways.
  • Church and Artillery are disabled by default and may be made available with party bonuses.
  • Frigate and Monitor are disabled
  • Saturated with unique mechanics and is basically five civilizations packed into one.

NOTE: this mod is absolutely not designed for scenarios whereyoudon't start in Exploration Age with your usual units, like Post-Industrial/Imperial. The way the cards and techs are designed basically breaks the civ if you don't play normally. Do so at your own risk.

Empire Wars, however, is now supported!

This mod is probably NOT compatible with other mods that add new content like civilizations. Map mods and whatnot may be okay.


This mod is right for you if you:

  • Live in Oligarchy, or a culturally adjacent country
  • Love the Oligarchian political parties
  • Hate the Oligarchian political parties
  • Like unique mechanics in a civilization

This mod is not right for you if you:

  • Want a normal experience
  • Want a balanced Ranked experience
  • Don't caremuch about Oligarchy or politics
  • Are the morose type (but this might cheer you up actually)

Our deadline has pushed us to release this mod a little unpolished, but we will continue working on it and update it, especially if official patches break some functionalities.

Upcoming features:

  • Fixing bugs
  • Polishing
  • Balancing (based on user input)
  • Modelling (this will come later, it's HARD!)
  • A real homecity

We have unlimited gratitude towards:
Ropi336, for his invaluable help with modding
AngelsSaint, for his invaluable help with texturing
KevSoft for his help with modelling, although we haven't done anything with it yet

Also keep an eye Ropi336's Eastern Europe Modtoo - his is much more balanced and historically fitting than this mod, and his mod inspired this one! It's sadly outdated now, but maybe he updates it in the future!

Napoleonic Wars:

This mod has made significant changes to how cavalry work and many other features of the game. The civs that benifit most from this mod are the 5 major powers during the Napoleonic wars. Austria (Old Germans), British, France, Prussia (new civ), and Russia. Spain was modernized to be a more light infantry civ rather than an archaic one and Sweden was altered a little to adjust for the changes thate were made to the general game play. The minor players; Dutch, Italy, Ottoman and Portugal received maybe one or two changes. 

Some Key Changes: Changed the Swedish Case Shot card to be granted by Advance Arsenal. Cavalry are no longer the damage soak that they were. Lancer cavalry and Cuirassiers have been granted to other civs. Stances for Infantry units have purpose as Volley = Line, Stagger = Skirmish, Defensive = Square, Melee = Column. Reworked Grenadiers into a Sapper unit (a builder/demo unit) and added Grenadiers to be similar to Soldados. There are more changes and would love to hear feed back about this. 

If this is too drastic of a change and would like just Austria and Prussia there is a minimal version of this mod here: https://www.ageofempires.com/mods/details/59564/

I still have the original mod if too many people are wanting the old mod back if need be. 

I would like to give a shout out to those who I was able to look at their work and learn how to mod or get ideas for what changes to make.

The ability of recruiting guardians can convert any type of treasure guardians.

KOREANS
-Civilization bonus: Irregulars and Sentries can be trained from villages and slowly regenerate hit points, starting from the Commerce age. Can't train cavalry until Fortress Age, but can train Rattan Shields (Fast Infantry) at the Monastery in the Commerce Age. Can get the aid of Chinese allies through cards and the Summer Palace.

Korean units:
-War Academy: Yumi Archer, Qiang Pikemen, Changdao Swordsmen, Arquebusier.
-Stable: Iron Flail, Yabusame.
-Castle: Flaming Arrow, Hand Mortar, Hwacha (Organ Gun).
-Dock: War Junk, Turtle Ship (Atakabune), Panokseon (Tekkousen).
-Other Units: Flying Crow, Shaolin Master, Disciple. Woldo Riders (Naginata Rider) and Meteor Hammers can only be obtained from the Home City.

-Korean buildings: Village, Rice Paddy, Monastery, Castle, Consulate, War Academy, Stable.
-Wonders: Confucian Academy, Porcelain Tower, Summer Palace, Temple of Heaven and White Pagoda. (Same as the Chinese)
-Consulate Allies: British, Russian, French and German. (Same as the Chinese)
NOTE: The reason why I use the same Wonders and consulate allies as the Chinese is because these mechanics are something difficult to modify.

-Historical Context: The Korean Kingdom of Joseon managed to survive and retain its autonomy despite the ambitions of several external powers for over 500 years.

UNIQUE CARDS:
-Hwarang Cavalry Volunteers (II): You receive a free Woldo Rider with all future shipments, starting with this one.
-Righteous Army (II): Ships 10 Irregulars. Ships 1 aditional Irregular for every Villager you have lost (up to a maximum of 20).
-Early Flaming Arrows (II): Ships a Flaming Arrow. Allows Flaming Arrows in Commerce Age.
-Korean Village Defenses (II): Villages shoot when garrisoned. Town Center attack increased.
-3 Hwacha (III, 200c): Ships 3 Hwacha.
-Turtle Hulls (III): Increases the hitpoints of Atakabunes and Tekkousens.
-Han Allied Army (II, 500c): Ships 9 Qiang Pikemen and 8 Chu Ko Nus.
-Mongol Allied Army (II, 500c): Ships 7 Steppe Riders and 6 Keshiks.
-Ming Allied Army (III, 1000c): Ships 15 Qiang Pikemen, 11 Keshiks, and upgrades them to Disciplined status.
-Qing Allied Army (III, 1000c): Ships 15 Chu Ko Nus, 11 Steppe Riders, and upgrades them to Disciplined status.
-Chinese Allied Army (IV, 2000c): Ships 12 Qiang Pikemen, 12 Chu Ko Nus, 8 Steppe Riders, 8 Keshiks, and upgrades all of them to Honored status.


V2 UPDATE ------------------------------------------------------------------

-1 Hwacha + 1 Castle Rickshaw (III)
-European Artillery Engineers (III, 400export): Ships 2 Falconets and an Arsenal Wagon with 4 artillery technologies.
-Eupseong (III, 300w): Ships a Town Rickshaw. Town Centers gain a powerful Cannon Attack that does not require garrisoned Settlers to be used.

V3 UPDATE ------------------------------------------------------------------

UNITS
-Changdao Swordsman: Cost increased from 50f/30c to 50f/35c
-Iron Flail: Cost changed from 110f/90c to 100fc
-Woldo Rider: Now only upgrades automatically in age III. Can be upgraded further at the stables with reduced cost
-Meteor Hammer: No longer upgrades automatically each age, now can be upgraded at the stables with reduced cost
-Hwacha: Upgrades names renamed to "Heavy Hwacha" and "Exalted Hwacha"
-Atakabune: Renamed to "Turtle Ship"
-Tekkousen: Renamed to "Panokseon"

CARDS

-Yumi Archer cards: Now can be sent TWICE
-Yabusame cards: Now can be sent TWICE
-Flaming Arrow cards: Now can be sent TWICE
-European Artillery Engineers: (III) Cost decreased to 300export (from 400e)
-Fire Dragon Pyrotechnics: (IV) Renamed to "Shinkichon". Now cost 1000c. Ships 1 Flying Crow, 1 Flaming Arrow and 1 Hwacha. Slightly improves their rate of fire. Also improves Hwacha area damage radius.

NEW CARDS

-Pengbaesu Army (III, 1000f): Ships 16 Rattan Shields and enables them to be trained at War Academies. Dim Mak tech (Rattan Shields get +50% attack) can be researched at the Monastery.
-Gabsa Army: Card removed
-Gaemamusa Army (IV): Ships 4 Woldo Riders and 4 Iron Flails. Enables the Gaemamusa Army to be trained at the Stables. Also enables the Gabsa Army (Woldo Riders + Meteor Hammers) if the "Forbidden Army" card is sent.
-Forbidden Army (IV): Ships 4 Meteor Hammers and 4 Iron Flails. Enables the Forbidden Army to be trained at the Stables. Also enables the Gabsa Army (Woldo Riders + Meteor Hammers) if the "Gaemamusa Army" card is sent.

-Chinese Frontier Troops: Enables Castles to train and upgrade the Standard, Ming and Mongolian Army. Chu Ko Nu, Qiang Pikemen, Keshik and Steppe Rider attack and hitpoints greatly increased, but they are also more expensive.

+Improved Wonders (I): Improves the Bonuses of each Wonder.
-Confucian Academy: Flying Crows spawn time -10%
-Porcelain Tower: Adds Bullionism tech (700e, Gives 1,500 XP)
-Summer Palace: Banner Armies spawn time -10% (This applies to ALL Banner Armies)
-Temple of Heaven: Adds Nanban Trade tech (325e, Crates and Trade Routes contain/generate +25% resources)
-White Pagoda: Adds native Shaolin Temple Technologies

-Chinese Expeditionary Fushuan (IV): Ships 1 Fushuan that can train some Banner Armies. (Note: The Korean Fushuan can train the "Old Han Army", "Standard Army", "Ming Army", and "Mongolian Army").

La bataille de ########### ### une bataille militaire qui se déroula le 26 octobre 1813 durant la Guerre de 1812 entre la colonie britannique qui allait devenir le Canada et les États-Unis.

Cette bataille est considérée comme l'un des hauts faits d'armes de l'histoire canadienne. En effet, le 26 octobre 1813, 300 miliciens, les Voltigeurs canadiens, ainsi que 22 Amérindiens, commandés par Charles-Michel de Salaberry, ont repoussé une force américaine de 7 000 hommes

[UK]

The Battle of the Châteauguay was a military battle that took place on October 26, 1813, during the War of 1812, between the British colony that would become Canada and the United States.

This battle is considered one of the great feats of Canadian history. On October 26, 1813, 300 militiamen, the Voltigeurs canadiens, and 22 Amerindians, commanded by Charles-Michel de Salaberry, repelled an American force of 7,000 men.

Take control of a small outpost and defend against 15 escalating waves of attackers! How long can you survive?

 

To install the scenario, copy it from your mods directory into your scenario directory. Something like:

\Age of Empires 3 DE\(numbers)\mods -> Age of Empires 3 DE\(numbers)\scenario

Just Prussia version 3.0
---------------------------
This will probably be the last major update that the mod will receive. I will try and keep it up to date and make little tweaks, 
but I think I'm satisfied with how the mods is right now. And so may I present:

Just Prussia Mod!

As a big fan of the Napoleonic Wars, I've always wanted to recreate the battles from history with the nations of France, Britan, Austria, Prussia, and Russia. And now you can too!
In this mod I have split the original Germany up into the Habsburg Monarchy or Austria (Original Germany) and Prussia, an all-new civ. When I first created this mod I had
a lot more tweaks to the modded civs and to multiple other balance changes. Over time I have refined it down to this version.

Habsburg Monarcy (Germany): Maria Theresa (Modding AI requires to overwrite the original code so I can't put the AI for this in the mod)
Civilization Bonus: Unchanged
Unique Units: Doppelsoldners, War Wagons, Settler Wagons, Uhlans (Shared with Prussia), Landwehr (shared with Prussia).
Royal Guard: Grenzers (Skirmishers), Czapka (Uhlans). Grenzers from the Hungarian Revolt are now called Honved
Germany is almost how it is from the base game other than the cards. The idea behind Habsburg Monarcy was to give them a more multinational feel and fill in some of their holes.

Prussia: Frederick the Great
Civilization Bonus: All units receive an increased to movement speed.
Unique Units: Schutze, Freikorps, Totenkopf Hussars, Uhlans (shared with Habsburg) and Landwehr (shared with Habsburg).
Royal Guard: ######## Schutze (Schutze) and Towarzysz (Uhlan)
Prussia is a very military based civ. They are very similar to Britain as some of their strongest units are Musketeers and Grenadiers. They are slower to get going but once their professional army has been establish they will dominate the batttlefield.
Their abundance of mining enhancing has the ability to speed them up as well as cards like Professionalism allows you to convert your military focus into an economic boost. 
Due to the Discipline that the Prussian instills in its troops, but knowing when to back out is important too because replacing elite troops can become quite expensive.

Schutze: A rifle light infantry that is halfway between a skirmisher and a musketeer. They have an damage boost against all forms of infantry.
Freikorps: A skirmisher that sacrifices damage for health. The opposite of a Cassadore.
Totenkopf Hussars: A Prussian heavy cavalry (similar to a Spahi) that can only be obtain through shippments that intimidates enemy lines and reduces their max health. 

There are a few other changes I have made to make it feel more like fighting in the Napoleonic wars. If you are intereseted in these changes you can find this mod if you search Napoleonic Wars. https://www.ageofempires.com/mods/details/55191/

I would like to give a shout out to those who I was able to look at their work and learn how to mod or get ideas for what changes to make.

This is a mod which adds 14 Civilizations, normally restricted to single-player content, into multiplayer: Barbary Pirates, Black's Mercenaries, British East India Company, Canada, the Falcon Company (Black Family Estate), the Hole in the Wall Gang, Japanese (Campaign), the Knights of Saint John (Malta), Morocco, Native Americans, Pirates, Somalis, Tatars and The Circle of Ossus. More to come?

 

This builds of the work of a previous mod, but fixes bugs, adds features, adjusts balance, and hopefully makes the civs more fun to play in all game modes. Big thanks to the people on the forums and official discord who have helped me with my questions.

 

Note: since this mod makes changes to several campaign civs, I'd reccomend turning this mod off when playing campaign missions to keep the experience as close to the originals as possible.

Napoleonic Epic Battle Scenario!

Welcome Players!

This scenario is focused on the late game and treaty style play.

It is a large-scale battle that is focused on destroying weapons caches to control the battlefield.

You are the British and the French, your enemy, will have better units, so you will need to upgrade your own to keep up.

You can't build walls or outposts, but you already have a base set up. You can only make a limited number of cannons, the French also have this restriction.

Advance to the Imperial age to get better units and eco techs.

Keep making settlers till you reach 99. (Start with 39).

Towards the end of the game, after you have destroyed theweapons caches, the AI will just start spamming units, so just push forward to the town center with petards. (Make sure you have completed the other main objectives first).

This is my second published scenario, so sorry if it's not as smooth as all that.

To save this scenario, open the directory on the mod page, copy the file in the folder and paste it into your scenario folder in your game files.

Enjoy!

UPDATE 2022/06/13: Enabled Socket Bayonet research at Arsenal for Dutch and Germans. I incorrectly assumed it would be (they even get the tech for free in Post-Industrial and Post-Imperial starts for some reason), but it's fixed now.

Enables Musketeers for the Dutch and Germans, with different prerequisites. Dutch need to send the Religious Freedom Home City card and then send the Blue Guards shipment from the Church. Germans need to get to Age 3. Germans also get Veteran Musketeers for free upon reaching Age 3 (which is standard for age 3 units, but just mentioning it in case).

The Dutch "Infantry Combat" and "Infantry Attack" cards will affect Musketeers. The German "Long-Range Infantry Hitpoints" card will affect Musketeers as well. Dutch Blue Guards have a Royal Guard upgrade that changes them to Imperial Dutch Guards, Germans keep standard Imperial Musketeers (I'm not against giving them an RG, just couldn't think of any neutral (aka wouldn't conflict with future mods/DLC that add Prussia/Austria) terms for a German Royal Guard Musketeer.)

Post-Imperial Dutch as well as both Post-Industrial and Post-Imperial German Musketeers come with their upgrades enabled, as is standard.

modName: Dutch Russian Ottoman Consulate for Indians and Chinese
description:
Enables Dutch consulate (with working Arsenal, Church, Bank with normal upgrades) for Indians and Chinese.

Also enables Russian consulate for Indians and Ottoman consulate for Chinese.

Regarding the Rifling tech available in the Dutch consulate Arsenal, the cards Royal Green Jackets and Repelling Volley have been modified to remove their damage multipliers against heavy infantry for units that benefit from Rifling (to avoid a potential situation where someone has 4x HI Gurkhas or Arquebusiers).
Simplified Version of the Venetian Flag for better in-game view
Pirate Flag for French Haiti Revolution

Train consulate armies from the Castle. Works for all vanilla TAD civs (without exception) and is by default compatible with my other mod Dutch Russian Ottoman Consulate for Indians and Chinese

Be aware that all consulate armies in production or queued will be immediately canceled as soon as End Relations completes.

Update 09/28/2022: thanks to mayosrojas and 热心市民刘先生 for their bug reports: French and British consulates fixed for Japan, Russian factory shipment now allows Japan to produce Heavy Cannons and India to produce Rockets from the factory. Blockhouse units added for Japan. Dutch church "Standing Army" upgrade now gives -10% to Banner Army train time, "Mass Cavalry" now gives -15% Banner Army train time.

China, India and Japan now have access to all consulate options. 

German consulate bonus changed to -15% Banner Army food cost and -10% Infantry and Cavalry food cost (bonuses do not stack for China, unless the Banner Army system is bypassed)

Japanese consulate name changed to Nagasaki Trade for the non-Japanese civs, End Isolation renamed to End Relations with Bakufu as well as the rollover text.

I'd recommend against using this mod with others that unlock consulates (like my other mod Dutch Russians Ottomans Consulate for Indians and Chinese) as it's redundant (it's mostly the same code anyway). This mod CAN be used and is perfectly compatible with Consulate Armies From Castle, however.

Regarding the Rifling tech available in the Dutchconsulate Arsenal, the cards Royal Green Jackets and Repelling Volley have been modified to remove their damage multipliers against heavy infantry for units that benefit from Rifling (to avoid a potential situation where someone has 4x HI Gurkhas or Arquebusiers).

I haven't fully tested this mod, so if you noticeany issues let me know.

Mahout Lancers gain a Stampede attack similar to the Royal Horseman's Stampede when the Tame Elephants card is sent from the Home City.

This is the "normal" version, which means that Mahouts can't break trees and have a base Stampede damage of 4 that maxes out at 9 (all cards, exalted, no Team cards). I have no idea if this is actually balanced, so feel free to make your own version if you need to (or just let me know what balanced numbers for this would be and I can change it).

Minor update: It looks like treasure guardians can't be aggro'd with the Stampede (the flag that keeps it from destroying trees seems to be the culprit). It works fine on everything else though. If this is a huge deal for you, the other mod (OP version) will aggro treasure guardians you walk near with Trample on.

For the true elephant enthusiasts out there. 

Mahout Lancers gain a Stampede attack similar to the Royal Horseman's Stampede when the Tame Elephants card is sent from the Home City.

 

This is the overpowered version, which means that Mahouts CAN break trees (and knock them down on contact) and have a base Stampede damage of 5 that maxes out at 12 (all cards, exalted, no Team cards). As with the normal version, I have no idea how good this makes the unit, (I just figured being able to grief other players by knocking down trees was bad) so feel free to make your own version if you need to, or just let me know what good numbers for this would be and I can change it.

The tooltip looks misleading, that's due to how the game localizes certain effects (the text is the same even if you're disabling the KM buff).

Imperial Age India can swap Karni Mata's weak 10% base rate gathering buff for a map-wide 20% gathering buff plus an extra 5% for rice paddies. I chose 20-25% with some very quick maths (I started a Post-Industrial game, teched to Imperial with KM, sent all relevant to Imperial Age economy cards (check screenshots) and then took 10% of that rate) which equated to roughly 20% of the base rate. It's not a perfect ratio since different resources collect at different rates, but the primary goal of having a viable lategame economy (in terms of % buffs at least) is achieved. Villagers end up collecting slightly (~0.02 resource/tick difference) faster than fully carded and upgraded Chinese villagers (1.25 vs 1.23 Food for example).

If anyone has any opinions on how to balance this better feel free totell me. Hopefully this mod won't be necessary in the future (if they add current-rate gathering multipliers).

Incidentally, you can delete your Karni Mata after researching the permanent buff, in case you wanted to free up space in the base.

This was initially intended to be for all TAD civs and then I realized China has Confucius' Gift and Japan has Spanish consulate's Merchant Marines tech (which this mod is directly based on, and I still didn't realize). Somehow, I ended up making another India buff mod. I briefly considered having this tech lock out those other techs if researched (and vice-versa), but this buff is strictly worse than either of those two options, so I decided against it.

Saw this mentioned as a concept and it seemed pretty interesting. I'll probably release a version for every other civ at some point but I'm more likely to implement it as a researchable tech ingame (adding cards is a lot of busywork).

Card is named Akbarid Reforms, age 4 card, sends 1 Villager/2 Sepoys as standard.

In the process of testing my last few mods I ended up actually using elephants for the first time and I realized that they handle incredibly badly. I might be a decade late to the pathfinding party but hopefully this mod helps. It "feels" better to me personally, but I am far from an expert on this so if any experienced India players could let me know if this is placebo or not, that would be appreciated.

Elephant "obstructionradiusx" and "obstructionradiusz" standardized to 0.7900, same as every other cavalry unit in the game. Elephants are normally 0.4900 obstructionradiusx and 0.9900 obstructionradiusz, which to my extremely untrained eye looks like it forms some sort of rectangular shape in contrast to the uniform dimensions used by the much better handling non-elephant cavalry.

The actual physical dimensions of the unit model seem smaller as well, but I'd rather not think about the balance implications of that, so long as the pathfinding works.

modName: Age V for Revolutions (Updated for Revolutionary France)
description:
03/29/2023: Thanks to FSlavik for the report, Popular Sovereignty is now available for Revolutionary France. Mod is updated. Other reports of revolutionary civs I've missed would be appreciated.

 

Adds a tech to the Town Center upon revolting called Popular Sovereignty (2500 food 2500 gold), which advances the revolutionary civ to Imperial Age.

I had a really strange idea when I realized that the 99 settlers per slot in a 4v4 game probably lag the game a lot. Well, this mod doesn't really solve that problem, but now you can eventually (by Age IV) transition your settler economy to an autogathering (torp-style) grid of buildings. 

Research the first tech (Automated Resource Extraction) at your Market in Age IV to get started.

 

Tech Tree

Age III:

Building: Hunter's Lodge

-Collects from crates, Cherry Orchards, Mango Groves and Swidden Fields at a slow rate. (35 range)

Ability: Bighorn Herd (300 second cooldown)

-Generates a charge every 100 seconds. When the ability is used, expends all accumulated charges to spawn 3 Bighorn Sheep (huntable) per charge. Max 7 charges.

Tech: Bison Herd (8000 Gold)

-Spawns a herd of 3 Domesticated Bison (50 Food initial 800 Food maximum, herdable) every 90 seconds until you have at least 15 bison.

Hunter's Lodge (only constructable through Imperial Wagon in Age III): Economic Exploitation (350 Wood 350 Gold)

-Hunter's Lodge now has a ranged attack (huntables only) and can gather from any farm (including Livestock Pens) buildings, herdables, huntables, trees, berry bushes and mines.

Age IV:
Market/Hunter's Lodge: Expanded Resource Extraction:

-Enables Resource Extraction Camps (Resource Extraction Wagon) and Resource Wagons at the Market and Hunter's Lodge

Building: Resource Extraction Camp

-Functions identically to a Hunter's Lodge, but is unarmed. Overlap resources with multiple Resource Extraction Camps to maximize resourcegeneration rate (but also run out of resources quicker).

Unit: Resource Wagon (500 Food 500 Wood 500 Gold)

-3 minute training time expensive Wagon that can turn into a Swidden Field, Mango Grove or Gold Mine (5000 of Food, Wood Gold respectively). Can also turn into a Cherry Orchard. Research Resourcing techs at your extraction buildings to reduce train time.

Tech: Increase Resourcing I-V

-Decreases Resource Wagon train time, increases the gather rate and # of resources gathered at once for Resource Extraction Camps and Hunter's Lodges. Enables Resource  Wagon spawning at the Hunter's Lodge. Cost of this tech increases every time it is researched.

 

Essentially, there's a high upfront cost and time investment but it becomes absurdly overpowered after you get past the initial setup. Onething I didn't realize but ended up enjoying is the small amount of decisionmaking required in planning your buildings out to overlap, since things like mines require a lot of space around them, and it's easy to block yourself off. 

This is very much a work in progress, I'm not really sure what I was going for with this but it ended up doing something pretty neat. Although I guess it does theoretically solve the Mill collision issue, by removing the need for Mills, or even Settlers (although the extraction buildings do generate a moderate food trickle off of nearby Mills).

There's almost guaranteed bugs that I haven't seen, so please let me know if you see any. The surprisingly large scope of this mod (it started out as a building that called in resource wagons) means I haven't been able to test it much, and the areas this kind of mod is liable to be most broken is when it's leveraged to boost a settler economy (which it is quite goodat, considering the Mill bonus and free hunts)

Overview

June 18th, 2022, marked the 210th anniversary of the beginning of the War of 1812, when the United States of America declared war against the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland and the dependencies thereof. The war was fought across British North America, with multiple battles along the Canadian frontier and included American invasions of Upper and Lower Canada, as seen in the Historical Battle scenario of the Battle of Queenston Heights.

This mod makes the Canadian Militia civilization from the Queenston Heights battle into a playable civilization for other game modes, and additionally expands upon them to give them unique units, techs, cards, and politicians based on Canadian history, primarily the War of 1812. 

This mod does not alter any of the original game's civilizations, nor does it affect the Battle of Queenston Heights as everything should be applied to my Canadians only. For multiplayer all players must have the mod installed and toggled on. This mod may have issues with other mods, particularly if they also have Canadians and used "Canadians" as the civilization file name. This mod is in English only, but it does have English string files for other languages so you can at least read something instead of empty voids.

Steam Users: For whatever reason the Steam Cloud does not like civilization mods, and that seems to be the main cause of them not working. If you disable Steam Cloud for Age of Empires III DE you should be able to play this and other mods that add additional civilizations. You may need to delete any glitched sp_Canadians_homecity files out of  C:\Users\USERNAME\Games\Age of Empires 3 DE\NUMBERS\Savegame after disabling the Steam Cloud.

Features

Civilization bonus: Can establish a Fur Trading Company which provides a Charter Bonus, generates furs (XP), has unique technologies to research, and has an extra type of Settler - Voyageurs - which can be trained from the Company.

Royal Guard units: Provincial Militia (Voltigeur de Québec), Hussar (Princess Louise's Hussar)

Unique Units: Fencible, Voyageur, Voyageur Canoe, First Nation Auxiliary

modName: Unique Church Techs Always Available (Updated, works for USA)
description:
UPDATE 2022/06/23: Added the USA unique techs, apologies to the state of Pennsylvania.

Makes the Royal Decree card (unique Church/Mosque) techs available by default for every civ that has them. I attempted to include Maltese and Italians but I have no way to test it so if there's any issues with those two let me know.

Note that this mod does not remove the Royal Decree cards themselves, as that will also remove them from any vanilla decks you may have.

Introduction

AoW European Risk is an 8 player Free for All Custom Scenario. AoW European Risk is designed to be played at [FFA] and [Post Imperial] settings. The main goal to achieve victory in the European Risk Scenario is by conquering all of Europe. There are 8 available civilizations to choose from. Depending which civilization, your pick will determine the country in which you spawn. Each civilization starts with 3 explorers and 30,000 wood.

➤ How to Play the Game

Before starting the game make sure it is set to [Free for All] and the start age is [Post-Imperial Age]. No player can choose the same Civilization twice. The game holds 8 players with 8 Civilizations to choose from. No Civilization can be picked twice.

Each country has trade posts scattered all over their prospective regions. You start the game with 3 explorers placed in 3 key locations within your region. Use the explorers to build Trade Posts. Trade Posts will provide unique imperial units to the country in which it’s located. Part of obtaining victory requires players to ensure all trade posts are built and you are efficiently micro-managing your units to properly mount an offensive or establish a defensive strategy against your neighboring enemies. The more Trade Posts you have, the more army you will produce; the more Trade Posts you have, the bigger target you place on yourself.

➤ Micro intensive gameplay

AoW European Risk is designed with slow spawn timers to create a required attention to micro throughout the game. Unit positions, perfectly timed invasion landings, and efficient use of naval power is what separates AoW European Risk from the rest. Your attention to efficiently micro your army, leveraging your position of power, and making strategic alliances will have a tremendous impact on your ability to wage war with success. Fail to address one of the things just mentioned and you will find yourself in a very uncomfortable position.

➤ Imperial Units

The game is designed to be played at [Post Imperial]. This is purposely designed to provide a strategy behind your choice of attacking a specific country. To understand this better position yourself as a French player. As a French player you have Britain in the north, Germany to your East, and Spain to your South. If you decide to take on Germany as France you must also consider the type of Trade Posts you will acquire. The type of Trade Post you acquire reflects on the type of unit you will receive from the Trade Post. This is important because if you take a War Wagon Trade Post as France you will only receive Age III War Wagon units in comparison to Germany receiving an Age V War Wagon. This mechanic adds risk vs reward. Is it worth it for France to take on Germany and acquire some Age III units in comparison to taking on Spain where most of their units align with France’s Unit Tree? The choice is yours.

➤ Playable Civilizations

AoW European Risk only allows for 8 playable civilizations

  1.       Britain
  2.       Sweden
  3.       France
  4.       Russia
  5.       Germany
  6.       Ottoman
  7.       Italy
  8.       Spain

Playing anything else besides the civilizations listed above will result in your immediate forfeit of the game.

Every "regular" unit a civilization can build (i.e. trainable out of barracks/foundry/stable equivalents, no mercs or outlaws (those are in a seperately packaged mod)) as well as all native warriors now have access to 9 levels of unit veterancy. The ranks are Bronze I, II, III; Silver I, II, III; Gold I, II and Elite. The progression is 2 > 4 > 7 > 12 > 17 > 22 > 28 > 34 > 40 kills, per unit. AOE3 veterancy is zero-sum and only the unit that gets the last hit gets the veterancy credit. That unfortunately is not changeable by me, and as a result veterancy tends to skew heavily towards high HP high cost units, which is also why Elephants are in their own category below.

Veterancy bonuses are split by unit type: Musket Infantry & Grenadiers, Rifle Infantry & Foot Archers, Melee Infantry, Melee Cavalry, Ranged Cavalry, Elephants and Artillery. Mansabdar Units have reduced HP scaling but otherwise the same bonuses. No bonuses for Hero units like Explorers, Daimyo etc.

Before I get into the stats, I'd like to state that a) I've definitely forgotten to add veterancy for some units (pretty sure I missed a few revolutionary units, and I can't currently test to see if I every Italian and Maltese unit has been added correctly) b) This mod is really not balanced and I invite you to use this mod and the Mercenary Veterancy mod (to be released shortly after this one) as a base to do your own veterancy scaling. The source code is in protomods.xml, and as far as I know the modifiers I'm using are the only ones available to the veterancy system (which is honestly a shame, there's an incredible amount that can be done with the system far beyond just making units stronger).

Also, I'm open to balance suggestions. No guarantee that I'll actually implement them but there's certain unit types I have pretty much zero understanding of and if you see something that doesn't work or doesn'tfit the unit feel free to let me know. It is possible to do individual veterancy per unit, but considering the scale of this mod I was content with just establishing a base to work off of, for now. The one positive thing about my approach is that it consistently scales power between units of different quality and cost efficiency if the same bonuses are used, and it makes changes/fixes really easy to implement in VSCode since I can just search and replace entire unit types whenever I notice I've made a mistake on something.

Bonuses:

As a note, Bonuses are per rank and percentages of the unit's base stats (as far as I'm aware). There are no cumulative veterancy bonuses, each veterancy's listed bonus is the entire and only bonus provided. If a stat is listed in consecutive veterancy tiers without changing, the stat does not change between those veterancy tiers.

The (completely optional and standalone) companion to Expanded Unit Veterancy. It's a bit risky adding veterancy ranks to mercenaries, since there is one standard for veterancy kill progression and it applies to ALL units with veterancy. At the same time, it's still very possible considering that each unit has individually defined veterancy bonuses, not to mention that mercenaries are far more likely to actually survive long enough to get veterancy compared to regular units. This mod is organized like its sister mod, with bonuses defined by unit type. 

As with the other mod, please let me know if I've missed any units or if I've miscategorized something, or any issues. This mod should work fine standalone as it includes the same veterancy system, which will harmlessly overwrite Expanded Unit Veterancy's if loaded after, or be overwritten by Expanded Unit Veterancy if loaded before.

Reduces the size of units in a similar manner to my Smaller Elephants mod. This time, it's as many units as I could find off the AOE wiki (hopefully all of them), including naval units.

This is an experimental mod that prioritizes pathfinding optimization over "natural" unit movement, and there will be a lot of clipping ESPECIALLY for naval units. On the other hand, this is to be expected since if there was no clipping the pathfinding wouldn't constantly be trying to correct itself.

Note that Settlers at Mills and Estates have had their production lowered to be equivalent to Rice Paddies (0.5 food/tick 0.34 gold/tick) since Settler collision is greatly reduced to the point of making the higher gather rate unnecessary.

I'm looking for feedback on how this mod improves (or perhaps worsens) performance, as well as feedback on unit control since I'm of the opinion that these changes help with both. At the same time my perspective by itself isn't really much to go on, so let me know if this mod has helped you.
