New layout for DEFINITIVE UI preset, a near perfect mirror of Age of Empires II Definitive Edition UI, Now with full scaling support, and tycoon mode support

Description

Reservistas Mosqueteros makes Insurgentes benefit from improvements that affect Musketeer-type infantry when the Reservistas card is active.

Features

  • Insurgentes now benefit from Ranged, Musket and Gunpowder infantry improvements after the Reservistas homecity shipment is sent. Any improvements for these unit types that were researched before sending the Reservistas homecity shipment will retroactively benefit Insurgentes.
  • Insurgentes will still benefit from Hand infantry improvements. They will also conserve their position in group formations.
  • The animations and weapon model used by Insurgentes when Reservistas is active have been changed.
  • Insurgentes can now use a spear as one of their possible melee weapons (this is only visual).

Cards:

  • [NEW] Peasant Levy (II): Levy calls Insurgentes instead of Militiamen and can be used repeatedly.
  • Peasant Rally (III): Added to homecity. Arrives fast, ships 20 Insurgentes and Insurgente speed is increased by 20%.
  • Reservistas (IV): The icon border is now blue and gold. It also has an updated description to reflect the changes made by this mod.
  • Jungle Warfare (IV): Will now affect the Insurgente’s ranged attack (this does not include the charged lasso attack) in addition to its hand attack.

Special interactions:

  • [Historical Battles] All changes mentioned will also affect the “Cry of Dolores” historical battle.

Compatibility

Incompatible with any mod that directly changes the base Insurgente unit or Insurgente exclusive techs/shipments. The changes will apply initially, but when Reservistas is sent they will be overwritten. A compatibility patch will be required.

Should be compatible with most other mods.

Language

Full text support for Spanish and English. Text for all other languages were made using translation tools, quality/accuracy may vary.

Known Issues

Any Insurgente queued for production will be canceled when Reservistas arrives.

Insurgentes created after Reservistas don’t count towards the “Strength in Numbers” achievement.

 

Find any bugs? Have any suggestions? Leave a comment in the discussion section below.

Swaps the flags for Mexico's Texan revolt to the Burnet Flag to match the Texians' Inspiring Flag icon. The Burnet Flag was the national flag of the Republic of Texas from 1836-1839, when it was replaced by the Lone Star Flag.

This version is usable online but the revolt's initial selection image will remain the Lone Star Flag (as the developers used the same file as the United States' Texas Age up and I didn't want to also change that one to the Burnet).

If you want a version that does alter the revolt selection image to the Burnet Flag while leaving the USA's Federal state as the Lone Star Flag, I made an alternate version using a politicianmods file to bypass that issue, however due to using a data file the alternate version will not be usable in online play unless everyone has it installed. https://www.ageofempires.com/mods/details/94366/

Swaps Malta's default Wood and Stone Wall skins for the Fort Walls seen in the campaign's Act I:Blood.

Note:

During testing the game kept putting the Fort Wall skin on backwards for existing Walls after using Bastion to upgrade them, for better immersion both the default Wood Wall and Bastion upgraded Stone Walls use the Fort Wall model which avoids that problem, however this does mean you won't be able to tell the tech level of a Maltese Wall without clicking on it to see the stat modifiers.

Because the Fort Wall has an interior and exterior, if you want your Walls to look good you may need to tweak the direction you start to build them in so it orients them the right way.

modName: Triggers that make look as if the AI was attacking you or fruit_triggers
description:
Some army triggers.

One condition and four effects

Condition 01: check amount of unitType in army

Effect 01: Army Deploy number of protounits at unitType of player, all map

Effect 02: Army Deploy number of protounits at unitType of player in area

Effect 03: set QV as amount of unitType in army

Effect 04: change all protounits of player to another protounit in area. Unable to preserve movement

Effect 05: change all protounits of player to another protounit, all map. Unable to preserve movement

Effect 06: delete all units of type

Effect 07: delete all units of type in area, all map

 

These will allow you to create attack waves that will behave as an army.

For example, you want to create attack waves of 16 Skirmishers and 6 Musketeers. Create a ghost army AttackWave and 4 triggers

Trigger 1, enabled

Conditions

Set QV1 as amount of Musketeers in army AttackWave

Set QV2 as amount of Skirmishers in army AttackWave

Effects

Enable Trigger 2

Enable Trigger 3

 

Trigger 2, disabled

Conditions

Quest var check: QV1<=15

Timer: 27

Effects:

Army deploy at UnitType of Player: AttackWave, Musketeer, 5, Barracks

Enable Trigger 1

 

 

Trigger 3, disabled

Conditions

Quest var check: QV1>15

Quest var check: QV2<=5

Effects:

Army deploy at UnitType of Player: AttackWave, Skirmisher, 5, Barracks

Enable Trigger 1

 

Trigger 4, enabled, looping

Conditions:

Quest var check: QV1>15

Quest var check: QV2>5

Effect: Army move: AttackWave, whereDoYouWantItToGo, AttackMove=True

————————————————————————

!!Please do not add reso##### such as textures from this mod to other mods without my permission!! Thank you! 

—————————————————————————

Good News! My good friend "Lawrence of Arabia", XBOX ID: "GE-Erika" officially joined the production team of this MOD (actually only him and me, barely considered a Team XD). The two of us have similar ideas, and he has stronger modding skills than me, and he gave me a lot of helpful advice during my previous modding pro#########

—————————————————————————

The appearance of some units has been upgraded with age up as an expedient method not to affect the mutiplayer games. In the future, I may launch a version that can fully express my idea and cannot be mutiplayer normally.

—————————————————————————

Added historical skins to European generic units, especially Royal Guard units, to differentiate them from each other. 

If you like the MOD, you can give a good rating and subscribe to my Bilibili channel, If there is an update, I will release the relevant video in time: https://space.bilibili.com/639148

When doing mods, I have maintained considerable restraint and have the following principles:

1. All unit skins must be based on historical images, not imaginary or stereotyped.

2. Only the common units in the game are modified, and the original units with independent shapes should not be changed as much as possible.

3. Try to fit the original style as much as ######le. Except for units with a large proportion of black and white, the player's color is dominant.

4. Absolutely avoid unit confusion, and do not replace the player's regular units with existing trainable units (local soldiers, mercenaries) in the game.

5. If the modified unit is a royal guard unit, the appearance will be restored according to the name given in the game.

Q&A:
1. Can I use this mod for multiplayer?
Now, yes.

2. Will the reloading action be retained after the needle gun?
No, that will affects multiplayer. 

3. Will the saber of the Ulan cavalry be changed to a long spear?
Won't. Because the restraint relationship of the Ulan cavalry in the game is more similar to the hussars, not the lancers, It will cause recognition trouble to the player and will not be modified.

This mod aims to change some design philosophies of the game and to create a smoother transition between archaic and modern units. Further changes down the line include reducing skirmishers range to make them less opressive units and allowing Rifling to return to their current age 3 range.

This is a concept proof, and a personal project.

 

Current Changelog
Pikeman (beefed up to dominate melee combat using packed formations to stack up dps)
- Food cost increased from 40 to 50
- HP increased from 120 to 150
- Melee damage increased from 8 to 10
- Added 1 range to the melee attack 


Musketeers (reworked as a premium unit in age 2 and a back bone infantry unit in age 3)
- Coin cost increased from 25 to 40
- Ranged damage increased from 23 to 30
- Melee damage reduced from 13 to 7, damage bonuses removed.
- Arsenal now reduces the coin cost of musketeers when built back to 25.
 
Hussar
- Coin cost removed
- HP decreased from 320 to 200
- Line of Sight increased from x to 20
- Melee damage reduced from 30 to 25

Dragoon
- Reclasiffied as AbstractHeavyCavalry
- Hp increased from 200 to 320
- Ranged attack has a minimum range of 2 (right now the unit will choose a new target or draw swords and engage in melee). All bonuses and penalties removed
- Melee attack increased from 11 to 30. All bonuses and penalties removed.
- Now benefits from Cavalry Cuirass

Arsenal techs
- Socket Bayonet now reestablishes the 20% melee resistance of musketeers as well as their melee attack and bonuses

Real Battles Sounds aims to make the sounds of muskets and rifles more realistic; excluding settlers and pistolmen.
The audio files of the firearms are the result of an arduous work from real sounds of the weapons of the time and other games.
I hope you enjoy it as much as I do and give your opinion in the review.

Description

Stacked Deck (Full 40) increases the maximum number of cards that can be in a deck from 25 (21 in the case of United States and Mexico) to 40.

Features 

  • All civilizations now have a maximum deck size of 40 cards.
  • Each Age still only holds 10 cards.

Compatibility

Incompatible with other mods that change the max deck size.

Compatible with mods that modify other features of the homecity (like adding/removing cards or changing the cards that are available in preset decks).

Compatible with mods that add new civilizations as long as they use the default deck size. For civilizations that use a custom deck size (like United States or Mexico) a compatibility patch will be required.

Language

No text changes were made.

Known Issues

None so far.

This mod intends to display the most accurate names for the different civilizations in the game (romanized version), in order to not generalize them as "Germans", "Japanese", etc. 

Names are changed based on the historical period the game was based on, taking into account units, flags, personalities etc. If you find this mod intersting, please subscribe to it, and let me have some feedback, 

thank you.

Civs:

British: -> "Great Britain" (1707–1800) The game features units like the Long bow, Queen Elizabeth and cards such as Virginia Company or House of Tudor, that would fit more with the Kingdom of England. But, the in game flag resembles the Great Britain. Also, some units like the Read Coat, the Ranger or the South Sea Bubble fit more with the late gameplay and the colonial era.

French -> "French Republic". The in game displays the The Royal Banner of France, but the Cuirassier, the voltigeur, House of Bonaparte card and Napoleon himself for sure resembles the Napoleonic France or French Republic (1804–1809).

Dutch-> "Dutch Republic"  (1588–1795) Based on Maurice of Nassau, also the Banks as economic feature and cards like the Dutch East Indian Company or the House of Nassau. Officialy it was "Republic of the Seven United Netherlands", but the name is too long for the layeout.

Spanish-> "Spanish Empire" Even Though Isabel la Catolica and some of the spanish archaic units might resamable more the 15th century, the Spanish Characteristic Colonial era was for sure between 1492–1976, that's also the in game flag. Spain can also send some Italian units like the elmeti or the Dutch Stradiots, showing part of the terriotires spain used to have in europe.

Portuguese-> "Portuguese Empire" (1415-1975). Portuguese also have an extensive history, the organ gun and Henry the Navigator would make the civ fit more into the Kingdom of Portugal. Although, because of the Caçadores, the Flag and the Navy potential, I find the empire a better name.

Germans-> "Kingdom of Prussia" (1701–1918). DE has portrait them as the Holly Roman Empire. Although, Frederick the Great himself held power over the Kingdom of Prussia, also units like the needle gunner, the czapka/uhlan and card like House of Hohenzollern and the Winged Hussar resemble more Prussian/Polish units. The War Wagon and The Doppelsoldner, not quite. Nonetheless, I chose the Kingdom of Prussia as the most accurate one for the game.

Russian->"Tsardom of Russia"(1547–1721). The in game resembles quite well The Tsardom with Personalities like Ivan the Terrible. Also, units like the strelets were made under Ivan in the 16th century. A card like the Suvorov Reform is part of the Russian empire, but I find the Tsardom more accurate.

Ottomas->"Sublime Ottoman State" (1299–1922). It fits perfectly with Suleiman the Magnificent, the Great siege of Malta, the Janissary, etc.

Aztecs->"Triple Alliance"(1428–1521) Mexica resembles better the Nahuatl speaking indigenous people of the Valley of Mexico, because Aztecs was not endonym. Although, mexica and thier allies had the expression “In Ēxcān Tlahtōlōyān”. This can be translated as "Triple Alliance"

Incas->"Tawantinsuyu"(1438–1533/1572). This was the name used by the Inca and their subjects to call the empire. Also it its the romanized version of the Quechua word.

Haudenosaunee and Lakota already reflect the correct autonym. Although, the Haudenosaunee also refer to themselves as Ongweh’onweh.

Swedes->"Swedish Empire"(1611–1721). Resembling the reign of Gustavus Adolphus, matching also the Caroleans and the card that refers to the Oxenstierna reforms.

Maltese->"Knights Hospitaller" (1530–1798) The official name would be Knights Hospitaller of Saint John of Jerusalem, but it's too long for the layeout, could be also refered as Hospitaller Malta.

Italians->"Kingdom of Italy" (1861–1946) I chose this name based on the historical relevance of Giuseppe Garibaldi for the Unification of Italy. Also, the Bersagliery unit fits into the Kingdom. But I'm aware that Italy as a civ might also contain features from the Kingdom of Lombardi Venetia or the Papal states.

Chinese->"Great Qing" (1636–1912). I clearly based this on Kangxi Emperor, The in game Flag and Manchu units.

Indians->"Mughal Empire" (1526–1857). Not much to say here, rather than the game compendium describes the civs as the Mughla Empire. Also, the Sepoy or the personality Akbar resembles this name.

Japanse->"Tokugawa Shogunate" (1603–1868). This is also a very unique case, beacuse the game placed the Japan Campaign and units like the Ashigaru more in the Azuchi Momoyama period or the Ashikaga period. But as players we use the civ as the Tokugawa Clan, including the flag. Therefor, I feel Tokugawa Shogunate is the most accurate, it could also be the Eastern Army.

United States->"United States of America" In order to have the official name.

Mexicans->"Mexican Empire"(1821–1823). Mexico, during the 19th century had different types of goverments, some of them rather short or just provisional. But taking into account the in game Flag, I chose the Mexican Empire as the accurate name.

Ethiopians->"Ethiopian Empire" (1270–1974). In the game the civ takes place around the late 19th century, which can be notice if you check out the breech loading system rifles of the Neftenya skirm or the Sebastopol Mortar. 

Hausa->"Hausaland" I understand Hausa is a correct autonym.  Although Hausaland or Hausa Kingdoms could resemble better the period between the 15th to 18th century.

modName: Epic Napoleonic Scenario The Battle of Krasnoi Russia 1812
description:
WARNING! Some PCs may experience performance issues. WARNING!

 

Here is another Napoleonic Epic Battle Scenario!

 

Welcome Players!

 

This scenario is similar to the others I have published, focusing on the late game and treaty style play. However, for this time you have all the resources you need (no vils) and 200 pop space for your army with upgraded units!

 

It is a large-scale battle that is focused on destroying weapons caches to control the battlefield, like the last one.

 

You can only make a limited number of cannons, the French also have this restriction.

 

Keep your hero alive, you will lose if he dies.

 

Towards the end of the game, after you have destroyed the weapons caches, the AI will start spamming units, so just push forward to the town centre. (Make sure you have completed the other main objectives first).

 

This is my third published scenario. I have created a scenario that I thought I would enjoy, it's probably not historically accurate, but hopefully, you and others will enjoy it.

 

To save this scenario, open the directory on the mod page, copy the file in the folder and paste it into your scenario folder in your game files.

 

Enjoy the Battle Players!

- Minimum lag: simple design, slow spawn rate, no cinematic, no special stuff
- As balanced as possible: small pool of mirrored units, symmetrical design of the map
- Easy to host and play: small file size, small map, four players only
- Designed in AOE3 DE

modName: A Voice for the Unknown: Additive Sound Mod
description:
While the vast majority of units in the game feature voice lines, some do not because the way their sound files are coded doesn't work for every civilization, or because they just do not have a sound file made for them. The goal of this mod is to help give a voice to these silent units that you may stumble across through converting guardians with your War Chief, getting a unit from a team card, or finding them on the Unknown map.

Features

  • All the units you can acquire from TEAM cards should now have sounds for all civilizations.
  • All Treasure Guardians that are missing their own XML sound files (mostly from Knights of the Mediterranean maps) should now have sounds for them. Colonial Guardians from African Royals maps should now properly have Grunt, Death, and Creation sounds.
  • Units that were formerly mute for the Inca such as Settlers should now have sounds.
  • Many of the special units you can start with on the Unknown map should now have sounds for all civilizations.
  • This mod should be multiplayer capable according to the in-game mod menu, and it alters nothing but the sound XML files for the units.
  • This mod ought to work with other mods since it's almost all additive, but there may be conflicts. I did add civlogic "none" for many of the units so if you've got a mod where you're playing a new civilization like John Black's Mercenaries it should work for them as well.

Update 13.27885 introduced additive modding for the sound files, meaning they can now be altered without breaking after updates, for example you may have noticed your General's Amelia Black skin didn't quite sound right if you had certain mods turned on. With additive modding that shouldn't be an issue and we can now mod sound files that should be resilient through updates. Most of this mod is additive files, a few are brand new XMLs that needed to be made for units that never had one.

*** Please Restart after install ***

|||RISK GoT||| v1.4 2023 August

This Risk scenario is inspired by the popular Game of Thrones saga. In this very large map of Westeros you are one of the powerful families that will have to fight for the control of The Seven Kingdoms. You start in your capital and expand to nearby towns and territories. Micro well and plan your strategies right, for the map is large and the units are few.

Special gameplay:

  • Very slow mustering rate (from 2 to 12 minutes).
  • Multiple mustering of small platoons (from 5 to 35 units).
  • The first time conquering a territory grants a mustering.
  • Very fast and efficient x2 explorers.
  • The posts at the center of the map are more powerful than at the extremes.
  • The posts at the capitals are more powerful than the villages.
  • Bonus units based on the number of kills and XP.

Strategy:

  1. Quickly build your capital and desired territories until everything near is taken.
  2. Plan the expansion and the defense. You will need to hold the territory until the next mustering to get those units.
  3. Use naval transport to surprise your enemies but we aware of the strong coastal defences.
  4. Use diplomacy to form alliances and keep the top player from winning.
  5. Do not help the top player.

Host it for 8 players, Free For All (FFA) and Post Imperial starting age. A powerful Gaia will take the families not taken by a player - avoid using bots for the civs below:

Civs/Families: (from South to North)

  1. Ottoman - Martell
  2. France - Tyrell
  3. Spain - Baratheon
  4. Britain - Lannister
  5. Sweden - Targaryen
  6. Dutch - Greyjoy
  7. Russia - Stark
  8. Germany - Night's watch

Cheers,
leFer

|Join the conversation in AOE Risk in discord.gg/MnEAmJEMNd|

In an attempt to bring a little balance to the walls some civs e.g America have an upgrade called Deredoubts which when used increases the health of the wall by 200% which in turn makes their fort walls more powerful than civs such as France. So with this in mind i have created another upgrade for all the civs that do not have the option of Decityfortification (City Walls) or Deredoubts so they can match closer in health with other civs.

This mod has the wooden fort walls and the Stone/Asian Fort Walls - then majority of the civs has another upgrade called Strengthen Fortification which increases the health of the walls by 200%.

I have made this a seperate mod incase people dont want the extra upgrade but still want the wooden fortwalls then you can stick to the 1.7 version

or if you dont want wooden fortwalls at all but just the stone/Asian fort walls then stick to 1.1

and dont forget if you just want it all with a bunch of fun extras there is Skirmish Madness :)

Also want to take second to thank people for trying out and enjoying what i have done here i really appreciate all the feedback i have had :)

12/18/2022 update:

Improved flag texture
Replaced UI portraits for Ashigaru Musketeer, Daimyo, & Shogun units

12/04/2022 update: In order to make this mod more consistent, the following characters/units have been renamed in the English version of the game:

Japanese AI character "Tokugawa" to "OdaNobunaga" (織田信長)
Japanese Unit "Shogun Tokugawa" to "OdaNobunaga" (織田信長)
Japanese Unit "Daimyo Kiyomasa" to "Hashiba Hideyoshi" (羽柴秀吉)
Japanese Unit "Daimyo Masamune" to "Takigawa Kazumasu" (滝川一益)
Japanese Unit "Daimyo Mototada" to "Shibata Katsuie" (柴田勝家)

Renamed daimyo units represent the senior generals of the Oda Clan in the 16th century.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Oda Clan (織田軍) mod implemented the following changes for the Japanese: 

Changed the original Japanese Flag to the Oda Clan Flag, which is based on the Oda family Crest - Oda Mokko (織田木瓜). Consulate image is included.
Changed the Japanese AI portrait to OdaNobunaga (織田信長), who was the first "Great Unifier" of Japan and the head of the Oda Clan from 1551 to 1582.
Remodelled the Ashigaru Musketeers - Honored & Exalted Ashigaru Musketeers will now carry the Oda Mokko banner.

Historical background: The Oda Clan was the first clan in Japan to use large number of arquebus to defeat their enemy in the Battle of Nagashino (長篠の戦い).  This battle is often cited as a turning point in Japanese warfare and the first "modern" Japanese battle.

This mod is multiplayer compatible.

***Please note: The Ashigaru Musketeer model is originated from "National style2.0 mod" by napoleongggg. I redesigned the banner for this mod.

Hello everyone! Welcome to the Age of the World mod for Age of Empires III: Definitive Edition!

FIRST DISCLAIMER: Downloading this mod will probably make your game much slower, however it is not frozen, it's just downloading a giant mod xD

Secondly, let me invite you to the mods' Discord server, we are currently at over 2000 members and here is where you can find multiplayer matches, give suggestions for the mod, connect to other players, report bugs and much more!: https://discord.gg/thcddGYS
Also, make sure to subscribe to mods' official YouTube channel where trailers are posted showcasing content coming to the mod: https://www.youtube.com/@AgeoftheWorldModforAoEIIIDE

Thirdly, there are optional civilizations, that need to be enabled via a small patch that you will also need to subscribe to, and you can do so here: https://www.ageofempires.com/mods/details/184215/
The civilizations in question are the Circle of Ossus and the Knights of St. John, and they were separated from the main mod to allow the players that want to play with and against historical civs only to do so, and other players who want to play with the Campaign civs to do so very easily. 

VERY IMPORTANT: All the files for the Campaign civs are still included in the main mod's files and the patcher only enables it, so it won't work without this mod, the main mod, being installed. Also, to play multiplayer, all players need to have the patch either enabled or disabled, but one or more player not having the same settings for the patcher will not allow you to play with each other or cause sync errors

So the concept of this mod is to preserve the civilizations of Age of Empires III:DE and keep them mostly as they are, while modifying some gameplay aspects and adding tons of new civilizations! Although, some vanilla civilizations will be heavily modified to fit the new cultures introduced in this mod. 
Here are the differences you can expect when playing this mod vs. vanilla DE:

- Defensive structures no longer have a build limit, and wall connectors can now transform into the civ's defensive building.

- All civs now have a late game infinite Wood building. For most, it's the Sown Grove - Grove Sowers can be trained from the Town Center at a food cost - but some civs have very different wood buildings.

Now to the new Civilizations and Cultures!

Note: each civ includes new units, but I encourage you to explore them yourselves as there are A LOT and I don't want to make this post exceedingly long!

New Culture: Legendary Outlaws

This culture's economy is all about gold. They have a unique age-up mechanic, Raids! Raids can be started with the explorer type unit of a civilization and have a higher gold cost and lower food cost than regular age-ups from the Colonial Age forward! They have unique Villagers who have either Gold costs or higher Food costs than normal, each with specific bonuses. They also each have a unique fort type that combines the functions of a Fort and a Capitol, providing both a strong frontier defense as well as a late game economic improvements. Their gold building is also available from Colonial Age

Civs in this culture:

Caribbean Pirates - Their unique Villager is the Crewmate, having a bonus at gathering gold. Their unique Dock has an anti-ship attack, and can train Treasure Ships - ships that can't attack but generate gold when tasked to a Pirate Dock, aswell as their unique roster of ships. They have access to the Spoils Camp that allows them to distribute the spoils from their raids (as well as some base spoils from previous raids) for varied effects. Their unique fort type unit is Fort Tortuga and their leader is Sir Henry Morgan.

The Cowboys - Their unique Villager is the Farmhand, a mounted villager! They have a wide variety of unique features, such as the gambling system at the Inn, having only ranged units (that can be upgraded via their Homecity to be slightly better at melee range), and having livestock trickle Gold when tasked to Ranches. Their Age-Up is also a mix between the base Outlaw Raid system and the American Age-Up, meaning they go on Raids, being able to pick what to raid, but each Raid will also provide a unique pair of cards (like the Americans and Mexicans). Their leader is "Old Man" Claton.

New Culture: Polynesian

This culture is all about... well, culture! Each civilization in this culture has a civ specific statue/sculpture with different variations that provide different bonuses. They cannot be built with a Villager, and instead builders can be sent from their Home City or spawned at the Ceremonial Site. Speaking of which, the Ceremonial Site is where they Age Up. Instead of selecting a Politician, they will select a God each age. Gods grant powers, which recharge over time, while also giving increased age-up rewards based on the age at which they were selected (similarly to Native civs). Their age up is particularly slow, but can be sped up by tasking Villagers to the Ceremonial Site, which also speeds up the spawn rate of statue builders.

Civs in this culture:

Rapa Nui - The Rapa Nui's unique statues are the moais. Inspiring Moais increase gathering rates around them, Channeling Moais slowly gather from natural resources around them and Healing Moais heal nearby friendly units. Their deities are Uoke, God of Destruction, whose power temporarily increases your units’ attack against buildings, Era Nuku, God of Feathers and Farming, whose power temporarily increases your farming rates, Make Make, Creator-God of Humanity, whose power temporarily decreases your land military units’ train times, Hina-Oio, Goddess of Sea Animals, whose power temporarily reveals every dock, ship, fish and whale on the map while increasing your fishing rates, and finally, Manana Take, Goddess of the Sky, whose power provides random resource crates.

New African Civs:

Moroccans - The Moroccans are well versed in the art of trade. This is reflected in one of their unique buildings: the Argan Plantation - representing one of their exports, the Argan Oil, here they can gather either Wood or Influence. This building can also be upgraded via their Homecity to have the resource that is not active to be gathered at 50% the speed as the active resource. They also have access to the Moroccan Marketplace that combines the function of the Livestock Market and a Farm.

Egyptians - The Egyptians set themselves apart from other African civilizations not only by the occasional camel unit but also by how they obtain Influence and how intricate their economical building placement can be. They obtain Influence mainly through Monuments, which generate it over time. This rate can be increased by building Temples around them. At the same time, Temples also improve the gathering rates of nearby Villagers and Monuments have an aura that allows nearby Villagers to generate XP while gathering. 

Zulu - The Zulu, like the Yoruba, don't have access to the War Camp, and have a separate infantry building, the Battle Camp, and horse building, the Horse Kraal. They are very unique in how they obtain Influence, gaining a certain amount of influence every time they train a unit (amount depends on the unit), and by having a lot of livestock, which is facilitated by their homecity shipments and their unique cattle unit, the Nguni Cow. Zulu livestock generates influence 3 times faster than any other african civilization. Additionally, they have access to the Ikhanda, which when built spawns a Matriarch and can garrison them gaining a ranged attack. Besides being spawned this way, Matriarchs can be obtained through homecity shipments and will slowly generate a chosen Zulu unit for free.

Yoruba - The Yoruba, like the Zulu, also don't have access to the War Camp, and have a separate infantry building, the Battle Camp, and horse building, the Stable. The Yoruba also don't have access to the Granary, and instead have Plazas. These have a build limit, but will trickle Influence very slowly, in addition to maintaining a Granary's functions. Their influence generation however, can be increased by the number of Villagers working within their aura.

New Native American Civilizations:

Cherokee - The Cherokee are similar to other North American Native civilizations. They are unique in their alliance with the Railroad Company, represented in their unique market - the Railroad Work Post. This Market also has a unique Big tech that allows Railroad Workers to be trained from the Artillery Foundry. These units attack at close range with a pickaxe but shine by throwing dynamite at enemy buildings. They can also send Imprisoned Settlers from their Home City in larger numbers than regular Villagers shipments.

Mayans - The Mayans are a mixture of their ancient selves and the Yucatec Mayans represented in the Mexican revolution, featuring a mixture of older and more recent units. Their shock infantry is particularly powerful, highlighted by the Hornet Throwers, who deal all their damage around a target over a few seconds.Their house unit, the Nah Hut, also allows Villagers to ######## ## #### gaining a ranged attack similar to the Town Center. 

Inuits - They Inuit are very visually striking, and are particularly good fishermen and hunters. They have access to the Hunting Cottage which lures hunts, contains their hunting upgrades, and increase the huntable gather rate of nearby Villagers. They also have a unique additional Villager - the Huskies! They start the game with 4 Huskies and 4 Villagers, and they have cards to send more, to be able to train them from the Town Center, and to increase their build limit. The Huskies can gather from animals and be tasked to the Community Plaza with half of the effectiveness of a regular Villager. 

Charrúa - The Charrúa are a native tribe from Uruguay, from the region of Rio de la Plata. They are semi-nomadic in AotW, meaning they can pack up their unique house, the Toldería, into a Charrúan Traveller, that can then unpack into (almost) any building for free, but taking a very long time to do so. The Charrúan Corral can train cavalry in batches of 7, and can heal nearby idle cavalry units. Also, instead of a regular Food building, they have the Pasture, which slowly generates Wild Cattle (an immoveable type of Huntable with slower gather rates than other Huntables) and can upgrade both the hunting rates and the yield from Huntables. And they still have many more features to explore!

New Subculture: Rainforest Natives

This is a sub-culture of the Native Americans, being very similar, but with their specific trait being that they can't directly gather gold. Their War Chiefs also have the ability to convert animal treasure guardians instead of human treasure guardians.

Caribs - Like has been mentioned earlier, they can't directly gather gold... but they do use gold. They obtain it from the Western Trade Post, their unique market, where they can buy chests of gold with food and wood at an even exchange rate. They also have access to the Caribbean Tree Plot, their unique Wood building that benefits from all tree gathering upgrades, and the Native Gathering House that trains shock infantry, alligators and Medicine Men.

Tupi - The Tupi can't gather Gold, but on the other hand don't use it at all. The Malocas can toggle between food and wood gathering, and instead of the Market they have the School! Here they can research economic upgrades and research techs that allow Villagers to be trained faster. They can research a tech at the Town Center that enables currency exchanges in the School. Furthermore, recruiting a treasure guardian with their War Chief will allow a certain number of them to be trained at the Animal Husbandry, their stable type building. Researching the Community Plaza's big tech will allow animals to participate in ceremonies aswell.

New Asian Civilizations:

Cambodian Khmers - As expected, they are an elephant civilization! They have their unique Wonders and a wide array of elephant units to choose from, along with cards to make them stro##### Since both their cavalry and artillery are elephant units, they are all trained at the Elephant Stables. Therefore, instead of the Castle, they have the Tower, which is essentially similar to the European Outpost.

New European Civilizations:

Jesuits - The Jesuits are one of the most unique civilizations in this mod. I placed them here, but I could've just as easily placed them under the Native Civilizations. They cannot gather gold directly either, but they can generate it with the Jesuit Churches and via Estates that are available to them one age earlier than normal and also cost less Wood to build. Their unique villager, the Missionary, doesn't have a ranged attack, but can slowly heal idle units (they also can't automatically gather from resource crates and need to be tasked manually). With each age up (excluding the last one) they will receive a Mission Establisher, allowing you to build a mission allying with one of four tribes: the Calusa, the Chichimeca, the Huron and the Guarani. This will allow you to train and upgrade their units in the Missions themselves aswell as researching their unique technologies in the College unique building. Furthermore, after establishing the first mission, the Shaman, a native villager, becomes available at the Town Center. The Shaman can build the Community Plaza and participate in ceremonies in it. Their Community Plaza big tech unlocks unique Jesuit ceremonies and allows Jesuit Priests to participate in the Community Plaza.

Finnish - The Finnish are the civ most similar to other civs already in game, while having a lot of elements keeping them very unique. Instead of houses they have Mercenary Lodgins, which besides supporting population can also train mercenaries and be improved via the homecity to trickle Gold. Instead of Mills and Plantations they have Maatilas. This building allows two reso##### to be gathered at the same time, being able to toggle between the 3 possible combinations. Instead of the Livestock Pen they have access to the Sámi Reindeer Farm, which slowly spawns herdable reindeer and can also train them at a faster rate (and also fatten livestock faster). Finally, the Finnish Dock spawns a Perch when built - a fish providing an infinite amount of food.

OPTIONAL CIV: Circle of Ossus - This civ is not based in history, instead being inspired by Age of Empires III's campaign. Therefore, it can be enabled by a separate patch as explained before. They have a unique Age-Up mechanic, aging up via Enclaves. Each Enclave will provide you two cards to add to your deck from the respective country, aswell as unlocking reinforcements to be sent via the in-game Enclave unique building consisting on unique units from the country. They have a plethora of other unique features that are better explored and explained in-game! Get the patcher here: https://www.ageofempires.com/mods/details/184215/

Since I've seen it requested several times, I decided to do some research and even came across a wikipedia article (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Highland_charge) demonstrating the use of swords by Highlanders. I modified the Highlander to use swords for his melee attacks.
kljh

DANES
-Civilization bonus: Receives a free Castle Rickshaw and Books of XP with every Age, and starts with 7 Settlers. Advances in Age by choosing a trade agreement alliance with other nation that exchanges resources and unlocks two cards. Instead of Outlaws, Landsknecht and Bosniak mercenaries can always be recruited from the Castle and Tavern. More mercenaries available with each alliance.

-Special Features: Can build Castles instead of Outposts. The Castle also functions as a tavern that can train many mercenaries. The Deck is limited to 23 cards. The Danes have almost no normal shipments of Settlers or resources starting from the Commerce age (II), but they get similar shipments through alliances. This is because the Danish colonies did not receive much support from their mother country, so they were forced to seek assistance through other means.
The Danes start with a Settler limit of 79, each alliance/revolution increases that limit by +5, except the alliance with the French and Germans, because of their immigrant cards.

-Royal guard units: Musketeer (Landsoldat) and Friskytte (Snaphane)
-Unique units: Friskytte

-Historical Context: The Danes managed to cling to their colonial holdings and, at times, to carve out a valuable niche in international trade by taking advantage of wars between larger countries and offering foreign trade under a neutral flag.

Assertive AI is a multiplayer compatible AI mod that expands on the default AI to provide a broader, more varied, and overall improved single-player experience.

Naval AI: The flagship feature of Assertive AI is its naval AI, providing widesweeping improvements to ships, defensive buildings, dock placement, and more. 

King-of the Hill: With the fixes to King of the Hill, you'll now see the AI both attack and attempt to hold the fort with formidable force.

Varied Strategies: Opponents utilize one of five distinct strategies: Rush, Naked Fast Fortress, Safe Fast Fortress, Fast Industrial, and Greed. Strategies include unique features like Age 2 forward bases, competitive fast fortress times, livestock gathering, and canoe training. Strategies are random, but weighted to a civ's typical playstyle.

Custom Wall Patterns: Civs can build a number of custom wall patterns around their forward bases, including star forts and some based on real layouts of historic forts like Fort Sumter and Fort Ticonderoga.

Team Attacks: AI players now use team-based conditions to start their attacks, meaning AI players are more likely to attack at the same time in team games.

Improved Coordination and Communication: In addition to expanded taunts, allied players can now notify teammates when they begin their attacks, communicating where they will attack the enemy. Allies are also more likely to help defend and support "attack here" requests. 

Newly Supported Maps: Ceylon is now playable! Recommend you play with an additional handicap of +15-20% for a competitive game. Archipelago is also playable in a beta state (With a special thank you to NewMercies1775 and his Better AI).

Historical Maps: Offense/defense dispositions are appropriately set for The Eighty Years War & The Great Turkish War, and the AI can now capture city states on The Italian Wars.

Standard Maps and Competitiveness: Improvements to standard maps include reworked retreat logic, walls, and fixes to livestock gathering. 

Difficulty Adjustments: Features only present in the Extreme Difficulty have been brought down to Hard and above (excluding cheats).

Raiding: Players can now conduct raids on villagers and check for them at nearby natural resources - including decaying hunts. Different personalities are more likely to raid than others, but stay on your toes because they all might give it a shot under the right conditions!


Thank you to everyone who plays and provides feedback, especially EntombedCurve02, and thank you to The Onyx Tiger for helping with historic wall patterns!

Assertive AI is included in Age of Pirates, a custom map and data mod by BaltazarGreat that adds new maps, units, and native settlements. If you do have Age of Pirates active I recommend you disable this mod, as this one does not include custom scripts written for those maps and units.

TL;DR: This mod turns the Theaters and similar cards (Dance Halls, Cantinas, Atonement, etc) into researchable technologies and Germans always have mercenary camps. It also adds new techs related to outlaws and a new mercenary, the Genoese Crossbowman.

 

This mod was intended to be part of my other mod “Home City Cards as Techs”, but it turned big enough to be a standalone mod. It never made much sense to me Ronin being the mercenary available to all european civs, so instead the theater cards enable the training of Inquisitors, Swiss Pikemen and Genoese Crossbowmen, a new mercenary based on the Pavisier.

Main features:

 • Wild West, Peacemakers and similar techs slightly increase damage and hitpoints of age 2 mercs (Cannoneer and Irish Brigadier), so they stay somewhat useful in later ages.

Ok, with the feedback that was posted on the original no barriers mod regarding the problems it causes the AI, it got me thinking maybe its not the population cost i need to concern myself over, inf population is essentially having so much cap that you just wont have enough space to reach it.

So in order to have this work better with the AI i have not changed or altered thepopulation cost, but increased the population cap to 999999999, now i have still removed the build limit barriers on most native units if not all, and removed the build limits on majority of buildings, so there are two ways to hit that cap one build a stupid amount of houses or by usuing the free upgrade via a town center called inf population cap, this will instanlty smash your cap up to 999999999, however the AI will not use this upgrade therefore to them the cap is normal and will not effect how they work hence AI freindly.

So if you want a large army then use the upgrade and build away im sure you wont reach that cap limit. :).

I have also added being able to build walls over trade routes on here just because why not.

This mod adds content based on the Kigenishi setting to Age of Empires III. Kigenishi is a fantasy setting based on Japanese and East Asian culture and mythology that is depicted in the novel "The Red-Haired Maiden". For expanded details about the mod's features and the way they interact with the original setting, follow this link to the work-in-progress information page: (external link)

Current Version: Demo v1 (January 2023)

While the contents of this mod are intended to be used in maps created for their use, the new civilizations are fully playable in any Skirmish game.

Current Features

  • Playable Civilization: The Majin
  • Playable Civilization: The Nam-eun
  • AI Personality: Lady Otoniwa (Majin)
  • AI Personality: Lord Captain Jae-hyun (Nam-eun)
  • Minor Civilization: Majin Pirates
  • Historical Map: San's Rebellion (Princess Osan Chapter 1)
  • Historical Map: Rise of Kanekita (Princess Osan Chapter 2)

Notes

  • This mod is a work in progress, and will hopefully have more content added or tweaked over time.
  • There is a list of known bugs and issues on the page linked in the first paragraph.
  • This mod will eventually supercede the (now barely functioning) mod for the Founding of Guwon.
  • While some 2D art and sound effects will be added in later versions, there is currently no modder-friendly way to add new unit models. Therefore, all units will use existing AoE3 models.

2

The Circle of Ossus was introduced to us in the Blood, Ice, and Steel campaign, and we soon learned that it was a powerful secret society on a quest for both eternal life and world domination. While we never learned the secrets behind the white animals, the Circle and other campaign civilizations like Malta certainly captivated our attention. While the Maltese are finally fleshed out and playable in the Definitive Edition, the Circle may never officially be, thus it's up to modders to bring her to life.

While others have gone down this road before me, this iteration of a playable Circle of Ossus civilization should have some never before seen features including an age-up system inspired by dialogue in the campaign. After creating the Circle, I wanted to dive a little deeper into the campaign lore and create a unique playable civilization for the Circle's archenemies, Morgan and his family. As of April 2024, that project has been finished and new civilizations representing Morgan and Lizzie as well as John, Nathaniel, Amelia, and Chayton are now playable in this mod.

Notes

Steam Users - Disable Steam Cloud for this game before attempting to download this and other civilization mods! The Steam Cloud wreaks havoc with mods' home city files and can render them unplayable with it enabled.

This mod may have compatibility issues with other mods, especially after the April update which modifies numerous files which are not compatible with additive modding (If you play with multiple mods enabled, always put the one you want to work the best at the top of the priority list). All players will need to install it if you wish to play multiplayer with it. It is in English only, though it has English string files for all other languages. This mod should not alter the campaign's Circle faction as almost all assets were newly made for it to avoid any issues with that. You can play as, with, and against the three campaign-inspired civilizations in this mod as they have AI coded for them.

Features

  • Circle Enclaves: The Circle's multinational Enclaves serve as both their civilization bonus and their Age-Up mechanic. For each Age you choose one of the Circle's Enclaves and it unlocks two powerful cards for your deck. One of the cards is the Enclave Support card, which offers you a bonus either directly from or inspired by that Enclave's host nation. For example, the German Enclave's Support card ships a free unit with all your shipments, while the British Enclave's Support card enables you to construct Manor Houses.
  • Unique Units: The Circle's unique units include the mysterious Boneguard: Wardens and Templars. They also have access to the Paladin, an armored heavy hand cavalry unit, as well as the heavy-hitting Great Cannon featured in Act II: Ice. Some of their archaic units can be replaced with more modern troops by sending in cards inspired by the Definitive Edition's Ageiversary updates for Germany.
  • White Animals: The Circle would not be complete without their telltale white animals that invoke fear and suspicion. Trainable from their unique Church, the Ossuary, your army can be supplemented with wolves, cougars, jaguars, and/or buffalo. Each animal has its own perk such as wolves being stronger in numbers and jaguars terrorizing nearby foes so they are less effective in combat. The animals have a shared build limit similar to normal pets.
  • Campaign Inspirations: The Grand Inquisitor leads your forces, able to convert both man and beast to the Circle's cause, plant the Circle's banner to inspire troops, and can unlock a deadly knife attack. The Circle can get access to the fixed guns, ammo depots, and heavy fort walls seen in the campaign.

  • The Black Family: In the biggest content update since the Circle was Reborn, the Black Family are now available as civilizations to play as, with, or against. The Black Family Estate represents Morgan Black's descendants, and, like the Black Family Estate in the campaign, features a mix of European and Native American mechanics - you can even choose to replace your units with Lakota units as an alternative method to advance to the Imperial Age.

  • Morgan Black: The Knights of Saint John represent Morgan and Elisabet Ramsey, and mechanically are a spin-off of my Circle of Ossus design, with some inspirations from the original design for the Italian civilization which was cut from the 2005 Age of Empires III. As Lizzie might say, the Knights start off rather quaint with an archaic roster, as they do in Act I: Blood, and with each Age you advance you will get to select a new unit to unlock for your roster.

modName: Soldado: spanish skin and anim fix
description:
I was thinking the other day: "Yeah, even though the Soldados are Mexican, it makes all the sense in the world that the Spanish could have them." Then, playing with the Spanish, I realized that I wasn't using them enough. I didn't like their design (despite being correct), and the Spanish economy was not enough to be creating them all the time.
So I modified the colors and the details, and put them on. With someone's advice, I put cows and sheep to work the haciendas and thus obtain free soldados.
They're awesome: attack up to 117 (108 siege) with missionaries close by, tough, and awesome in design. I love them.
Also, fixed the Soldado's mortar animation (both Mexican and Spanish), which is officially the villager's shotgun shot and is now the grenadier's mortar shot, with heavy recoil when firing the grenade.

如果需要重置等級，你需要刪除主城，方法-->打開   []  C:\xxxx[用戶名]\Games\Age of Empires 3 DE\xxxxxxxxxxxxx[steam ID]\Savegame，選取你的主城xml並刪除，然後重啟即可重置主城。

Initial codebase! -> El Ermitaño (Link to Discord)

Multiplayer testing -> Patrick and Voorhees

Music :

1.) Main Menu and Revolution -> Luis Humanoide

https://www.youtube.com/@LuisHumanoide

2.) Multiplayer Lobby Menu -> Karl Jenkins

Inspiration :
1.) Unit Cosmetic Mod (boylive16)
2.) Age of WW1 (Stark22e)
3.) Unit Historical Appearance (GwentMaster)
________________________________________

TL;DR CMV V Features :

> Colonial guardian replaced most generic units.
> Native units became either Roman or Spanish.
> Female troops for archers and skirmishers.
> Default skin explorers get a remesh.
> Priests became Nurses (Native civs included).
> Hausa became an Imperial German colony.
> (X) Flamethrower + Organ gun gatling sound.
> Most mercenaries reskinned.
> Many changes aimed for cool factor.
________________________________________

Original Text :

Are you a fellow veteran of AoE3 who have been playing this game since release and felt that you are getting too old to look at the same musketeer models again and again?

Were you a bit disappointed that AoE4 became a remake of AoE2 instead of a Company of Heroes ripoff?

Are you a fervent supporter of gender-equality and want to see more woman encouraged into traditionally male-dominated professions?

##### you ever wished there were some Spaniards who decided against colonizing the native Aztecs and ###### then helped them modernize instead?

Have you watched Zulu (1964) and think "damn, these Englishmen are sharply dressed" as the British redcoats were getting a taste of the Zulu spear?

Are you crazy about anything Victorian-era related and couldn't stand the sight of native people in their traditional clothing (or lack thereof) and wished to dress them up all nice and proper?

Hell, do you feel a sense of boiling frustration when the word "colonial" was replaced with "commerce" and felt the need to follow your forefather's footsteps by committing genocide and subjugating another civilization with less advanced understanding of science and the word of our lord and saviour Jesus Chirst?

.......

Well, it seems you are in luck today! This mod will fulfill all of the aforementioned statement!......except the last one, you psychopaths.

 

CMV replaced most generic unit models with the colonial ones added in the African Royals DLC.

But there is also extensive model + unit voice replacements done to every civs main combat units and mercenaries.

> Some units were turned into female units re-using existing icons and have their voice response switched to female version.

> Certain Europeans units retain their cool uniforms from the 5.6 Unique Historical Army Skins which was the original codebase of this mod.

> Outlaws became Spanish renegade fighting for the freedom of the native people against Western colonization.

> Hausa became an alternative history German colony.

> Ethiopian levies and some rifle native units became more dignified with proper uniform and timeline-proper armaments.

> All civs default skin explorer plus some newly added DLCs ones get a uniform and weapon change.

> Priests and certain native priest units became a nun instead. (Don't ask me why, I'm weird)

> Flamethrowers and Organ guns have gatling gun sound instead. (I tried turning them into Gatling gun but they will not be usable online so only the sound remained)

> And more! Discover the rest for yourself, since my hand hurts just typing these.

________________________________________

This mod was not made with historical accuracy in mind and mostly re-use the in-game assets, therefore I apologized for my lack of model editing talents.

The mods was made by some crazy guy on his spare time using one of the best mod currently on AoE3 as the codebase (5.6 Unique Historical Army Skins S.XVII-XIX)

I simply bring a historically-accurate mod and turned it into a weird mash of Victorian-era madness. With some personal addition.

Big thanks to El Ermitaño and this friendly Discord group for allowing me to use the resources and codebase! : https://discord.gg/ZKZ7ybnY

Also refering credits to the author of Unit Cosmetics (boylive16), Unit Historical Appearance (GwentMaster2163), and Age of WW1 (Stark22e) for being the initial motivation for starting this mod. I originally want to turn all musketeers into a northern musketeers, 3 weeks later and now this abomination of a mod gets unleashed into the world. Life is weird.

________________________________________

 

I may not be updating very frequently, but if you encountered any bugs or crashes please left them in the comment.

Certain units will have some visual bugs that is beyond my understanding of digital magic wazoo that is AoE3 xml tags.

Also, another apologies to any programmers who happened to see the messy *.xml and decided to pull their eyes out to stop the pain.

modName: Herdable Turkey replaces sheep for aztecs, haud and lakota
description:
Based on history, no Native American tribe domesticated sheep. The ancient Mexicans domesticated the turkey, so I decided to make the turkey replace the sheep for the Native American civilizations (except the Inca, who already have the llama).
Technically speaking, the HerdableTurkey is a new unit, with behavior exactly like the sheep, but turkey appearance, icons, animations, and sounds.
Unfortunately, I couldn't put a player color collar on it, these textures work weird.

The Siege of Rhodes of 1522 was a siege organised around the island of Rhodes by the Ottoman armies in 1522.

It was the military event that definitively put an end to the presence in the Eastern Mediterranean of the military orders born of the Crusades. The Hospitaller order was driven from the island by Suleiman the Magnificent after a long siege of five months.

A mod which will increase certain shipments during the campaign unit counts, some based on the trial released in 2005 and some based on the asian dynasties released in 2007 and some for both.
Central Plains Ren & Ling

Description

Native Alliances - Jesuits overhauls the Jesuit Mission minor civilization with new units, technologies and mechanics.

Features

Team Bonus:

Trade routes generate 10% more resources, as long as you or a teammate is allied with the Jesuits.

Units & Buildings:

  • Arcabucero: Musketeer-type infantry that benefits from the Valor promotion. Good versus Pike infantry instead of Cavalry.
  • Jesuit Missionary: Healer that can convert enemy units (both Gaia and player).
  • Congregacion: Church like building that can train Jesuit units and improves the gathering rate of natural resource in an area around it, gathering rate is improved the further away it is from a Town Center. Benefits from some Mountain Monastery and Cathedral improvements.
  • Conquistador: Adjusted as follows:
    • Cost changed to 70f, 90c.
    • Now benefits from the Valor promotion.
    • Now uses its hand attack when engaged in melee combat.
    • Both versions (from mercenary cards and Trading Posts) now share selection.
    • The selection highlight is now an oval instead of a circle.

Technologies:

  • Heterodoxy (I): Improves the healing rate of all Healer-type units and increases the XP/Influence trickle from Trading Post on native settlements.
  • Ad majórem Dei glóriam (I): Ships 1 Congregacion Wagon and makes them buildable from the Trading Post on Jesuit settlements.
  • *Imported Matchlocks (II): Increases the range and reduces the train time for all Gunpowder Infantry and Cavalry.
  • *Scripturalist (II): Increases the Jesuit Missionary's hitpoints and lets them heal units while in combat and from further away.
  • Repurposed Merchant Ships (II): Ships 1 Caravel with 6 Arcabuceros onboard and the Caravel’s range attack is improved by the number of Arcabuceros onboard. It also increases Caravel build limit or makes them buildable for civilizations that don’t have them.
  • Tercio Armory (IV): Increases the hitpoints, attack and cost of Arcabuceros and Conquistadores, and giving then a damage multiplier against Cavalry and Infantry respectively.
  • Neophyte System (IV): Changes the gathering rate aura from the Congregacion. It now affects all resources and is no longer diminished by Town Centers, but its effect is reduced by half.

Special interactions:

  • [All] Civilizations that can ally themselves with the Jesuits through other means (currently only Mexicans and Ethiopians) will have all the Jesuit units, technologies and additional technologies (*) available in the Congregacion building.
  • [Ethiopians] The Church from the Jesuit alliance can be converted to a Congregacion.
  • [Ethiopians] The Portuguese Units unlock from the Portuguese alliance will stack with Repurposed Merchant Ships. Whichever is acquired first will unlock the Caravel and the other will increase its build limit.

Compatibility

Incompatible with any mod the changes the Jesuit Mission minor civilization. A compatibility patch will be required.

Incompatible with any mod changes the “unittransform.xml” file.

Language

Full text support for Spanish and English. Text for all other languages were made using translation tools, quality/accuracy may vary.

Known Issues

None so far.

 

Find any bugs? Have any suggestions? Leave a comment in the discussion section below.

I created this Colloseum from scratch, I wanted to do it my way and add some simple but cool concepts at the same time.

All the rules are constantly reiterated on your game screen so it doesn't matter if you forget it's written all along the game on your objectives on the right side of your screen.

This is a beta, so please bear with me, I'm alone on this scenario and I'm testing it myself in real life to observe the game balance.
I took a lot of my time to balance it as well as possible for its release
The list of features of my scenario: 

- All civilizations are available

- You gain units every 200/500/800/1000/1200 enemies killed after 1400 it's stat bonuses depending on your civilization
- Playable for 8 players
- Automatically age postimperial, so no need to put it before starting the game
- System to get life back in your base when not fighting
- Flag control system to get gold and wood to repair your fort or walls
- Gold can also be used to buy couleuvrins to balance the game if one player gets artillery before the others
- Anti-camping system, if too many units are in the fort the units around the flag will die
- Maximum number of units 200 and not 200 population, I prefer to specify.
I put a limit of 200 units so that the game is not too much impacted by fps drops especially with 8 players, after testing several times in real conditions it works very well in performance with 8 players
- One more chance" system allows when your fort has 50% life to use a chance that will destroy any enemy in your base, you have a tent as an indicator in your base, if it's there it means you haven't used your second chance, if it's not there then it means you don't have a second chance
- No super powerful unit

To use the scenario:
- download it, then go to multiplayer 
- host a game then select Colloseum_By_Troteck

You have hosted a game of Colloseum !

[FR]

J'ai créé ce Colloseum de zéro, j'ai voulu le faire à ma façon et ajouter des concepts assez simple mais sympa en même temps.

Toutes les règles sont reprécisées en permanence sur votre écran de jeux donc ce n'est pas grave si vous oubliez c'est écrit tout le long de la partie sur vos objectifs à droite de votre écran.

Ceci est une bêta, donc soyez indulgent, je suis seul ### ## ############### et je test moi-même en condition réelle pour observer l'équilibre de jeu.
J'ai pris beaucoup de mon temps pour l'équilibrer au mieux pour sa sortie
La liste des caractéristiques de mon scénario : 

- Toute les civilisations sont disponibles

- Vous gagnez des unités toutes les tranches de 200/500/800/1000/1200 ennemis tués après à 1400 c'est des bonus de stat selon votre civilisation
- Jouable pour 8 joueurs
- Automatiquement age postimpérial, donc pas besoin de le mettre avant de lancer la partie
- Système pour récupérer de la vie en dehors des combats dans sa base
- Système de contrôle de drapeau pour obtenir de l'or et du bois pour réparer son fort ou ses murs
- L'or permet aussi d'acheter des couleuvrines pour équilibrer si un joueur obtient l'artillerie avant les autres
- Système anti-camper, si un trop grand nombre d'unités se trouvent dans le fort les unités autour du drapeau mourront
- Nombre d'unité maximale 200 et non 200 de population, je préfère préciser.
J'ai mis une limite de 200 unités pour que la partie ne soit pas trop impactée par des chutes de fps surtout avec 8 joueurs, après avoir testé plusieurs fois en condition réelle cela fonctionne très bien en matière de performance avec 8 joueurs - Système
" une chance de plus" cela permet quand votre fort a 50% de vie d'utiliser une chance qui va détruire tous les ennemis dans votre base, vous avez une tente comme indicateur dans votre base, si elle est présente cela veut dire que vous n' n'avez pas utilisé votre seconde chance, si elle n'est plus là alors cela veut dire que vous n'avez plus de seconde chance
- Pas de super unité surpuissante

Main units:

Tofangchi: Tough heavy infantry armed with a musket. Fights well against cavalry.

Tribal Swordsman: Cheap archaic heavy infantry. Decent against cavalry and buildings.

Tribal Archer: Cheap archaic archer. Good against infantry.

Qizilbash: Well trained heavy cavalry armed with a lance and a bow. Good against light infantry and artillery, and decent against heavy infantry.

Ghulam Rider: Heavy cavalry with high hitpoints. Good against light infantry and artillery.

Zamburak: Light ranged cavalry

Mercenary units:

Qurchi: Persian heavy cavalry with a fast attack. Good against light infantry and artillery.

Jazayar Fusilier: Heavy mercenary ranged infantry armed with a larger long-range musket.

This Mod provides promotions to certain Aztec units. I'm convinced it makes the Aztec playable, 'cause right now they are very weak. 

Otontin Slinger: ROF

  • Level 0: 1.50 ROF
  • Level 1: 1.35 ROF
  • Level 2: 1.20 ROF
  • Level 3: 1.05 ROF

Jaguar Knight: Speed, ROF

  • Level 0: 4.75 speed, 1.50 ROF
  • Level 1: 5.225 speed, 1.35 ROF
  • Level 2: 5.7 speed, 1.20 ROF
  • Level 3: 6.4125 speed, 1.05 ROF

Skull Knight: Max HP, Damage

  • Level 0: 
  • Level 1: +15% HP, +15% Damage
  • Level 1: +30% HP, +30% Damage
  • Level 1: +45% HP, +45% Damage

Arrow Knight: ROF, Range

  • Level 0: 1.50 ROF 
  • Level 1: 1.35 ROF, (double for siege), +1 range
  • Level 2: 1.20 ROF, (double for siege), +2 range
  • Level 3: 1.05 ROF, (double for siege), +3 range

Eagle Knight: Damage, Range

  • Level 0: 
  • Level 1: +15% Damage, +1 range
  • Level 1: +30% Damage, +2 range
  • Level 1: +45% Damage, +3 range
 
For Otontins, Arrows (and in lesser extention, Eagles), the goal is not to make them great units, but to maximize their combat abilities as a group by desynchronizing their attacks, which redistributes their attacks better and minimizes wasted fire.
 
For Skulls, seeks to promote the conservation of these units, a case that synergizes magnificently with the Aztec healing dance. The damage can seem a bit excessive, and although the HP can also be very high (>1000), let's remember that they are heavy infantry and therefore very vulnerable to their counterattacks.
 
For Jaguars, it is your standard promotion.

With the help of many dedicated playtesters and advisors, I have been able to recreate the popular AOE3 Legacy scenario Arena! This map is 100% xs code meaning the scenario editor was not used. This means it will appear under custom maps rather than custom scenarios. The general flow of the game will feel similar to those who have played the older scenario, but I plan to continue to make small tweaks and changes as the development of this map progresses.


How to play:

Each turn you will gain an amount of gold. It is determined based on how the last round went. Winning gives you extra gold (not an oppressive amount) as a bonus so spend it wisely. With those resources you have receieved, upgrade and build your army!

After the timer runs out for the buy phase of the game, you will now have your units spawned into the battle island in the middle. Now is where you put your units to work attemptting to either capture and hold the flag or destroy the opposition's army with superior positioning.

Once that phase concludes, the winning team is assigned a bonus and the game continues.

First to 5 round wins will achieve victory. Typically this takes 10-15 minutes to play allowing you to play multiple civilizations and battles each session!

 

Hosting:

If all players have the mod installed and working, then you host it as you would any other custom map. If players are joining that do not have the map installed you will need to "refresh" the map download for them. This means you will switch the map to another map (can be anything), then switch back to the Arena version you'd like to play.

 

Playable Civilizations:

Currently there are 10 playable civilizations and they are as follows: Spanish, British, French, Dutch, Portuguese, Russians, Germans, Ottomans, Swedish, and Americans.

The remaining civs are not functional meaning you will have to rehost if anyone pick those. They are planned to be added into the game between June 2023 - September 2023.

FAQ:

  • I never play scenarios, are any of my skills valuable in this map? YES! The game focuses primarily on unit compositions, tactics, and micro. If you have those skills you should be able to be a top player in this as well.
  • Do you play cards in this? No. Currently I have not found a way to set a deck for players in the game, meaning that all balance is thrown out if you can ship units. I have instead opted to give civilizations unique techs at different rounds during the game. For example British get Yeoman the first turn of age 3 (+4 longbow range). Expect 5-6 techs per civilization.
  • I found a bug, how do I contact you? Find me on the Royal Discord server and message me there. I also have text channels where you are free to post bugs, balance issues, etc.
  • What is the best civilization? Most are quite strong. From my questionairres I gave to playtesters, most civs follow what their strengths and weaknesses are from the main game. My recommendation is to try the civilizations you like in the main game and see if they match your playstyle in this game.
  • Are you planning to make any more old scenarios into custom maps? Yes! 100% I will. Right now I will focus on this one for the summer and after that I will make a poll so that the community could vote on what they'd like to see next!
  • Can I play FFA in this map? Unfortuneately not at the moment. I have made the map in a way where FFA is disabled. Honestly if you are looking for that type of experience KOTH will provide that for you.
  • How can I help? By the end of June I hope to have all the code publically available under an MIT license so anyone can see and modify the code. If you'd like to help the project out contact me via the Royal Discord server and I can find something for you to help with!
  • How long does it take to make a custom map like this? Usually a good amount of time. One thing that never got added is a proper XS Debugger to the game. This means as you are making the map, you will not be notified if you made an error and where it is. So if I've been working on a major system for a while between tests, and it breaks, you can double the amount of time it takes to do. To redeem this I will be making a preprocessed language that compiles into xs so others will not have the same headaches while making maps. That project is still 6 months away at the earliest.

Resource trickle from Trading Posts (representing tribute from conquered peoples); the Aztec were very good in commerce, the central market (Tianguis) was huge.

This mod completely overhauls the AoE3 UI, providing a more informative and engaging experience for newcomers and veterans alike! The following features are revitalized by this mod:

UNIT DESCRIPTIONS: Key strengths, weaknesses, and features are now clearly displayed for nearly every unit in the game.
UNIQUE CHURCH CARDS: Hovering over these cards now tells you what technologies you'll unlock, including their effects, prices, and age requirements.
OTHER HOME CITY CARDS: Particularly vague cards have also had their descriptions overhauled to provide more useful information.
NATIVE TECHNOLOGIES: Native techs have had their effects clarified, and the critical game information is presented more clearly.
NATIVE CAMP DIALOGUES: Where possible, individual native camps now say something unique to the player when clicked on.
TREASURE DESCRIPTIONS: Critical game information is presented more clearly, without losing the fun flavor descriptions.
ABILITIES: A few hero abilities have had their descriptions reworked to provide more clear gameplay information.

This mod IS MULTIPLAYER COMPATIBLE, and it is available in English, Spanish, German, and Portuguese!

Credit:

Casey "Iron Kaiser" S. - English translation, coding
Fobius - Spanish translation (Units, Treasures, Techs), significant advice and assistance.
Gaius Iulius Megas - German translation
Lemos - Portuguese Translation
Testificater - Spanish translation (Unique Church Cards)

modName: Qing Eight Banners,Ming Dynasty,Mongolia明朝清朝蒙古mod
description:
将中国单位修改为清八旗的风格，添加明朝和鞑靼
该mod将战役中国（寻宝船）主城改为明朝样式，可作为可选阵营，将原版中国改为清朝八旗样式，并将战役鞑靼主城改为可选阵营鞑靼人，针对不同文明，相同兵种有不同的外观。
众所周知，帝国时代3中最大的槽点，就是战役中黄坚带领一群清朝的兵，打着黄龙旗去新大陆，这是非常不符合历史的，针对此，我近期研究了战役主城可选技术，并且综合submodel语言，实现了同一个兵种的不同外观和动作，这样就可以实现战役中国的全明朝外观了。此外，历史战役中的鞑靼人也有中国的部分兵种，我也将其改为了游牧民族风格。现在分别介绍一下（明，清，鞑靼，日本的男性村民都有区别）：
1.大明介绍：
大明为战役中国主城修改而成，只能玩家选择，无AI，将战役中国主城的旗帜改为明朝旗帜，英雄为总兵，黄坚，陈烙，所有兵种均为明朝样式，和之前发布的new ming dynasty一样，明朝诸葛弩还是手持弩（满清和鞑靼改为步弓手），此外连枷骑兵增加环臂甲，升级后换上大明铁盔；城堡中增加火枪骑兵和一窝蜂，红衣大炮，武钢车，偏箱车，三眼铳骑兵，铁军，佛郎机炮。可以量产，码头里面增加宝船，最多生产2个，村落模型改为民居样式；此外将铁军改为重步兵属性，近战克骑兵，增加近防和远防；战役中国主城卡片原来是残缺的，现在与中国主城卡片一致，而且最多可以选40张卡；大明的领事馆新增荷兰，西班牙，葡萄牙，土耳其，日本，这些都是历史上与大明打交道的国家，卡片新增"永乐远征"，这是原来系统里面的废卡，打卡后增加明军生产速度，并节约成本。大明长枪兵打卡“旧朝改革”后变身狼筅。
2.大清介绍：
大清为原版中国修改而成，有AI，由于本来这个游戏的中国设定就是大清，所以就将其改为真正的大清八旗样式。所有兵种工业升级为满洲八旗铠甲，帝王升级为近代湘军，淮军，武卫后军，城堡也可以生产镶黄旗的火枪骑兵，还有盾车和红衣大炮，威远将军炮，满洲骑射手，弗朗吉炮，北洋水师和格林机枪，此外码头可以生产定远镇远铁甲舰，铁甲舰可生产北洋水师和格林机枪。
3.鞑靼介绍：
这个鞑靼泛指这个时期的蒙古各个国家政权，我借用了之前boylive16制作的战役主城mod中的鞑靼主城修改而成，只能玩家选择，无AI。将兵种都改为游牧民族的风格，鞑靼英雄是一个骑射将军的形象，还有一个萨满法师，并配上一条宠物狗。鞑靼作为游牧民族，一开局就有200人口，鞑靼的村落为原版蒙古包形式，与大明和满洲的住宅式村落形成鲜明差异
马厩可以生产草原骑兵，怯薛，流星锤，连枷，骑射手，其中草原骑兵一阶段穿好衣服戴好帽子，一改之前原版的光头，怯薛的帽子为新作的蒙古帽子，然后流星锤用的是原版样式，连枷骑兵的高级形式为身穿铁甲，头戴铁盔，颇有草原霸主的感觉，鞑靼骑射手修正为原版样式，和目前的俄罗斯还有土耳其都不一样。鞑靼的步兵在城堡里面生产，有长矛兵，火绳枪，步弓手，长刀兵，投石车，虎蹲炮；其中长矛兵一阶段身穿质孙服，头戴游牧帽，二阶段改为铁盔，三阶段为身穿扎甲和铁盔；火绳枪兵也改为蒙古样式，是用原版模型加蒙古帽子组合的；步弓手也是新的样式，也可打卡获得猛火油柜，虎蹲炮，飞鸭，长刀兵，都是特殊鞑靼外观，鞑靼可以生产伊玛目，是萨满样式，鞑靼村民也是特殊外观。新增"游牧民族"卡片，这是原来系统里面的废卡，打卡村落可以生产村民，新增"巴什基尔叛乱"卡，打开后可以生产鞑靼骑射手。
这次mod还是要感谢阿拉伯的劳伦斯提供的各种模型，吓死蛮牛提供的村落，炮兵，投石车等各种模型，Dablive提供的镶黄旗旗帜，知乎作者DeiGratia制作的架空大明国旗，以及milk制作的一窝蜂和宝船，boylive16制作的鞑靼主城，希望大家在制作新mod的时候，如果要采用此mod中的模型，请提前与我联系（wjp910212@qq.com），因为很多都不是我做的，如果不打招呼就贸然取用是对原作者的不尊重，感谢大家配合
希望大家喜欢，请给五星好评，感谢！

3V3 HOST ONLY, LAST TWO SLOTS FOR BOTS, no single player as of yet (possible, but unlikely). Move files from mod folder to scenario files in your player's directory.

Scenario is a remix of collo, risk and dota 2 (very bad attempt of dota 2*)

This has only been hosted a few times in 2022 and early 2023 before I stop updating it (Not sure if it still compatitble to play as of right now, because  of the updates etc), so expect bugs, OOS & connection issue. Doesnt support single player.

Features

  • Lane Creeps/Minions system. Its like Dota or League, they get stronger every few minutes.
  • There's a day and night cycle for each civilization with unique alignments: Lawful, Chaotic, and Neutral. Lawful civilizations are stronger during the day but weaker at night, while Chaotic civilizations are stronger at night but weaker during the day.
  • Sometimes, wagons arrive on the continent. These wagons are automatically taken over, but you need to protect and guide them to your base. Doing so grants permanent stat boosts, mercenaries, artillery, resources, and hidden units. You can also eliminate your opponent's wagons by controlling specific areas of the map. (Penalties for camping the wagon entrance included)

  • Wagons can also be captured by liberating them from cages, you should also defend these to stop your opponents getting additional advantage. (blockade or something)

  • The main goal is to destroy your opponent's Fort, but it's challenging to do so directly. You need to target and destroy their outposts first. Each time you eliminate one of these defenses, it weakens the central defense of the Fort, making it easier to eventually take down.
  • Upkeep system, you can have as much units on the field, but you got to pay for upkeep for every 100 pop of units including natives.
  • Throughout the maps, you'll find trading posts. Some offer free units or gold, depending on whether there are gold mines nearby. If there are, they generate gold; if not, they provide free units. The flags placed across the map grant a broader line of sight range.

  • Upon achieving 200 kills, you unlock 2 new units. A maximum of 10 civilization units can be unlocked this way. However, the 10th unit is accessible through a wagon, which demands a specialized weapon wagon to be obtained (through the rotational shop).

  • There is a shop system, which gives temporary boost for all your units, however are reset by the end of the day. It opens during day and  close by dawn, it is the only way to also obtain mercenaries wagon, 9th and 10th unit wagon and cheat wagons. The items generated each day are random. (Buying Mercenaries contract only last for 1 day and are part of the random rotation)
  • Each wagon has a rarity system, Common, Uncommon, Rare,  Legendary, Extraordinary and Special. The rarer the better.
  • Obtain gold from trading post, escorting wagons and killing your opponent units.
  • And many, many other features

Alightment System

Lawful - 15% ATK & HP during day and vice versa during night.

Neutral -  No Bonus

Chaotic - 15% ATK & HP during night anda vice versa during day.

 

Lawful Civilization

  • Brits, Dutch, French, Germans, Ottomans, Ports, Russia, Spain, Swedes,

Neutral Civilzation

  • -Chinese, Indians, Japanese, Inca, United States, Mexicans, Italians, Maltese, Finnish.

Chaotic Civilization

  • Aztec, Haude, Lakota, Ethiopians, Hausa, African Tribes, South Americans, Balkans, North Americans

Known Bugs

  • Out of Sync (Out of my control)
  • Connection (Optimized very well but still out of my control as it over 4.8MB trigger file)
  • Trading Post Reward (Some reason will stop working, although rare, out of my control)

It is very hard to host, because a successful connection depends on the player's internet, computer performance and whether the game decides to out of sync or not (maybe could be fix if there was a trigger that effect proto units by their traits instead of making hundreads of trigger for the specific unit type). I also lost the design documents for this game, so I basically lost track of all the hundreads of QV variables and it been a while since I played this game. If you do get connected, enjoy.

description:
**   NEW VERSION (VII) IS UP   **

As always, legacy version remained up for
people who prefer this version more.
