Verify installation size at this folder (977MB) :

C:\Users\YourName\Games\Age of Empires 3 DE\
7something\mods\subscribe

_________________________________________

CREDIT : BIG THANKS TO ALL OF YOU! <3

Initial codebase! -> El Ermitaño (Link to Discord)

Music :

1.) Main Menu and Revolution -> Luis Humanoide

https://www.youtube.com/@LuisHumanoide

Inspiration :
1.) Age of WW1 (Stark22e)
2.) Capybara Civilization (Stark22e)
2.) Unit Cosmetic Mod (boylive16)
3.) Unit Historical Appearance (GwentMaster)

________________________________________

TL;DR CMV VI Features :

> Colonial guardian replaced most generic units.
> Native units became either Roman or Spanish.
> Female troops for archers and skirmishers.
> Default skin explorers get a remesh.
> Priests became Nurses (Native civs included).
> Hausa became an Imperial German colony.
> New artillery, ships, and combat sounds.
> Most mercenaries reskinned.
> Many changes aimed for cool factor.
________________________________________

Original Text :

Are you a fellow veteran of AoE3 who have been playing this game since release and felt that you are getting too old to look at the same musketeer models again and again?

Were you a bit disappointed that AoE4 became a remake of AoE2 instead of a Company of Heroes ripoff?

Are you a fervent supporter of gender-equality and want to see more woman encouraged into traditionally male-dominated professions?

Have you ever wished there were some Spaniards who decided against colonizing the native Aztecs and then helped them modernize instead?

Have you watched Zulu (1964) and think "damn, these Englishmen are sharply dressed" as the British redcoats were getting a taste of the Zulu spear?

Are you crazy about anything Victorian-era related and couldn't stand the sight of native people in their traditional clothing (or lack thereof) and wished to dress them up all nice and proper?

Hell, do you feel a sense of boiling frustration when the word "colonial" was replaced with "commerce" and felt the need to follow your forefather's footsteps by committing genocide and subjugating another civilization with less advanced understanding of science and the word of our lord and saviour Jesus Chirst?

.......

Well, it seems you are in luck today! This mod will fulfill all of the aforementioned statement!......except the last one, you psychopaths.

________________________________________

 


The latest game update changed the mod upload
checks. This rendered most mods that introduced
new file or modified mesh to be recognised as 
"Not Multiplayer Compatible". I hope the devs
did not have any other intention other than to fix
and improve multiplayer stability. But in the mean
-time, we'll have to wait and see.

I waited a decade in hopes of getting an RTS set
in the late-Victorian to pre-WW1 era. AoE4 was
what I thought the logical sequence of timeline is.
Well, AoE4 happened, I kept an open mind and
decided to buy it even though it set in Medieval-
early Gunpowder era. Total disappointment.
Many people considers RTS like AoE2 or SC2 as
the pinnacle of what an RTS game should be.
But, I don't know why after all these years AoE3
is the only RTS game that actually "clicks" it for
me. Maybe it's the timeline, the janky UI, the vast
offering of unit types? I don't know, but AoE3 is
to me, at least, the best RTS game with CoH2 in
close second place.
I hope there are sensible people in charge of this
franchise, and specifically AoE3. Please don't 
let the balance sheet be the downfall of this game.

I know there are people who got handed a similar
"deal". I have no coding knowledge nor 3D model
-rigging skills. But this here is the closest thing to
what I dreamt of "Age of Empires 4". Albeit much
less refined since it's a work of one man. I hope I
can share this journey with you all.

________________________________________

Labyrinth DE - by Pegasus77

This is a multiplayer scenario for up to 6 human players (but also works with fewer or even solo). As a unified team, your objective is to navigate through a huge maze full of enemy units. The enemy is an AI player. Each human player starts with a relatively weak unit, which will transform into a better one with each level up after a certain number of kills has been reached. Next to transformations into better units, killing enemies also rewards you with gold - a vital currency for purchasing valuable upgrades from shops that you can find throughout the map.
The Labyrinth itself is divided into 7 distinct sections, referred to as Stages. In order to advance to the next Stage, your team has to defeat the Boss of the current one. If you manage to kill the final Boss, you win the game. But be careful: The enemies become increasingly stronger with each Stage!

The original Labyrinth was a scenario that I made for Legacy back in 2010, which reached its peak of popularity around 2011-2014. Following the release of the Definitive Edition, I decided to completely remake it while striving to maintain its original spirit. Enjoy!

Installation:

  • Subscribe to this mod and wait for the download to be finished.
  • That's it. You are now able to host the scenario.

Online Setup:

  • In the game browser, press 'Host game'.
  • As game mode, select 'Scenario'.
  • The game now opens your scenario folder. Select the Labyrinth DE scenario file.
  • Press 'Host game'.
  • Player 7 must be an AI player on team 2.
  • All other players must be team 1.
  • You may substitute any number of human players with AI players.
  • Even if you choose to play solo, still host this as a multiplayer scenario to ensure everything works accordingly.

How to play:

  • Each player controls one unit, which is their hero.
  • Should your hero die, it will respawn at the last checkpoint and you will lose 1 food.
  • If your food reaches 0, you lose!
  • Kill enemy units to level up and gain gold.
  • Each time you level up, you will transform into a better unit.
  • At the start of the game choose your desired transformation succession.
  • Use gold to buy powerful upgrades in shops that you can find throughout the map.
  • Over time, you are rewarded with wood, which is needed for mighty magical powers.
  • Each player has a unique magical power that can be activated by pressing the p key on your keyboard.
  • Using your magic power costs 100 wood.
  • The Labyrinth is devided into 7 Stages.
  • To progress to the next Stage, eliminate the current Stage's Boss.
  • Once you have killed that Boss, search for the exit gate.
  • Moving to the next Stage clears units from the previous one, reducing lag.
  • Win by conquering all Stages and defeating the final Boss!

General Features:

  • Choose from 10 different transformation successions with over 20 transformations each!
  • Infinite level-ups that increase your HP and strength (even when you have already reached your final transformation).
  • Your hero can respawn if you still have food left, which makes dying less drastic.
  • 7 Stages with different themes, climates and thematic units.
  • 7 Stage Bosses.
  • 4 Mini Bosses that freely roam the map.
  • Gold gain and upgrade shops.
  • Unique magic powers, triggered by pressing a hotkey.
  • Final Boss that uses magic powers against you.
  • Hidden treasures offering gold, wood, extra lives, or kill points.
  • Countless custom triggers that I scripted just for this map.
  • AI players will distribute evenly among the human players (if possible) and follow them through the maze, joining them in combat!


Transformation Successions:

  • Germanic
  • West-European
  • Scandinavian & East-European
  • Mediterranean
  • African
  • Indian & Middle-Eastern
  • East Asian
  • North-American Natives
  • Central/South-American Natives
  • Revolutionary


Magical Powers:

Each player has access to a mighty magical Power, that is bound to your player slot. You can activate your Power by pressing the p key on your keyboard. Using your Power costs 100 wood, which is countinously awarded in small amounts while you play. Due to AoE3 trigger mechanics, there might be a moment of delay before your Power actually launches. Keep that in mind while playing.
The following Powers are available:

Silver Aura: Unleashes a mighty shockwave that damages all enemy units in its way and provides you with a shield that maximizes your current resistance for 30 seconds.
Team Heal: Self explanatory - this Power heals you and your entire Team. You can only activate this Power once every 30 seconds.
Transformation: You transform into an all-mighty Learicorn for 30 seconds.
Dynamite: A dynamite charge is placed on the ground beside you, which then immediately explodes, severely damaging all enemy units nearby.
Random Military Allies: Two military units, chosen at random, are summoned to assist your hero. After 30 seconds are over, they die.
Clones: Conjures two identical clones of your current hero that will die after 30 seconds. 

Enemy units killed by Powers (Silver Aura, Dynamite) and those killed by summoned units (Random Military Allies, Clones) also count towards your kills. Likewise, you are also rewarded with gold for these kills.

Information:

This is a fairly huge map. Finishing it will probably take a couple of hours, though it highly depends on your playstyle and experience.
Due to its size, loading the map at the start of a multiplayer game might take a moment, especially for those with slower PCs.

If you notice that this mod has been updated, hit the update button in the mod launcher and wait for the download to be finished. Always ensure you're playing the most recent version (highest version number).


Good luck and have fun!

by Pegasus77

by BIRON

This is an ambitious new mod designed to completely overhaul the way ship combat works in this game. Ships will naturally excel in either long, medium, or close range engagements by having better LOS, better accuracy, or the ability to lay down consistent broadsides. 

Standard ships have been combined into one shared build limit, allowing you to make up to 12 ships in any combination. Stick with the well rounded Frigate, or combine the long line of sight and accuracy of the Caravel with the disciplined broadside of a Galleon to outmanuever your opponent's fleet.

European, Asian, and Colonial ship types have been reworked. Stay tuned for a rework of African and Native American ships, as well as heavy balance changes.

This is very much a WiP alpha build, so please bear with me and let me know if you encounter any issues!

Compatibility: This mod is compatible with both Assertive Seawall Naval AI and Age of Pirates.

---

Planned Changes:

  • More ship types
  • More clearly defined roles
  • Bonuses and auras for some ship types
  • More techs
  • Rework to African and Native Navies
  • Balance

Feedback is always appreciated, so please let me know on the forums if you have any suggestions for improvements or what should be included!

TL;DR CMV VII Features :

> Most infantry received a 1900-styled uniforms.

> Female troops for archers and skirmishers.
> Default skin explorers get a remesh.
> Priests became Nurses (Native civs included).
> Hausa became an Imperial German colony.
> New artillery, ships, and combat sounds.
> Most mercenaries reskinned.
> New custom portraits for most standard units.
> Stable and Monastery function as radio stations.

________________________________________

Original Text :

Are you a fellow veteran of AoE3 who have been playing this game since release and felt that you are getting too old to look at the same musketeer models again and again?

Were you a bit disappointed that AoE4 became a remake of AoE2 instead of a Company of Heroes ripoff?

Are you a fervent supporter of gender-equality and want to see more woman encouraged into traditionally male-dominated professions?

Have you ever wished there were some Spaniards who decided against colonizing the native Aztecs and then helped them modernize instead?

Have you watched Zulu (1964) and think "damn, these Englishmen are sharply dressed" as the British redcoats were getting a taste of the Zulu spear?

Are you crazy about anything Victorian-era related and couldn't stand the sight of native people in their traditional clothing (or lack thereof) and wished to dress them up all nice and proper?

Hell, do you feel a sense of boiling frustration when the word "colonial" was replaced with "commerce" and felt the need to follow your forefather's footsteps by committing genocide and subjugating another civilization with less advanced understanding of science and the word of our lord and saviour Jesus Chirst?

.......

Well, it seems you are in luck today! This mod will fulfill all of the aforementioned statement!......except the last one, you psychopaths.

________________________________________

Culmination of the fantasy I developed after felling from a bed while sleeping.....

This one will be the last version I will upload, at least for a while until we get some new items/units added by devs in new DLC.

This mod is not WW1 or WW2, but I incorporated some of the period musics just to build the atmosphere.

I don't think I have to say this, but just in case. All the characters are fictional, and this mod is not a political statement of any kind.

Some elements (such as music tracks) can be swapped between prior versions by going to the directory mentioned at the start.

Make sure .mp3 and .wav file extension matched what you intended to swap with.

Enough reading, now it's time for you to go die for your nation! Adieu.

modName: Age of the World - Campaign Civs Patch
description:
DISCLAIMER: THIS IS NOT A FULL MOD, THIS IS ONLY A VERY SMALL PATCH TO ENABLE THE CIRCLE OF OSSUS AND THE KNIGHTS OF ST. JOHN IN THE AGE OF THE WORLD MOD AND IT WILL NEED TO BE INSTALLED TO PLAY WITH THE CIRCLE OF OSSUS!!!!!!!

DISCLAIMER 2: MAKE SURE THIS PATCH IS HIGHER IN THE PRIORITY ORDER THAN THE MAIN AGE OF THE WORLD MOD IN THE MODS MANAGER OR IT WILL NOT WORK AND CAUSE ERRORS!!!!

So, as advertised, this is merely a small patch that enables the Circle of Ossus and the Knights of St. John in Age of the World. These civilizations are separated from the others because they are fantasy civilizations based in the Age of Empires III campaign and not rooted in history, so they are separated from the other civilizations to not break emersion from players who prefer to play with only historically based civilizations.

That being said, the Campaign civs have a plethora of unique features, mechanics, buildings and units that make them worth exploring!

Downloading this patch will enable the Campaign civs to be played by you *and* the AI.

To play Age of the World multiplayer, all players need to have the Campaing civs patch enabled or disabled.

Download the full Age of the World Mod here: https://www.ageofempires.com/mods/details/126056

This is an ornamental map of Venice. I have not added any gameplay or units.

Also, please feel free to use this map if you want to make a scenario, but credit this mod :)

There is an explored and an explorable version of the map.

This was just an exercise in trying to make a map that looks like Venice as part of a long term 'Diorama' project of mine. Keep an eye out for the Diorama series in the months after the release of this map. I took it out because it is much bigger than the others and has less ambience, so it didn't fit the theme of the collection.

Note that I may update this mod in the future if I come back to this mod over the course of finishing my Diorama series.

Changes the sound for the Divine Strike ability of Japanese monks to have a female voice (I borrowed the knocked-out sound for one of the singleplayer campaign characters) for when using one of the Japanese female hero cosmetics. Since this is not a data mod, it can more easily be enabled/disabled whenever you decide to change your Japanese hero cosmetic (unfortunately, due to how the game engine works, an ability can only have one sound effect).

This mod adds some universal arsenal techs like Military Drummers and Trunion for civs that lack a way to upgrade the speed of their infantry and/or artillery.

Military Drummers has been added to all three Asian civs, Haudenosaunee and Hausa, and Ethiopia. China and Ethiopia can now research Military Drummers for 5 % extra movement speed to complement their team cards that buff infantry speed by 5 % at half the price. The Ethiopian Coffe Consumption card now affects all infantry units not just ranged infantry.

Units that are quick by default are unaffected by the added tech.

Ranged Cavalry Caracole is also available for all three Asian civs with Howdahs and Yabusame Riders unaffected.

These additional arsenal techs are available in Indian houses and Chinese villages after sending Royal Green Jackets and Repelling Volley respectively.

The effects of Counter Infantry Rifling and Ranged Cavalry Caracole are now available to all civs one way or another as well, now your native alliance skirmishers can finally compete with Euro civs.

Inca and Aztecs get the Rifling bonus by sending the support shipment of their skirmisher unit, Africans through their arsenal and China and India through the shipments mentioned above and Japan with the Dutch consulate.

Added Incendiary Grenades to be available for Germans, Portuguese and Spanish by default.

  • Duvarlar ticaret yolu üzerine inşa edilebilir.
  • Demir At (Tren) ve Ticaret Arabası yol üzerindeki birimlere ve kapısız duvarlara hasar verebilir.
  • Osmanlılar için değirmenden otomatik Spahi üretimi.
  • Osmanlı Ana Şehri ve Camii'ne Ezan ve Sela Ambiyansı.
  • Ölü bedenler kalıcı.
  • Unuttuğum bazı küçük değişiklikler olabilir. Bazı değişikler önceki modlardan esinlenildi.

Who is SuperJenya?
This character became famous in the Russian community for making aoe3 guides. As you understand, he was Ukrainian. All the rofls about him went because of his behavior, self-importance and stupidity. But not only for these reasons this mod was created to make fun of this character, but also for other big reasons that it will take a lot of time to tell. Today, the Russian community is divided in two. In one, political nonsense is going on, in the other, everyone gets along with each other and plays for fun.

REVOLUTIONARY CHILEANS

This is a simple mod with certain suggestions to expand the Chilean Revolution deck.

[Old text written before the official December 2023 patch, retained for context]

Some explanations: The last time Chile’s revolution was updated, several cards focused primarily on the maritime economy were added, but most are cards from ages I and II, when revolutions need cards with a power of IV+ to be useful. “Fishing Fleet” and “2 Steamers” are good cards, but the rest that are not shared with other revs are not as good and you are forced to play on maps with sea to effectively use the Chilean revolution, and I think that the Colombian revolution with its imperial ships is superior on sea maps. For all this, I thought it was necessary to make a proposal on how to improve the Chilean revolution, in the form of a mod. I think that the biggest problem that the Chilean revolution has is the great power that the Hussars of Death have, which makes it a somewhat more difficult revolution to balance, which could be the reason why they added such low level cards in their last update.

For this reason I do not include cards that affect cavalry or sea, I only add cards related to the land economy, artillery and infantry. Perhaps this combination of cards may seem somewhat unbalanced, but my intention is to propose some ideas about what can be done with Chile.

NEW CARDS:

  • Fundos: Ships 1 Hacienda Wagon and increase the Hacienda build limit +5. Replaces the construction of Livestock Pens with Haciendas. (Historical context: Much of the Chilean rural economy consisted of numerous haciendas that are also called fundos.)
  • Barbacoa: Haciendas fatten Livestock faster and will automatically harvest them when fully-fattened. (Historical context: The Asado, barbacoa, parrillada, etc. is a common characteristic for Latin Americans in general, very important in Chilean culture.)
  • U.S. Guns: (1000c) Ships 7 Gatling Guns and enables them at the Artillery Foundry. Replaces Dragoon training with Carbine Cavalry. Allows you to research Heavy Gatling Guns and Imperial Carbine Cavalry at a reduced cost (-20%).
  • French Guns: (800c) Ships 5 Napoleon Guns and enables them at the Artillery Foundry and Tavern. Allows you to research Imperial upgrades for Napoleon Guns and Musketeers at a reduced cost (-20%).
  • German Guns: (1000c) Ships 4 Horse Artillery. Allows you to research Imperial upgrades for Horse Artillery and Skirmishers at a reduced cost (-20%).
  • British Guns: (1000c) Ships 3 Heavy Cannons. Allows you to research Imperial Cannons and Heavy Ironclads at a reduced cost (-20%).

These cards represent how Chile, a small country, was able to purchase large quantities of modern weapons. Historical context: Starting in the 1870s, due to the recent war against Spain (Chincha Islands War, 1865-1866), and in preparation for a possible war due to boundary problems with its neighboring countries, Chile began to purchase large quantities of weapons to the main military powers of the time. The use of these weapons was fundamental in the War of the Pacific (1879 - 1884). Among the most important purchases are:

-Artillery: Krupp (Prussian), Armstrong (British), La Hitte (French). (Krupp guns were the main artillery pieces of the Chilean army during the War of the Pacific.)
-Rifles: Gras (French), Comblain (Belgian), Kropatschek (Austrian). (Gras and Comblain were the main rifles used by the Chilean army during the War of the Pacific, and both were adapted to use the same ammunition.)
-Carbines: Wínchester (United States), Spencer (United States). (The main police force of Chile is currently called "Carabineros de Chile", due to the historical importance of these weapons in the country.)
-Machine guns: Gattling (United States), Hotchkiss (French).
-Steamers: Magallanes (British, Corvette, 1874).
-Ironclads: Almirante Cochrane, Blanco Encalada (Both were built in the United Kingdom in 1875. After capturing the Peruvian Ironclad Huáscar, and purchasing more ships, Chile came to possess, for a time, the largest fleet in the Pacific, which among other things allowed it to annex Easter Island, which belongs to Chile to this day.)
*This listing does not include weapons or warships acquired before 1870. Source: González (2010), "La artillería en la guerra del pacífico".

  • Civic Battalions: Ships 10 Musketeers and they cost -25% but have -20% hit points and attack. Enables your Musketeers to benefit from Combat promotions to receive extra hitpoints and damage for each defeated enemy military unit. (Historical context: The soldiers belonging to the civic battalions were militia units (non-professional), who received little training, but who trained regularly and with a strong dose of military tradition and patriotism, so the Chilean state could recruit a considerable number of soldiers with a certain basic level of training in a short time. The civic battalions were of great importance during the War of the Pacific against Peru and Bolivia, where the majority of Chilean soldiers came from these battalions. After participating prominently in some battle, civic battalions could be promoted to professional line infantry regiments.)
  • O'Higgins: Bernardo O'Higgins arrives to lead your revolutionary army! Ships 1 General that can receive Home City shipments, build Cathedrals, and inspire military units. (Historical context: Bernardo O'Higgins Riquelme (20 August 1778 – 24 October 1842) was a Chilean independence leader who freed Chile from Spanish rule in the Chilean War of Independence. He was a wealthy landowner of Basque-Spanish and Irish ancestry. Although he was the second Supreme Director of Chile (1817–1823), he is considered one of Chile's founding fathers, as he was the first holder of this title to head a fully independent Chilean state. He was Captain General of the Chilean Army, Brigadier of the United Provinces of the Río de la Plata, General Officer of Gran Colombia and Grand Marshal of Peru.) [Extracted from Wikipedia]

NEW TECH:

  • Our Lady of Carmen: (250wc, Available at the Cathedral) Ships 1 Cathedral Wagon. Cathedrals generate Experience at a much faster rate. (Historical context: Chile has numerous cathedrals, and the devotion to the Virgin of Carmen is the most important in traditional Chilean Christianity.)

The Votive Temple of Maipú (Spanish: Templo Votivo de Maipú) or Basilica of Our Lady of Mount Carmel (Spanish: Basílica de Nuestra Señora del Carmen) is a Catholic church located in the Chilean town of Maipú. Its construction was ordered by Bernardo O'Higgins in 1818 as an act of thanksgiving to Our Lady of Mount Carmel for the intercession for the victory of the Chilean Army in the Battle of Maipú, where the Independence of Chile was assured. It is called votive because of the vows that O'Higgins made to the Virgin Mary. [Extracted from Wikipedia]

----------------------------------------------------
V1.1:

-Added new official cards from the December 2023 update.

-Chilean Mining: (Improves Coin gather rates) Card Removed.

-Fundos: No longer improves Settler spawn rates. Now replaces the construction of Livestock Pens with Haciendas.

-----

This is my pitch for a possible improvement for the Chilean revolution. If any developer is interested in my design, Please feel free to use it and contact me if you want, I’d love to see some of my content made official. Any questions, suggestions or corrections are welcome.

This mod should work in any language, but with texts only in English or Spanish (Only if the Spanish language is selected).

Have Fun!

Enhanced AI
Author: EsteGringo
Version: 1.0.9
Last Updated: December 29th, 2025

 

Changes in 1.0.9:
AI allies are now more cooperative. They are more likely to help you defend your base or listen to your attack requests.
AI allies are now more likely to accept your requests for coordinated attacks. (The necessary conditions for this were nearly-impossible by default!)
Major improvements for rushing behavior in the AI.
Fixed an issue where the AI would not always use its dock wagon on certain maps.
Fixed an issue involving the rebuilding of forts.
Enhanced forward base behavior.
Various enhancements to strategies.
Livestock gathering improvements.
The AI may create a small invasion base if it intends to invade a large island.
Ottomans are more likely to revolt as the Barbary States on island maps.
Civilizations favor their royal guard units more and are less likely to use weak units later in the game.
Units are more likely to be trained at secondary bases.
Portuguese will try to maintain their maximum amount of town centers if feitorias is active.

Changes in 1.0.8:
Adjustments to land-based patrols so they are more likely to return to base if it is under attack.

Changes in 1.0.7:
Refactored the naval invasion logic some more.
Added King of the Hill support.
Fixed an issue where too much artillery was being trained.
Added Swahili Coast map support. (I forgot about this map!)
Added the ability for the AI to collect sea treasures.
Refactored various parts of the code for better performance on older or low-end systems.

Changes in 1.0.6:
Updated card selection logic.
More refinements to various parts of the naval invasion logic.

Changes in 1.0.5:
AI now trains allied naval units if they are available for training.
AI now makes sure all docks are accompanied by at least one tower within a certain range on naval maps.
AI now makes sure at least one tower is near each factory.
Fixed an issue with Battleship transportation dropoff points.
Fixed an issue that could cause the invading armies to stay on the coast or run around in circles, even after 
finding the enemy base.
AI will now maintain a couple of priests or monks.
Added logic so the AI can begin to use healing units to heal injured units.
AI may now build as many churches, basilicas, or mosques as it can.
Added more improvements to how the AI ages up.
Refactored some naval logic.
Improved the prioritization of various plans.

Changes in 1.0.4:
Fixed an issue that prevented the AI from making up to 7 docks.
Fixed an issue that prevented the AI from making enough fishing boats on naval maps.
Fixed an issue that dealt with military maintenance.
Randomized the new dock attack logic so it will randomly select a dock from a list of docks it has seen.

Changes in 1.0.3:
The AI is better at aging up.
The AI selects more beneficial and interesting politicians now.
Japanese will now train Shinobi if they have Daimyo Tokugawa present.
The AI should perform better on Ceylon.
The AI will send some of its troops to destroy enemy trade posts or patrol the map.
The AI should attack a lot more often on higher difficulties once it gets a decent economy going.
The AI will now occasionally try to destroy all of your docks on water maps just to be cruel.
If economic conditions permit and the AI is in the Imperial Age, they will create extra military buildings.
Custom AI card deck creation now supports distinction between land and water maps. Name your decks "Enhanced-Land" for land maps and "Enhanced-Naval" for water maps. Rename your "Enhanced" decks appropriately for compatibility with this update. The readme section regarding this has also been updated.

Changes in 1.0.2:
You may now create your own decks for the AI to use. See the instructions in the readme
file that is included with the mod for details.

Changes in 1.0.1:
AI will now more consistently maintain their maximum amount of towers. (Up to 14 with cards!)
Lakota/Sioux build and maintain teepees now.
Fixed a bug that prevented ships from training units during island invasions.
Rearranged and refactored some code, also made some things more performant. (Technical stuff, it doesn't matter.)

Changes in 1.0.0:
Enabled Aztecs to train Skull Knights via ceremonies.
Improved naval invasion logic.
If ############## is researched and the fort build limit has not been reached, Explorers will now be tasked to build a fort.
The AI will now try to maintain as many trade posts and alliance units as it can.
Greatly improved warship behavior.
Removed some redundant logic.

Changes in 0.9.9:
Improved the AI's ability to rush and made it more aggressive in general.
Aztecs now train Eagle Scouts.
Spanish now train Missionaries and War Dogs. They use Unction too.
Chinese now train Disciples.
Italians make use of more Architects now.
Minor improvements to the Archipelago logic. It is still very experimental.

Changes in 0.9.8:
Greatly improved card selection for: Aztecs, Chinese, Ethiopians, Huadenosaunee/Iroquois, 
Huasa, Inca, India, Italians, Japanese, Maltese, Mexicans, Sioux/Lakota, and USA.
The AI will use Livestock Pens more often now and they are no longer restricted to just land maps.
Adjustments to the naval logic.
Fixed an issue with ships loading artillery due to a recent official game patch change.
Very experimental custom Archipelago logic is now implemented. The AI will likely not be a challenge on Archipelago yet.
The AI will now build bases on islands in the Archipelago map. This will be implemented for land and other sea maps soon.
Refactored various areas.

Changes in 0.9.7:
German Settler Wagon maintenance has been improved. It worked before, but it's more consistent now.
Improvements to card deck selection.
British AI can use Florence Nightingale if they select that card and research it.
Warships are more aggressive.
Livestock pens are now used by the AI for land maps!
Ships should respond to being attacked a little bit better.
Handicaps brought back for Hardest and Extreme difficulties for now. Handicaps were brought back
because it is too difficult for some people to read this mod's description.

This is a project to make the AI more interesting and competitive. It is usable on both land and sea maps. It is not setup for Archipelago yet, but it will be. 

This is an open source project by its nature, so you are free to do as you will with it. If you want to use code from this, give me credit for what you take from this project.

Some Main Features:
It will cross the sea, land troops, and attack you.
It will use battleships.
Ships will patrol the seas.
It builds walls.
The AI uses church techs and units now.
The AI will use special units. For example, Germans will train Settler Wagons, the Dutch will train Blue Guards,
and Ottomans will train Nizam Fusiliers.
Card selection has been modified so more upgrade cards are used.

Upcoming Features and Fixes:
New base building patterns for the AI.
Fix the minor bugs involving island invasions. Sometimes invading units stay on the coast for awhile, for example.
General refinements to the military and economic strategies.
Find out why the Ottomans do not select the infinite Sipahi cards.
for mates who know what it is

**DISCLAIMER: This mod is not intended to support slavery or the Lost Cause of the Confederacy, it is only made for historical interest, fun, to include more States and gameplay elements, etc.**

CONFEDERATE STATES OF AMERICA

This mod allows you to revolt from the United States of America (USA) in the ages II, III and IV to become the Confederate States of America (CSA), and you can choose to Age Up within the CSA in ages III and IV, choosing another Confederate State. It also allows you to return from the CSA to the USA in the Ages III, IV and V.

The time it takes for the revolution to happen is the same as it takes a normal Age Up in their respective Ages (no quick revolt). When a revolution is made from the USA to the CSA, the cost is 700/1000/2500fwc to Age III/IV/V, the Deck changes to a Confederate Deck, the Settlers become State Militia (Guard State Militia in age IV, Volunteers in age V) and Gatling Guns production is replaced with Falconets. When advancing in Age from one CSA State to another CSA State, or when returning from the CSA to the USA, the cost is the same as a normal Age Up. When returning from the CSA to the USA, the production of Gatling Guns and your original Deck are restored, keeping Federal Cards. In no case is the production of Settlers blocked.

CONFEDERATE FEDERAL STATES

Confederate Tennessee (III), Kentucky (III), South Carolina (IV), Texas (V) and Florida (V): Same stuff as usual.

Plus 10 new States!

Fortress Age (III)

  • Confederate Arizona: (700fwc) Ships 400 Wood. 
    FEDERAL CARDS
    -Arizona Cowboys: Ships 7 Cowboys and enables them to gather food from herdable animals.
    -Sonora Valleys: Villagers gather from all natural resources and livestock faster.
  • Confederate Missouri: (700fwc) Ships 400 Wood.
    FEDERAL CARDS
    -Bushwhackers: Ships 10 random Wild West outlaws.
    -Partisan Ranger Act: Ships 3 Wild West outlaws. Removes the shipment cost of the cards "Advanced Saloon" and "Dance Hall".
  • Confederate Oklahoma: (700fwc) Ships 4 Cherokee Riflemen.
    FEDERAL CARDS
    -Confederate Cherokee: Ships 4 Cherokee Riflemen, 1 Native Embassy Travois and allies you with the Cherokee at your Native Embassy where some of their upgrades and units are available.
    -Native Mounted Volunteers: Ships 6 Native Carbine Cavalry, ready to defeat the enemy cavalry, and allows you to train some more at the Stable.

Note: During the Civil War the State of Oklahoma did not yet exist, but some of the Indian tribes of the territory corresponding to the current State of Oklahoma supported the Confederacy.

Industrial Age (IV)

  • Confederate Arkansas: (1000fwc) Ships 600 Wood. Enables Industrial Age stuff, but remaining in the III Age.
    FEDERAL CARDS
    -State Troops: Ships 12 State Militia and allows them to construct economic buildings. Mills, Estates, Trading Posts and Town Centers cost -25%.
    -Third Arkansas Regiment: (300fwc) Ships 10 State Militia, 10 Regulars and 10 Sharpshooters. Inspiring Flags reduce its cooldown time to a third and can be placed anywhere. 
  • Confederate Georgia: (1000fwc) Federal Cards of Georgia are stronger. Enables Industrial Age stuff, but remaining in the III Age.
    FEDERAL CARDS
    -Augusta Arsenal: Ships 3 Falconets and they get +20% hit points, -20% train time and take up one less population slot.
    -Columbus Supply Network: Industrial Age unit upgrades only cost Wood. Trade routes generate +35% resources.
  • Confederate Mississippi: (1000fwc) Ships 1 Horse Artillery. Enables Industrial Age stuff, but remaining in the III Age.
    FEDERAL CARDS
    -Mississippi River: Ships 4 Infrastructure Wagons. All shipments arrive considerably faster, including this one.
    -Vicksburg: Ships 1 Fort Wagon and 3 Outpost Wagons; Fort build limit +1, Outpost build limit +3.
  • Confederate North Carolina: (1000fwc) Ships 1 Horse Artillery. Enables Industrial Age stuff, but remaining in the III Age.
    FEDERAL CARDS
    -Wilmington Blockade Runners: Crate shipments from cards are deposited directly into your stockpile and deliver additional resources. Any which have been sent may be sent again.
    -Fort Fisher: Ships 1 Fort Wagon and 3 Culverins.

Imperial Age (V)

  • Confederate Louisiana: (2500fwc) Mercenary and Outlaw get +25% hit points and attack.
    FEDERAL CARDS
    -Louisiana Tigers: Ships an additional Zouave mercenary each time this card is sent, starting at 10.
    -European Brigade: (2500c) Ships 4 Highlanders, 4 Jaegers, 4 Fusiliers and 4 Mounted Riflemen. Increases all mercenary attack and hitpoints.
  • Confederate Virginia: (2500fwc) Mercenary and Outlaw get +25% hit points and attack.
    FEDERAL CARDS
    -Tredegar Iron Works: All artillery, buildings and ships are constructed faster.
    -Bellona Arsenal: (2500c) Ships 9 Napoleon Guns. Increases all Mercenary attack and hitpoints.
  • Confederate Alabama: (2500fwc) 1 Fort Wagon and +1 Fort build limit.
    FEDERAL CARDS
    -Selma Naval Foundry and Ironworks: Ships 3 Heavy Cannons. Enables an additional Ironclad to be constructed.
    -Mobile Bay: Forts have more range and become able to attack other buildings.

Note: Revolting to Alabama, Texas and Florida adds to the Artillery Foundry the tech "La Hitte Guns", an Imperial upgrade for Napoleon Guns.

NEW CARDS

-Texan Haciendas: (III) Ships 2 Hacienda Wagons and enables Haciendas to automatically harvest Livestock when fully-fattened. Haciendas can be rebuilt at the State Capitol with a limit of 2.
-Artillery Company: Ships 2 Napoleon Guns and enables them at the Artillery Foundry and Tavern.
-Regular Company: (III, 250f) Ships 11 Regulars
-Ranger Company: (III, 300 f) Ships 10 Sharpshooters
-Hussar Company: (III, 400f) Ships 7 Hussars

-Confederate Battalions: (IV, 500f) Ships 5 Regulars, 5 Sharpshooters and 5 Hussars. Upgrades Company cards to Battalions, that send more units, and any which have been sent may be sent again.
-Artillery Battalion: Ships 4 Napoleon Guns and enables them at the Artillery Foundry and Tavern.
-Regular Battalion: (IV) Ships 16 Regulars
-Ranger Battalion: (IV) Ships 15 Sharpshooters
-Hussar Battalion: (IV) Ships 9 Hussars

-French Arms: (V, 900c) Ships 6 Napoleon Guns and enables them at the Artillery Foundry and Tavern. Decreases the cost of all unit upgrades -10%.
-Mexican Arms: (V, 800c) Ships 5 Falconets. Decreases the cost of all unit upgrades -10%.
-German Arms: (V, 800c) Ships 4 Horse Artillery. Decreases the cost of all unit upgrades -10%.
-British Arms: (V, 800c) Ships 3 Heavy Cannons. Decreases the cost of all unit upgrades -10%.

-----

Why the CSA?
-The American Civil War is an event of great historical relevance for the time this game is set
-Allows more American States to be included
-Allows more Gameplay elements to be included
-Other countries have many revolutions, Mexico has 7 revolutions, but the United States has none (Bear Flag Revolt is more of a pseudo revolution)

Why the CSA battle flag (aka rebel flag) and not the actual CSA flag?
The CSA used 3 flags during its existence, the icons and flags in the game look very small and none of these official flags can be seen well. Using the battle flag makes more sense for the Inspiring Flag. Also, the battle flag looks cooler.

-----

Mod created by Ahriman204

Hello everyone, this is my first mod, so I apologize in advance for any bugs you might encounter.

It has been my dream to play with any South Slavic nation in AOE since I first played AOE1. I have long hoped to see at least one of them added to AOE2, as that would be the most plausible timeline. Inspired by the amazing civ mods in the workshop, especially Age of the World, I thought about creating my own civ. I ran into a comment from the creator of the Just Poland mod, where he basically described how he created his civ, by simply overwriting an existing civ mod. Inspired by his courage, I did the same, with his mod as the basis. The problem was that I did not know how to create models, and he had all the Polish ones already in the mod. It became clear to me that the only way to bring the South Slavs into the game was to create a faction that incorporates all of them, as I had models for some of the units already in the game. I planned a very humble civ with only a few units, but as my skills grew, so did my plans become more ambitious. I hope you will enjoy the final result.

Now historically, the South Slavic peoples were mostly under control of other countries during the time period of AoE 3. However, there have been strong movements among the main people groups for the creation of a state where all South Slavs could live freely, which eventually did happen in the 20th century. There were many individual rebellions, but very few unified ones.

This civilization is composed of different units from all South Slavic peoples. It represents a hypothetical civ that could have been created if the South Slavs had united during the AOE 3 era. As its army would have been composed mostly of soldiers who fought as either mercenaries or foreign levies, most of their roster benefits from promotions. You start with units from the three central South Slavic peoples: Serbs, Croats, and Bosniaks and, while aging up, you slowly liberate the other people groups. Every time you liberate a Slavic people, in addition to a thematic bonus, you also unlock a unique unit in the liberation camp. The unique units were realized so that they complement the civ's weaknesses and are strong and cheap units, but have a build limit of 15. As you can only unlock one unique unit per age, there is a strategic decision to be made when aging up.

I hope you will have as much fun playing the civ as I had making it. I don't know how well-balanced the civ is, so I am looking forward to your feedback on that. The civ does not have an AI, but I might tackle that in some of the future updates. Update 2.0 will contain bugfixes, more unique cards for the deck, and unique voicelines for most units, as currently, they all share the same. The WoL team was so kind to provide me with the voicelines for the villagers, and I'll try to come up with some more for the remainder of the units.

If the civilization does not appear after subscribing to the mod, please restart AOE 3.

Enjoy the mod,
Ahriman204
A mod which buffs the royal decree techs in the campaign. In addition ships pop blocks to support the units given.

The last DLC and the European content drop revamped the design philosophies of European civs. The inclusion of the royal houses, new cards and maps of the unexplored setting of the 1600 kinda move the theming of these civs away from the original idea of “settlers in the Americas”. Back when the game first launched the devs intended the colonial setting as a way to keep the “starting anew” feeling from previous games.

As such, these civilizations have become “umbrela” civs like the Hausa or the Indians. That is, civs that are meant to represent a broad period of time, territorial extensions and cultural diversity. Take for example the French: They retain their original fur trader identity (CdBs, native alliances, wilderness warfare), incorporating their new Bourbon identity (royal muskeeters, royal army, bourbon troops) and revolutionary (french revolution units), Napoleonic France, and even Imperial France (zuaves).

With this understanding, I’m tried creating a mod, just as a concept proof. For this I picked the Spanish civilization, the european civ I'm most familiar with.

I tried to make a modular subfaction civ in which you start branching out into different paths.

From base Spain, you can opt for 3 different spanish subfactions in age up.

  • Picking up the Continental Spain, you will be able to access to the spanish gold card and Tercio armies, banner armies of pikeman and early skirmishers that cost food and gold.
  • Choosing the Viceroyalty of New Spain will grant access to haciendas (and eventualy Soldados down the line) and Zapotec Allies.
  • Finally, the Viceroyalty of Peru will focus on mining bonuses and renegade bands (Helbardier and conquistador shipments).

Only the Viceroyalty of Peru is sorta finished, with options up to the industrial age. This path also has a different tech tree.

In the end, I found this way of doing things wasn't the most appropiate, as there are many changes that are imposible to do with by just tweaking an existing civilization (for example, making VoP have a slower xp curve to create a mechanic where you exchange gold for an xp boost), and this ended up creating issues (that I eventualy solved) with the UI.

Want to try it out? Here you have.

Battle of Palashi - 1757

 

Nawab Siraj was the grandson of great Nawab of Bengal, Ali Vardi Khan, who defeated the English and Maratha empire in battles. Now Nawab showed same ambition as him and challenged the East India Company influenced by French East India Company. Siraj wants to kick the English out of Bengal but Lord Robert Clive will not leave the riches of India so easily. What follows is the battles of Plassey that saw betrayal , chivalry and struggle. This battle paved the way for the dominance of British empire in India and the decline of French in India. In the end what was to be just a small battle during the seven years war proved to be one of the most pivotal moment of Indian history.

Eighteen minutes. What could you do in eighteen minutes? You could watch most of a show, play some Age of Empires, cook a meal... but in April 1836 the Texan revolutionaries under command of General Sam Houston won independence for the fledgling Republic of Texas by defeating the army of General Santa Anna on the banks of the San Jacinto River in just eighteen minutes. As the Republic of Texas, the Texans fielded their own Army, had their own Navy, and even a Marine Corps. They established relations and trade with foreign powers including France, and the federalist republics of Yucatán and the Rio Grande when they too revolted against Santa Anna.

A fan favorite what if civilization due to the Battle of the Alamo and other facets of the Texas Revolution becoming immortalized in pop culture, this mod introduces the Texans as a fully playable civilization, taking a stroll through Texas history for inspiration on units, mechanics, cards, and even their twist on Politicians which are all figures straight out of the history books.

Notes

Steam Users - Disable Steam Cloud for this game before attempting to download this and other civilization mods! The Steam Cloud wreaks havoc with mods' home city files and can render them unplayable with it enabled.

This mod may have compatability issues with other mods. All players will need to install it if you wish to play multiplayer with it. It is in English only, though it has English string files for all other languages. I did start working on the AI code but the way the Ranch works presented an issue that may not be possible to bypass, so there is no AI support at this time. 

There are a few cosmetic changes to content from the original game:  the Mexican's Texas revolt now uses the Burnet flag of the early Republic of Texas instead of the later Lone Star flag; Frigates for American factions (United States, Mexico, Texas, and campaign civlilizations) now have a star on their sails; Mexicans and Texans have regional Settler models; the Explorer unit displays less extraneous attack information as the Heavy Strike and Swashbuckler attack have been coded to be unlocked by their respective cards and not be always active.

Features

  • Founding Fathers and Mothers of Texas: Historical Texan figures replace traditional Politicians, and add unique cards to the Home City deck over the course of a match. From the Marqués to the Defiant Defender, the Founders' cards provide a toolkit to adapt and evolve your deck based on the flow of the match.
  • Volunteers: Upon reaching the Fortress Age, two cards are added to your deck which allow you to build Volunteer Camps which automatically train armies of powerful volunteers to augment your army. Additional unit types can be unlocked at your Volunteer Camps by shipping their cards found in the Harbor.
  • Unique units: It wouldn't be Texas without the Texas Rangers, and they are represented by the Mounted Ranger, a Dragoon variant which can dismount to fight on foot as stealthy ranged shock infantry. The shotgun-wielding Trooper replaces the Hussar as a hand cavalry unit that can also fight at short range, the Rifleman replaces the archaic Crossbowman, and the Texian Militia is Texas' unique Musketeer which can attack targets faster as their hitpoints dip.
  • National Guard units: Texian Militia (Milam Guard) and Mounted Ranger (Texas Ranger).
  • Ranching:  Ranching has been a major Texan industry for nearly three centuries, and this is represented by the two cows they begin the match with along with their economic building, the Ranch. Ranches fatten livestock and can be toggled to produce Food, Coin, sheep, or cattle.

modName: Improved Music: a party of sounds!
description:
Don't Forget to tell your friends about this mod, so you can experience the inmersive experience in your multiplayer games!!!

This mod aims to improve the A E S T H E T I C experience of playing a game. Specifically, is intended for music-heads who really would want to feel inmersed in the era of Age of Empires, including in the musical aspect.

Features:

  • New feature-lenght jingles for many civs
    • Rule Britania for the British Empire
    • Battle Hymn of the Republic for USA
    • Huapango de Moncayo for Mexico
    • Many more... and many more to come!
  • More thematically and complete ability sounds
    • Listen to the full Radetzky March while allied with the Habsburg
    • Bourbon royal armies march at the beat of La Mussette
    • Celebrate the Royal Fireworks with La Rejoussanse
    • ...With the towrow row row row row of the British Grenadiers
  • Whole New sound effects!
    • Listen at the Big Ben when you use Greenwich Time
  • Much more to come!!!!

Known Issues

  • Many sounds last longer than their intended effects, IE, Bourbon Royal March last for less than the half of their sound. Tit for tat shrugs
  • As we creaded new sound effects, we had to modify files that make the game not compatible with multiplayer. If you like this mod, please tell your friends to subscribe and like.

Known bugs

Unforunatelly, as many civ themes are longer than the original jingles, is not unusual that ambient music won't sound for a while after the theme is over. It seemeling fixes at random if left alone.

Mostly not my work, just made adjustments for personal use.

The Wake Island Battle


Scenario for a single player which takes place on the atoll of Wake Island in the Pacific Ocean, an island rich in natural resources coveted by the great European maritime powers.

The indigenous populations developed equitable exchange relations with French explorers, France having undertaken to protect the island from pirate invasions and the covetousness of other great nations in exchange for the possibility of exploiting the deposits of island gold.

The map is the largest size available and the naval units have been completely reworked: slower, stronger, more expensive, with firepower commensurate with the size and number of cannons of the ship, etc. Vessel construction limits are much greater with the ability to build several huge fleets.

Whoever controls the oceans controls the land.

Second Tikal-Calakmul war  (648-695 A.D.)

 

Hidden in the dense jungle of the Yucatán, the Mayan civilization thrives. Massive stone pyramids carved with images of gods and kings tower above the sea of green vegetation.

The Yucatan is dominated by two city-states, Tikal and Calakmul. Competing for control of the rich trade routes throughout the peninsula, the two cities have warred with each other for as long as anyone can remember.

To secure the jade and cacao trade, Tikal founded the city of Dos Pilas. Now, the old king of Tikal sends his youngest son, B'alaj Chan K'awiil, to rule it while his oldest son will rule Tikal itself.

B'alaj, however, is not happy with his small inheritance. The god of war plants seeds of resentment and hate in him and B'alaj is convinced that would be a better ruler of Tikal than his brother

Do you wish British units actually spoke the English of the Revolutionary War and the Napoleonic Age? Do you find the American voice acting dry and awkward? Do you want to give your AoE3 a fresh coat of paint? Then look no further, because the Better English Voice Over mod is exactly what you're looking for! Featuring over 20 voice actors, BEVO provides new and improved voice lines for English and American units. Over 50 units in the game have been revoiced by volunteers and fans of the game. Trust me, this'll lead to a far more vibrant and exciting experience playing these civs.

Furthermore, many of the campaign hero explorers (Morgan, Lizzy, Alain, Warwick) have been given additional VO from their campaign adventures.

KNOWN ISSUES: Currently, the US Hussar and UK Battleship do not have new voicelines.

ARTILLERY REVAMP

This is a simple mod that allows you to test possible improvements for the artillery of Asian, European, Federal American and Inca civilizations. This mod can be expanded in the future to cover the other civilizations, focusing mainly on the late game.

  • Asian Civilizations: adds technologies to the Castle that allow producing Falconets, Culverins, Mortars and Heavy Artillery using Export.
  • European and Federal American Civilizations: adds a technology to the Artillery Foundry that allow Heavy Cannons to be produced in the Imperial Age, and gives bonuses to the artillery that depend on each civilization.
  • Inca Civilization: Thunderbolts of Illapa technology also enables Light Cannons to be built and upgraded at the Stronghold.

Heavy Artillery (Heavy Cannons, Rockets, Great Bombards and Papal Bombards) don't train in batches.

Asian Civilizations (except Indians)

  • Imported Cannons (III, 100t): Enables Falconets, Culverins, Mortars and Heavy Artillery to be purchased using Export.
  • Improved Cannon Trade (IV, 150t): The costs of Falconets, Culverins, Mortars and Heavy Artillery are split into Export and Coins.
  • Heavy Artillery Engineers (V, 500c): Inviting skilled engineers enables Heavy Cannons to be built at the Castle.
  • Imperial Cannon (V, 1500wc): Imperial upgrade for Heavy Cannon.
  • Mass Production (V, 300fc): Heavy artillery are produced faster.

Indians

  • Imported Cannons (III, 100t): Enables Falconets, Culverins, Mortars and Heavy Artillery to be purchased using Export.
  • Improved Cannon Trade (IV, 150t): The costs of Falconets, Culverins, Mortars and Heavy Artillery are split into Export and Coins.
  • Mysorean Artillery Engineers (V, 1500wc): Inviting skilled engineers enables Imperial Mysorean Rockets to be built at the Castle.
  • Mass Production (V, 300fc): Heavy artillery are produced faster.

ASIAN ARTILLERY COSTS

Without / With Improved Cannon Trade
-Falconet: 363 export / 250 coin 181 export
-Culverin: 363 export / 250 coin 181 export
-Mortar: 326 export / 225 coin 163 export
-Cannon: 580 export / 400 coin 290 export
-Rocket: 435 export / 300 coin 218 export

*The resource conversion I used to calculate the artillery export cost is 100wood = 100coin = 72.5export (using as a reference the Hatamoto Samurai from Japanese Isolation, which costs 290export and is equal in stats to the Ronin that costs 400coin).

British

  • Royal Artillery Engineers (V, 1500c): Inviting skilled engineers enables Rockets and Heavy Cannons to be built at the Artillery Foundry, and makes their upgrades cheaper. All artillery train 20% faster.

Dutch

  • Mounted Artillery Engineers (V, 1500c): Inviting skilled engineers enables Heavy Cannons to be built at the Artillery Foundry. Artillery gets +1.1 speed, +2 Line of Sight and train 10% faster.

French

  • Napoleonic Artillery Engineers (V, 1500c): Inviting skilled engineers enables Napoleon Guns and Heavy Cannons to be built at the Artillery Foundry. Gives all artillery (except Mortars) a devastating close-range attack against units.

Germans

  • Kruttz Artillery Engineers (V, 1500c): Inviting skilled engineers enables Heavy Cannons to be built at the Artillery Foundry. Artillery get +15% attack.

Italians

  • Papal Artillery Engineers (V, 1500c): Inviting skilled engineers enables Papal Bombards and Heavy Cannons to be built at the Artillery Foundry. Papal Bombards moves 10% faster and take -1 population. Artillery get +10% hitpoints.

Maltese

  • Order Artillery Engineers (V, 1500c): Inviting skilled engineers enables Horse Artillery and Heavy Cannons to be built at the Artillery Foundry. Enables Culverins and Mortars to be built at Forts.

Mexicans

  • Chapultepec Artillery Engineers (V, 1500c): Inviting skilled engineers enables Heavy Cannons to be built at the Artillery Foundry. Artillery get +8% hitpoints and attack.

Ottomans

  • Istanbul Artillery Engineers (V, 1500c): Inviting skilled engineers enables Great Bombards to be built at the Artillery Foundry. Ships 1 Great Bombard and 2 Artillery Foundry Wagons.

Portuguese

  • Bombard Artillery Engineers (V, 1500c): Inviting skilled engineers enables Li'l Bombards and Heavy Cannons to be built at the Artillery Foundry. Li'l Bombards moves 10% faster and take -1 population. Artillery get +10% attack.

Russians

  • Tsar Artillery Engineers (V, 1500c): Inviting skilled engineers enables Great Bombards and Heavy Cannons to be built at the Artillery Foundry. Ships 1 Imperial Great Bombard and reduces the cost of Imperial artillery upgrades.

Spanish

  • Segovia Artillery Engineers (V, 1500c): Inviting skilled engineers enables Heavy Cannons to be built at the Artillery Foundry. Falconets, Horse Artillery and Heavy Cannons take -1 population.

Swedes

  • Svea Artillery Engineers (V, 1500c): Inviting skilled engineers enables Heavy Cannons to be built at the Artillery Foundry. Artillery get +15% hitpoints.

United States

  • Artillery Mass Production (V, 1500c): Inviting skilled engineers enables Heavy Cannons to be built at the Artillery Foundry. All artillery cost -10%.

Other European Civilizations: (Mods, etc.)

  • Heavy Artillery Engineers (V, 500c): Inviting skilled engineers enables Heavy Cannons to be built at the Artillery Foundry.

---

This is my pitch for a possible Artillery Revamp. If any developer is interested in my design, please feel free to use it and contact me if you want, I’d love to see some of my content made official. Any questions, suggestions or corrections are welcome.

This mod should work in any language, but with texts only in English or Spanish (Only if the Spanish language is selected).

Note for other modders: anyone can modify this mod, make the adjustments they deem necessary and publish it as their own, feel free to do that, the idea is to be able to test various possible changes or improvements to the artillery, and also have fun with it, etc.

Have Fun!

modName: Rise of Nobuaga - Battle of Okehazama 1560 (by StereoQuasar163 )
description:
How to install : Age Of Empire III Definitive Edition : How to install scenario | custom maps (with subtitle for UK)

 

Battle of Okehazama 1560

 

In 1560, Imagawa Yoshimoto, a powerful warlord who controlled Suruga, Tōtōmi, and Mikawa Provinces amassed an army of 25,000 men to march on Kyoto to challenge the Ashikaga shogunate for control of the country. The army followed the route of the Tōkaidō highway, and crossed from Mikawa into Owari province, which had recently been united by the local warlord Oda Nobuaga. At the beginning of Sengoku jidai Oda clan was one of the minor clans. Imagawa was dominating Japans politics. But Oda Nobuaga would not let Yoshimoto to cross his lands to reach Kyoto.

The 25,000 Imagawa forces quickly overran the Oda's border fortresses of Washizu, Matsudaira forces led by Matsudaira Motoyasu took Marune fortress, and Yoshimoto set up camp at Dengakuhazama, located in the village of Okehazama, just outside of what is now the city of Nagoya. In opposition, Oda Nobunaga could field only about 2000-3000 men.

So Nobuaga had to find other ways to undermine Yoshimotos army. He recruited his most loyal ninja, Hattori Hanzo to infiltrate Yoshimotos camp at night and create chaos so that Nobuaga could easily crush disorganized army. He also needed Portuguese firearms to defeat Imagawa army.

 

Scenario Features - 

  • Stealth mission in night carried out by Ninja and Shinobis
  • Multiple side objectives to increase Nobuagas power
  • Final epic battle of Okehazama

Triples population max cap to 600. Also increases max house build limit by 3x (to allow you to get to 600 pop).

Current Version: 69

ULT = RANDOMIZER + Special Map

EXT RE = Default

To host, move the files from 

Users\%Name%\Games\Age of Empires 3 DE\%Numbers%\mods\subscribed\%Scenario In Question%

To here

Users\%Name%\Games\Age of Empires 3 DE\%Numbers%\Scenario

 

Map is originally from 2009 and left the naming scheme how it was with the addition of Collo. All the triggers for the scenarios are remade and thanks to the new mod proto, more upgrades could be implemented , which was the original intention back in TAD. We thought we got the AOM triggers to work, but no it was through modding, so the scenario entirely was scrapped. It was only untill late 2021 when the new mod protounits were released and began working on it.

Other than that, it plays like regular collo/wollo with additional upgrades from traditional version.

Rules ---

FFA Settings

Don't need to choose civ, only in game

3 Wood = 1 kill

1 Food = 5XP + 1XP (TOTAL XP / 3500)

Place any units in the center, and a wagon spawns. You can escort them back to your base to redeem free resources.

200 Kills, New Units.

800+ Kills, Cheat units. 

Shared Unit training time, meaning you cant walk around in a circle spawning units quickly.

Destroy all enemy camps to win.

There is scaling in the game because base stats for some units such as mercenaries are very high and tends to get very strong quickly. This is more aimed for balance, however it breaks the idea of getting true value of upgrades, for example reaching 100% range resistance on iron troop, 100% melee resistance on Armoured Pistoleer, 4000 HP Zouave, 800 ATK Harquebusier in 15 minutes. It becomes more like 80% Resitance, 1200 HP Zouave, 300 ATK Harquebusier... 

Rule of thumb is mercenary units, critical mass unit (Giant Grenadiers), Zouave like units (no heavy, light infantry identification), glass kite units (Abus etc), elephants, high range/melee armour, exceptionally high hp and attack units.

 

 

==Shop== Generic (Right Circle)

All Unit Combat 45% = 1000 Wood

All Unit HP 15% = 200 Wood

Infantry Speed 5% = 150 Wood

Infantry Attack 15% = 150 Wood

==Shop== Advance (Left Circle)

Infantry Rate of Fire 2% (Max 100)  = 100 Food

Artillery + Grenadier Area Of Effect 0.25/0.15+ = 80 Food *REMOVED

The Battle of Shiroyamatook place on 24 September 1877, in Kagoshima, Japan. It was the final battle of the Satsuma Rebellion, where the heavily outnumbered samurai under Saigō Takamori made their last stand against Imperial Japanese Army troops under the command of General Yamagata Aritomo and Admiral Kawamura Sumiyoshi. The battle culminated in the annihilation of Saigō and his army, marking the end of the Satsuma Rebellion. The Imperial Army's victory consolidated their power, and the Satsuma Rebellion was the last instance of internal mutiny seen in the Empire of Japan.

模组简介：

原拉科塔，豪德诺索尼，阿兹特克，意大利在满足一定时代条件后可以在不同建筑物自动生产独特单位

中国引入了三四种要塞建筑物，具有独特的攻击特效，有概率造成高额暴击，AI会主动运送建造和升级

非洲文明上调了宫殿的属性，AI会在后期研究城市防御科技，加强建筑物战斗力

对大部分文明，修改了部分船运效果，新增和移除了部分船运，以适配本模组

......更多

Immersive Treaty

Dsc @ elegy5275

12/10/2024 v1.2.0

22 civs overhaul, 3 revolutionary civs redesign

Supports English

Do you want to experience the immersive team-based large-scale combat of Age of Empires III Definitive Edition? Or you simply like playing treaty games, but are discouraged by the poor civilization balance? Or are you fed up with the devs being so dismissive and are waiting for the next game update? Then welcome to use the Treaty patch made by Elegy and DinoQAQ.  The original intention of this patch is to increase the team coordination and civilization balance of the game. At the same time, without changing the core features of each civilization, it also adds many interesting new mechanisms that are in line with the characteristics of civilizations. In addition, the 3 revolutionary civs of Napoleon France, Canada, and Finland have also received DLC-level quality updates in this patch, making them playable in standard NR 40 games.

支持简体中文，正體中文

你是否想要体验沉浸式团队大规模作战？或者你本身就喜欢条约，喜欢玩各种各样的文明但是被糟糕的平衡性所劝退？或者只是单纯嫌弃官方摆烂太久，正在等待版本更新？那么欢迎使用由Elegy和DinoQAQ制作的条约Patch。本Patch的初衷即是为了给这个游戏添加更多的团队协调性和后期平衡性，同时在不改变各文明核心玩法的同时也新增了许多有趣且符合文明特点的新机制。此外，拿破仑法国，加拿大，芬兰三个举拳头国家在本patch中彻底重制。

modName: Lifetime of War: The Swedish Stormakstiden
description:
Play through 10 campaign missions detailing the rise and fall of the Swedish empire, from 1630 to 1718. Focused on the exploits of Gustavus II Adolphus and Charles XII, these missions range from standard AoE III matches to timed events to pitched battles. Each mission is based on historical events, though of course gameplay was the first and foremost focus when creating them. The missions included are:

  1. The Lion From the North - Play as Gustavus II Adolphus and lead the Swedish into joining the Thirty Years War, changing the course of the war and even that of history.
  2. Gott Mit Uns - Command the combined army of Sweden and Saxony against the Imperial Hapsburg army on the fields of Brietenfeld, securing Adolphus as the Father of Modern Warfare.
  3. Carolus Rex - Propel to the dawn of the 18th Century and join the new king, Charles XII, in his quest to defend Sweden from the combined might of Denmarck-Norway, Saxony, Poland-Lithuania, and Russia.
  4. Karolinen's Bon - With the Great Northern War fully underway, lead the Swedish elite Carolean soldiers in a bloody campaign across Poland in a bid for the throne.
  5. Killing Ground - At the climax of the conflict in Poland, confront the Saxon army aided by the Russians and bring an end to their power once and for all in one swift, brutal strike.
  6. March Into Russia - With Denmark, Poland, and Saxony now out of the way, the Swedish King is able to turn his full focus on Russia, meeting in battle at the Dnieper River near Holowczyn.
  7. Poltava - After many years of marching and fighting in Russian land, lead the underfed and undersupported Swedish army against the numerically superior and recently modernized Russian army.
  8. The Royal Guard - Now in exile within the Ottoman Empire, King Charles XII seeks to renew the war effort against Russia, though to no avail. Now, lead the small elite force of Livgard to defend their king against an increasingly angry Ottoman host.
  9. Ruina Imperii - Forced out of Ottoman lands, guide King Charles XII back home to Sweden, careful to avoid all the enemies that have renewed hostilities against Sweden.
  10. Long Live the King - Bring the King back home, once last time.

Of course, the missions will be updated once the new Polish and Danish civilizations release, and new features may be added (i.e. potential multiplayer integration). Feel free to leave feedback and recommendations in the comments. Thank you to Sabaton and their album 'Carolus Rex' for the inspiration and exposure to this fascinating page of history many years ago.

modName: Complete  Enhance the Original Campaign Blood Ice Steel  War Chief
description:
这是一个针对原版战役的修复性Mod，帝国时代Ⅲ决定版，新增了很多新国家和新元素，但对于原版战役（鲜血寒冰和钢铁）和酋长战役而言，因为最初一些文明的缺失，导致许多战役文明的科技树是残破和混乱的，例如战酋战役中的XPSPC，殖民地民兵是一个很好的创意，但从始至终的出现让人很出戏，另外还有，诸如华盛顿大陆军中的大量印第安面孔。另外要注意的是，Mod只针对战役进行了修改，对正常对战并无影响。不过还是建议大家不要在联机是使用本Mod以免出现不必要的问题。

English:

This is a completion action for the original campaign, given that with the development of Age of Empires Ⅲ (especially the Definitive Edition), many new countries (such as DESwedish, DEInca, DEAmericans, DEMexicans, and DEMaltese) and other new elements have greatly increased the playability of the game. However, in some of the initial campaigns and their civilizations, due to the initial mix, many campaign civilizations were extremely damaged and chaotic, such as XPSPC in the Campaign of the War Chief, xpSPCColonialMilitia were a great idea, but their appearance from beginning to end was very dramatic. In addition, there were a large number of Indian faces in the Continental Army such as Washington. This module has perfected them. Additionally, it should be noted that mods have only been Mod for campaigns and have no impact on normal battles. However, it is still recommended that you do not use this mod online to avoid unnecessary problems

Hello, I'm Galvinha. Feeling somewhat disappointed with some of the nerfs, buffs, and reworks that certain developers have implemented for various civilizations, I decided to venture into modding with the goal of achieving the best possible balance. I started by working on a rework for the 'Lakota' civilization, my favorite in the game, but I plan to release updates for other civilizations as well. In short, through this mod, I aim to further balance the game by emphasizing each civilization's strengths, ensuring they all have unique gameplay and mechanics. My focus is on enhancing micro-management and making the game better for competitive play.

So, feel free to request new changes for any civilization in the game, and I will consider making them. Spread the mod to your friends and colleagues because, like me, I know that the developers have made some civilizations almost completely useless, as they have only received nerfs since the launch of AOE3DE.

This is my first time making a mod, so I need your feedback and constructive criticism to help me improve in the future. You’ll likely notice some bugs that might slip past me, haha.

NOTE: When you activate mods in the game, you'll be able to play online matches with them. However, you will only see and be seen by players who also have the mod installed. Because of this, it's important that many people use it. To go back to seeing vanilla matches, simply deactivate the mod.

Friend Code (Steam): 393216892

Steam: Galvinha

Age of Independence is a mod project aimed at enhancing revolution gameplay, focusing on improving the treaty experience for the revolutions while minimizing chages to standard. A major secondary feature of the mod is that it makes revolutions available to more civilizations where appropriate. In the future, this project may allow for new revolutions as well.

Currently, it is only a preview, featuring changes for the Haitian, Egyptian, American, and Californian revolts.

Unfortunately, I've had trouble finding balance testers with similar schedules and skill levels. Balance likely isn't where I want it yet, but is a priority going foreward. Please feel free to share recorded games played with this mod to help improve it!

modName: Mr Bank instead of El Presidente
description: Just a joke don't tell me it's dumb or unbalanced

Survival Map OR Settler Massacre.

You probably know it, but if not, Map with NO RESOURCES and ONLY Settlers. Rely on shipments to build economy.

This map was design to be a Mexican Standoff esque map. You attempt to fight your neigbours, but you get third party by other enemies as all land bridges connect to one land mass. 

Despite being a water map, docks are disabled. (Was originally cliffs, but it had constant camera obstructions, so that was scrapped.)

Has 15 map variations with unique designs. 

Other than that, it plays like traditional Survival MAP/ Settler Massacre. 

Has AI Support*** but I only got it to work by giving them a resource boost if the game detects that the player is a computer. Also they will start to build economic buildings at 2 minute mark.

infinite Wanderlust

Federal Name for Unique Units
Work in progress - Open to feedback

COSMETIC ONLY MOD for Mexico unique units. Mexico being a "Federal" Civilisation in contrast to European Civs, I created a mod just to make a visual distinction and add extra “flavor” to differentiate “Federal Civs” from “European Civs.” 


UNITS status change to:

  • Veteran - become ->  State  
  • Guard - become -> Federal 
  • Imperial - become -> Presidential 


"Soldados, Salteador, Chinaco" becomes "State, Federal and Presidential"

  • "Veteran Insurgentes" - becomes ->"Feral Insurgentes".


For Yucatan Only: 

  • "Yucateco Insurgente" becomes "Insurgente Yucateco " (Makes more sense in Spanish this way)
  • “Veteran Yucateco Insurgente” - becomes ->  "Feral Insurgente Yucateco " 
  • “Guard Yucateco Insurgente” - becomes ->"Champion Insurgente Yucateco " 

(Native Like name status).


For Maya Only: 

Guard/Imperial "Cruzob Infantry"  Become "Champion " and "Legendary" Infantry

  • “Guard Cruzob Infantry” - becomes -> "Champion Cruzob Infantry" 
  • “Imperial Cruzob Infantry - becomes -> "Legendary Cruzob Infantry" 
  • “Imperial Yucateco Insurgente"- becomes -> "Legendary Insurgente Yucateco " 

(Native Like name status).


CARDS Changes:

"Queen's Dragoons" Card changes name to "Queen´s Dragones de Cuera" (For Historical reference).


WHAT'S NEXT:

  • Working on adding the Dragoon and other "universal" units (Falconets, Heavy Cannon, hussars, Ships, extra units, etc) to the "State/Federal/Presidential" status.
  • Update Tech icons to unique new Icons/medals for more "Federal Unique Theme"
  • Looking for Historical Information that can be referenced, like "Modragón Cannons", "Saint Chamond", "Dragones de Cuera", etc. I'm open to ideas, not to be super historically accurate but a little more like Historical References in names (For InGame UI/UX reasons)
  • Planning to update the USA status too.

 

EXTRA:

Speaking of history. Discovery Age and Colonial Age names restore in ALL areas. (roll-over text, info, menus, texts, etc.)

Age of Empires II: The Age of Kings (usualmente abreviado Age of Empires 2 o AoE2) es un videojuego de estrategia en tiempo real ambientado en la Edad Media, el segundo de la saga Age of Empires y fue lanzado en 1999. Desarrollado por Ensemble Studios y publicado por Microsoft Games para PC. Este juego esta desarrollado para Microsoft Windows y PlayStation 2.

El 24 de agosto del 2000 es lanzado el primer paquete de expansión, Age of Empires II: The Conquerors

En 2013 se lanzó una versión remasterizada llamada Age of Empires II: HD Edition; que poseía mejoras en la interfaz ############## # en la jugabilidad online. Al que siguieron otras tres expansiones: The Forgotten, The African Kingdoms y Rise of the Rajas.

En 2017 se anunció una nueva versión final del juego, Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition, y se lanzó el 14 de noviembre de 2019.
