modName: Fixed and updated campaigns (not finished yet)
description:
This mod aims to fix some long standing campaign issues, some of them even dating back from the original AOE3 release in 2005. Given that the game has changed quite a bit since 2005, I also made some changes to make it more up to par with the regular DE content.

Current status: Only Act 1 of Vanilla campaign can be considered finished although there may be some issues remaining. I made some quick changes to Act 2-3, TWC campaign and TAD campaign but not much.

INSTALLATION: Just subscribe to the mod ingame, restart your game and you should be good to go! To check if it is working correctly, just start the first campaign mission of Blood Ice Steel. If you see a maltese hospital outside of the fort (where there was a barracks) then it's working.

NOTE 1: this does NOT fix the 8 crossbows shipment in Act 1 to avoid a conflict with the "Crossbow Baaalance Patch" from TheDunamai. Use that mod to fix it.

NOTE 2: Since Update 8 (v1.9), this mod overwrites the main UI files for a minor fix. If you use any UI mod, you may have to change the priority of your mods in the ingame mods manager to make sure the game uses the UI you want

General changes (please see the changelog.txt file inside the mod folder for a complete list of changes):
 - Fixed building animations of many campaign heroes. New animations have been added in the Definitive Edition but a lot of campaign units were not updated
 - Fixed missing sound for some campaign heroes when building a building
 - Added stars on minimap for campaign heroes
 - Some fixes and changes to homecity cards:
   - Removed card level requirements in Act 1-2-3. In legacy AOE3 you used to require level 10 or 25 before unlocking new cards. In DE, this was confusing as it wasn't shown in the UI
   - Cards total number is now fixed at 40 in Act 1-2-3. The deck builder could not add more than 10 cards per age so you were already limited to 40. Now, it is just more clear
   - Some cards have their numbers fixed
   - Cards with purple borders are now infinite cards
   - Some cards are now sent at a different age, to match regular homecities. Some cards, like "Heavy fortifications", had an incorrect age since AOE3 legacy and others just haven't been updated in the DE
   - In Act 1, the fireships shipment icon has been fixed
   - Removed act 2 cards from act 1 of TWC campaign when buying cards. It was too confusing with the new DE UI 
   - In Act 3, the "Advanced artillery" card has been updated to match its skirmish counterpart (since Update 6)
 - Added ability to build some new buildings (I did not test them much, they may break some missions):
   - Taverns in Act 2 and 3
   - Livestock pens in first part of TWC Act 2 (since Update 4) 
   - Saloons in first part of TWC Act 2 (since Update 5)
 - Added ability to train new units:
   - Spies in Act 1-2-3 (since Update 3)
   - Petards in Act 2-3 (since Update 3)
   - Horse artilleries in Act 2-3 (since Update 3)
   - Native scouts in TWC Act 1 and second part of TWC Act 2 (since Update 3)
   - Grenadiers in first part of TWC Act 2 (since Update 4) 
 - Fixed sound for some units 
 - Few techs adjustments here and there 
 - Age-up changes:
  - Added new politicians in Act 1-2-3. Since the DE, it does not make sense to have only 2 politicians. There are now 3 politicians for each age in Act 1, 4 in Act 2 and 5 in Act 3
   - Removed useless Age 5 option in TWC Act 2. It made no sense to have an age 5 without any age 5 tech, and it wasn't available in TWC
   - Set 4 politicians for each age-up in first part of TWC Act 2 (since Update 4). Was between 3 and 5 politcians before, depending on the age-up
   - Replaced Haudenasaunee tribal council with Lakota tribal council for age-ups in second part of TWC Act 2 (since Update 5)
 - Worked around fake players issues
 - Fixed many invalid forbidden techs in a lot of missions. The most notable is age306 ("A pirate's help")
 - In Act 1, you cannot train hoop throwers in the native embassy anymore and hoop throwers are now correctly placed in the artillery depot
 - Replaced revolutionaries by the campaign specific colonial militias in TWC Act 1. You should no longer have two types of revolutionary/colonial militia, except in age3x06 ("Valley Forge") but this is a special case (since Update 1)
 - Hostiles natives now use the Aztecs civilization instead of the Pirates civ in TAD China (since Update 1)   
 - Replaced pirate flag by neutral single-colored flag for the Reno Gang and Hole in the Wall Gang in TWC Act 2 (since Update 7)
 - Added missing regular +2 XP/s trickle in TWC Act 2 (since Update 4)
 - Small fixes and tweaks here and there. A detailed changelog is available in the mod folder

WORKS IN MULTIPLAYER!

A bunch of cringe nations/civilization/fractions (15+): Prussia, Switzerland, Finland, Canada, Belgium, Bavaria, Tatars(tan), Austria, Venice, Scotland, Kongo, Hansa, Courland, Hetmanat, VOC, Burgundy, Sicily, Boers and more in development - Ireland, Bohemia, Pirate Republics.

  • For now, I'm focused on implementing the concepts for the nations—I've been hit by a wave of inspiration in that regard, and the feedback an AI chatbot can provide fuels my interest.

  • Voice lines are very meticulous work; I will only get to them after implementing all the planned nations.

  • I would still prefer not to implement Poland and Denmark from the unreleased DLC; I intend to see what these "liars" will ultimately deliver. Although I won't be making Poland itself, what will become Sicily is my original concept that I initially designed for Poland.

  • Adding AI access to these civilizations is a rather complex task (initially, this was a mod for PvP or coop with friends). The files for customizing the game's AI are significantly different from the rest of the game files, and even then, the AI won't be able to implement some functions anyway (like age-ups via shipments for Courland). For now, Assertive AI is fully integrated into the mod (you don't need to download it separately, its files are already inside). It only affects the basic AI of enemies. AN+ IS NOT COMPATIBLE WITH OTHER AI MODS, NOR WITH AGE OF THE WORLD (BECAUSE IT HAS ITS OWN AI FILES), NOR WITH OTHER GLOBAL MODS.


  • If VOC or any other nation does not appear - either completely clear the 'C:\Users\***\Games\Age of Empires 3 DE\***\Savegame' folder, or find the 'sp_Mao#NationName#' files and delete them specifically. Before starting the game, disable Steam Cloud synchronization.


The creation of this mod began as a simple overhaul of some vanilla mechanics, then evolved into adding new features for existing factions and revolutionary paths. Now it's somehow ballooned into 10+ new 'civilizations' (though that term feels overly grand - half are just German regional variants like Prussians/Bavarians/Austrians/Swiss)

This mod is developed by a single sleep-deprecating clown in their spare time, playtested by a group of equally questionable friends. We make no claims about historical accuracy (though an AI helps with authentic naming) or perfect balance - in fact, you might wake up to six balance patches in one day as we stress-test everything in multiplayer matches. (We could use local versions for testing, but getting my friends to install anything more complex than a steam workshop mod requires divine intervention - so straight to the live site it goes!)

Sometimes the site struggles with updating archive contents - you end up with empty folders after an update. But everything seems to be working now... probably.

NEW CIVS:


Prussia

Settlers are the counterparts of Coureurs des Bois, but are trained for wood instead.
Barracks and Stables have limited numbers, and after construction, they spawn several units automatically.
The Fort is replaced with "Vanguard" – similar to a standard fort but with a Kallanka-like function, allowing it to train or summon Teutonic units (similar to the American "Levy Minutemen"). New Home City cards focus on adding local units to the Vanguard and enhancing them.

Swiss

Settlers are created automatically, and advancing to new ages is done with gold. The Town Center generates gold and increases this value for each building placed nearby. The barracks and stables are limited to a single instance; they train units faster, can upgrade them, and speed up the work of nearby outposts. Outposts can produce all national units. The port creates ships via the Basilica-Style Сards (FakeCards), the market is used to buy resource crates and contains economic 'fakecards', while the arsenal holds artillery 'fakecards'. The Town Center can mobilize settlers – this is something between levy militia and starting a revolution. New cards in the deck focus on increasing the limit of various buildings, improving pikemen, and boosting resources in resource crates (similar to the 'Silk Road' for the Turks).

Finland

Settlers are trained for wood, wood is gathered faster, and all buildings are cheaper. Explorer-settlers are available in limited numbers; they can still collect treasures, but their primary role is to gather Influence from natural resources. Docks create fishing grounds where boats can collect Influence. Each new age provides unique technologies available at the Lumber Camp. The Lumber Camp harvests resources from nearby trees and generates Influence, which is also produced by building houses. Influence is spent to train foreign units via Consulates or supply wagons "Wagon Barracks" (I used Influence instead of Export to avoid introducing the Asian nations' age-up system). New Home City cards include shipments of foreign units and upgrades that transform militia into other units.

Belgians

This сiv is inspired by my own new revolution. Instead of a Stable and an Outpost, there is a Commandery. Factory Wagons are available for construction, and the factory limit is increased to 10. However, each factory built permanently slows down unit creation speed and increases building costs, but it also accelerates age advancements and building construction speed. New cards in the deck ship a foreign unit, add it to the Commandery, and grant a unique bonus specific to that nation (Banks, Haciendas, Feitorias, etc.)

Bavaria

All units when trained provide 50% food and 50% XP instead of XP alone. Taverns provide a free Settler wagon when built and can later train them. Churches generate XP for themselves and allies. New Home City cards focus on improving Taverns, adding new mercenaries to Taverns (similar to the Swedish system but Tavern-exclusive), and increasing Tavern build limits.

Canada

Each Manor slowly produces a Courier. Settlers and Couriers have increased ranged attack but reduced attack speed. Explorer-settlers are available in limited numbers; they can still collect treasures, but their primary role is to gather Influence from natural resources. Each new age provides unique technologies available at the Lumber Camp. The Lumber Camp harvests resources from nearby trees and generates Influence. Age-ups, explorers and militia are shared mechanics between Canada and Finland. A unique variant of the Mountain Monastery is available that passively creates crates of XP/Influence or Moose, and contains trade agreements with other nations like the Consulate, but using Influence. Manors can train foot and mounted militia that don't less HP. All home infantry has penalties to damage/health, which can be removed and upgraded with additional investments. New Home City cards deliver units and transform militia into them.

Tatars

Tatar Khanate. Age advancements are implemented as "season changes"; they do not provide instant resources/units. Instead, they activate a positive effect that lasts for the entire age (and is replaced by another upon the next advancement) and a temporary negative effect that lasts for a limited time (a new, non-random effect starts at the beginning of each age). Instead of houses, the Yurt is available - a multifunctional building that serves as a house, an animal pen, an early Stable, and, like a Torp, it gathers resources from its surroundings. Three early cavalry units are available at the Yurt: a heavy Steppe Rider, a ranged light Keshik, and a melee light Tarhan. In Age III, a full-fledged Stable becomes available, offering a Light Horse Archer, a Heavy Rider with a flail, a Heavy Rider with a meteor hammer, and a Light-Heavy Horse Archer (similar to the Lakota). The Castle provides access to a Heavy Cavalry Torch Thrower and Hand Mortars. Since native infantry is absent at the basic level, a niche Bandit unit - the Steppe Nomad - is available at the local Embassy; they can use a pistol to act as heavy infantry or switch to a bow to become light infantry. A Dance Ground is available, with a unique dance to create Mangudai - heavy ranged cavalry that become stronger in a group, and a dance to generate gold. New Home City shipments are focused on gaining access to Islamic, Buddhist, and Pagan "Churches", on specifically improving certain units, and on unlocking access to Russian/Ottoman/Chinese-mercenary units.

Habsburgs

Explorers can build Watchtowers (Grenzer Outposts), which allow for one-time unit summons. Age-up mechanics function similarly to the US/Mexico, through a choice of Crown Lands of Austria-Hungary or allied Habsburg monarchies. Each choice grants access to new units and resource shipments from that 'State'. Similar to Mexico, early revolutions are available, with different decks depending on the age you revolt from. New Home City cards focus on increasing the unit summon pool and a host of team shipments. Revolution cards unlock Consulate unit shipments. Unit shipments follow the 'Circle Army' mechanic (Germany), but due to their quantity, they mutually accelerate the rotation cycle of similar shipments.

Venice

Venetian Republic. There are two types of settlers: one (Sutlers) strengthens the others (Journeymen); neither can build. Unique construction wagons increase the efficiency of settlers/buildings around them and are available from the Town Center. A unique building is the Merceria, a mini-Factory that can produce Sutlers or resources. The Explorer can construct buildings but does so for coin. In the Barracks and Stable, all units are available in small, limited numbers, but they are very powerful and cost 2 population each. With each shipment, the health of mercenaries passively increases. The concept of new Home City shipments is that all native units are available as a two-time shipment (red frame) and send units beyond the build limit, but if a shipment with a mercenary of the same type is sent, the native unit will be replaced by that mercenary.

Scotland

Hunting is prohibited by default; instead, a starting Berry Orchard is available, followed by either livestock breeding or Mills. Starting from Age III or via shipments, Grain Carts can be created, which fatten sheep and can periodically create new ones for free; each researched technology creates an additional sheep. "Houses reproduce by budding, the main thing is not to forget to build them" – after building a house, a new House Wagon is created from it, and so on in a cycle. Units from the Barracks and Stable require an additional resource cost and have penalties to their stats. Age advancements are implemented as a choice of a Scottish Clan, following African logic (5+1+1+1). Each clan provides a bonus – 2 new shipments added to the deck, a Castle Wagon, and within the Castle itself, access to their unique unit for free periodic training; this unit negates part of the stat penalty for its type and can completely remove it in Age IV. Wallace's Militia can be summoned from Castles and Town Centers; they are very weak individually, but their stats increase exponentially when in a group. Barracks units: Pikeman, Musketeer, Skirmisher, Halberdier, Clinkerne (Shock infantry). Stable units: Hussar, Dragoon, Cuirassier. Highlanders and Mounted Arquebusiers are always available in the Saloon. New Home City shipments are focused on improving the food economy from Farms and livestock, as well as various upgrades for Highlanders.

Congo

The Congolese. At the start, instead of basic settlers, Badia settlers are available (frankly - slaves). They can only build fields and gather resources. Through them, you can order shipments (fakecard) that will replace all Badia with resources/military units/settlers. Badia can be trained at the Bazaar, and they also have a herdable animal mechanic, meaning other players can capture them. Basic settlers are available in smaller numbers and for coin. Age advancements follow the standard African model (5+1+1+1), with new technologies from them added to the Bazaar. The Bazaaris a pawnshop; while converting resources, it also generates influence. Badia can be trained here, and technologies can be purchased for influence. Units in the Military Camp... Even I can't remember their names... Spearman - less HP, more damage than a standard Pikeman; Archer - fires faster, low HP, low damage; Knife Thrower - deals hand damage at range, good early-game shock light infantry; Warrior - high HP, low damage, a combination of a Cossack and a Steppe Rider; Marksman - high HP, medium range, high damage, low multipliers, a Landwehr+Evzone combination; Grenadier - has an armor aura, periodically throws grenades, analogous to the Prussian Grenadier. The following unique units are available at the Palace: Priest - classless infantry, deals damage to heavy infantry and light cavalry, can heal, analogous to the Indian Urumi; Axe Thrower - concept similar to the Maltese Sentinel, but instead of buffs from buildings, gains speed from losing HP, available after a specific age-up, has a technology that improves him but makes him one and a half times more expensive; When artillery is unlocked, a Gatling gun is available instead of a Falconet. New Home City shipments are focused on investing resources in Bazaars, gaining resources for kills or losses, and additional transformations of Badia into European units.

Hansa

Hanseatic League, not a nation, but a trade alliance. They start the game with Hanseatic Merchants – a 4-in-1 settler unit – and can later build them in Age III; each shipment increases their gathering speed by 1%. Houses are completely absent, but instead, with each age, the population cap and Town Center build limit increase (though it doesn't grant Covered Wagons immediately). Early recruitment of Landsknechts is available at Trading Posts, and from Age III, they are available at the Tavern and replace standard mercenaries. The Explorer can place a Mercenary Banner, which acts as a shipment drop point and a training location for Tavern units. The Barracks offers a unique Pikeman with high base damage and a forced march ability – the Knecht, a direct analogue to the Brunswick but available as a standard unit, and a Hand Cannoneer. The Stable provides a unique early Dragoon – the Reiter, and a standard Hussar. Age advancements follow the US model, but instead of choosing a state, you select a city from the Hanseatic League, which also grants access to 2 new shipments for your deck. New Home City shipments focus on increasing the number of Town Centers, the population they support, and passive trickles from them (similar to Portuguese Feitorias, but split across multiple shipments).

Duchy of Courland

Modern-day Latvia. Settlers are divided into 5 different types, each trained at a separate Town Center and receiving additional bonuses when working within its radius. Age advancements are performed by sending a shipment from the Town Center (like a fakecard from a Basilica), which eliminates variability in age-up choices. Each advancement unlocks access to a new Town Center and its corresponding settlers, as well as a new Amber Mine builder. The civilization has access to Export, which is gathered passively and at the Mine (Estates are completely unavailable). The Consulate offers alliances with the suzerain – Poland, the neighbor – Prussia, and two colonies – Tobago and Gambia. The Barracks provides access to a Strelets (Russian), Skirmisher, and a unique variation of heavy infantry – the Šautinieks, a Musketeer that is essentially a Pikeman with a mu##### The Stable offers basic Hussars and Dragoons. The Barracks allows for the training of Amber Guards in limited numbers for Export; these units spread an aura that enhances other Amber Guards or units with the 'Amber Guard status'. New shipments in the deck are focused on strengthening the effects of Amber Guard auras and granting the 'Amber Guard status' to other unit types so they can also receive buffs from Amber Guards.

Hetmanate

Ukrainian Cossacks. Unique settlers have a reduced gathering rate for all resources, automatically regenerate HP, and provide their own population (but still consume population slots). Settlers are trained automatically like the Ottomans, have no build limit, but their training rate cannot be increased. With each shipment, additional settlers appear from EVERY Town Center. The Blockhouse offers foot Cossacks who can mount horses (Pikeman+Uhlan, Strelets+Dragoon), a musketeer with very long range (Serdiuk), and a skirmisher who can entrench to increase range and defense (Haidamak). The Stable is only available in Age III and offers only one heavy cavalry unit with very high base damage (Hetman). In the Tavern, Bandits are available at a split cost of food and coin, have a lower base population cost, but their upgrades are now split into three separate upgrades, like standard units. All unit upgrades, including Bandits, increase their cost and the experience gained for them. Most shipments in the deck have a unique [delayed] property – upon arrival, shipments provide their primary initial effect, and upon the arrival of the next shipment, a secondary additional effect from the previous one is triggered. Sometimes the delayed effect can activate after two subsequent shipments, and sometimes after the next shipment, it can completely cancel the initial effect.

Dutch East India Company (VOC)

Has very expensive settlers, which are trained in batches of 3; a batch costs 100 of each resource. Age advancements are represented by different bureaucratic officials; each advancement has its own standard cost and duration, and a commensurate reward. Asian market upgrades, rice paddies with their improvements, Dutch Banks are available, and the Capitol is absent. In Age II, a Native Barracks is available for hiring military units; it contains unique, cheap units that are considered Native Warriors. Starting from Age III, a European Barracks becomes available, containing all Dutch units (already upgraded to Veterans). The Explorer can receive economic shipments and, from Age III, military ones; they are analogous to Pashas, additionally increasing HP for each technology researched, and can interact with Banks/Factories to improve their resource production. The concept of Home City shipments: unit/mercenary shipments are added to the Bank after being sent and can be re-ordered as a fakecard, paying the unit cost; the units themselves are consulate units. Other economic and infinite shipments are not added to the Bank; instead, they cost double (2 shipments at once) and send twice as many units, improve gathering with double efficiency, send more than twice as many resource crates; settler, artillery, and navy shipments require an additional resource payment and either improve gathering/units or send slightly more units at once.

Burgundy

Duchy of Burgundy. Settlers are trained in groups (like Russians), and for gold (like Dutch). Settlers inherently gather resources from berry bushes faster. Age advancements are represented by political figures (not abstract), each advancement ships mercenaries and grants access to them; the pool of advancements is randomized each time, and mercenaries can repeat. Instead of a Mill and Plantation, a Winery is available, which produces food/coin by choice (similar in efficiency to the Dutch Bank), and can plant bushes with food/coin/experience, creating an alternative and more active economy; settlers cannot work even on allied buildings. National military units are trained one by one, their training speed increases with each shipment, their upgrades are divided into 4 tiers and are independent of Age, but late-tier upgrades in early Ages are much more expensive. All National military units deal siege damage in melee. The Barracks provides access to: Picard Crossbowman – a Yumi analog, Pikeman – a Yamabushi analog, Epéphancer – a Samurai analog, Pomis – an Ashigaru analog, Cortaux – a Shinobi analog, Franche-Comté – a Schiavone analog; the Stable provides access to: Chevalier – a Naginata Rider analog, Mounted Crossbowman – a Yabusame analog... The concept of siege damage in melee is taken from Japan in WoL, so almost all units stat-wise are ####### of Japanese ones. Shipments in the deck send units in an infinite format (to avoid gaining the training speed bonus from the passive); instead of unit upgrade tactics – wine shipments, which increase stats more significantly but temporarily reduce them; cumulative bonuses to production and the number of Wineries/Factories are aimed at bringing passive income to the level of the Dutch.

Sicily

Settlers have a higher base cost and cannot build houses. The Parliament is used to build houses, periodically producing a choice of wagons. Each building has a set of upgrades called [Charters]. Researching a Charter in a building requires a shipment point, which is refunded upon completion. Each Charter researched unlocks a new age-up option related to that building. Unexplored Charters are lost in the new age. Some cards in the Home City deck also have the [Charter] tag. Upon arrival, they activate all additional effects associated with researching Charters in buildings. Military units are trained in contingents (similar to the Chinese). They have base stat bonuses, but the cost of contingents is split across all three resources. The primary economic building is the Feudo - a combination of a Mill and a Plantation that can gather two types of resources simultaneously, including Wood. New Home City shipments are focused on long-term benefits: Resource trickles instead of Settler shipments, Resources delivered with each age-up instead of resource crates, Additional units with each subsequent shipment instead of large, one-time unit shipments, Weak but infinite versions of standard upgrades instead of basic tactics.

 

NEW REVOLUTIONS:

Greeks 

Available to Italians, Maltese, Balkans and Ottomans
Settlers become Sacred Band. Royal Embassies begin to provide passive gold and exp. Deck focused on native units and naval economy.

Belgians

Aavailable to Dutch, French and Germans
Stables become commanderies, the civ loses access to its original cavalry. Each supply brings an additional wagon for the Commandery. Deck focused at obtaining consular units and passive resource trickle.

Irish

Available to British and Swedish
Settlers become analogous to Courier-de-Bois, reduce food and wood production speed by -33%, increase gold production speed by +50%. Barracks become mercenary camps, and taverns become saloons. +40% to health and damages of mercenaries. Deck focused on mercenaries.

Manchus

Available to Russians
Deck focuses on turning original units into Asian units, with each subsequent shipment sending additional units after the transformation.

Austria

Available to Habsburgs
Economic improvements.

"New" Hungary

Available to Habsburgs
Military improvements.

Austria-Hungary

Available to Habsburgs
Combination of the previous two, and naval improvements.

P.S.

For these new civilizations/nations, I drew inspiration from classic AoE3 mods like Napoleonic Era and Wars of Liberty. I've also adapted concepts and assets from AoE3DE mods including: South Slavs Civilization, Eastern Europe Civilizations, Basic European Civ (Swiss), Age of the World, AGE OF WORM 2.0, and Age of Pirates.
This mod gives the AI personalities silly names.

This mod allows you to send Home City Shipments to your Explorer or civ-equivalent hero unit.

(v2)

This mod makes the Team Castle Rickshaw cards actually send everyone Castle Rickshaws.

This is accomplished by adding a new building called "Asian Castle" which duplicates the Japanese castle.
When the Japanese player sends "Team 1 Castle Rickshaw" or "Team 2 Castle Rickshaws" every player now gets Castle Rickshaws.
Non-Asian civs can build the Asian Castle from the Castle Rickshaw.

Please note that the AI doesn't understand how to use this and ignores it (patch for this might come later).
This mod makes military units automatically attack nearby walls.

This mod removes some restrictions on wall placement, allowing for the following:
- The straight wall piece (and its gate) can now be built over trade routes.
- Walls from different players can now be joined together.

Note that this mod is a little finicky, and requires very careful placement of the wall pieces to work.

Overhauls the UI to bring back the feel and functionality of the legacy game into the Definitive Edition. From the main menu to the skirmish HUD to the multiplayer interface, the whole UI receives a makeover. 

  • Warchiefs style main UI interface
  • Reworked Multiplayer UI
  • In-game UI varying with civ choice

Stay tuned, spread the word, and enjoy the new interface!

IMPORTANT: Enabling this mod causes a crash to desktop (CTD) when first returning to the main menu. This is expected, and your game should restart without issue. 

Attempting to run this mod with other UI mods runs the risk of causing a CTD. This UI mod is not compatible with other UI mods, and should be kept at the highest priority to avoid issues. If you run into an issue where you can't navigate to the mods page, locate your subscribed mods folder and manually delete the mod folder and entry in the .json file.
A mod which buffs the campaign unique techs. These buffs stick around and stack with other mods.

Overview

ZombieVill Reborn is a 25-round, 7-player vs 1-computer survival scenario. This custom scenario is based on the original ZombieVill made by FireStorm21 in AOE3 vanilla, made from scratch in DE with new units, balance, and fun easter eggs. This scenario is balanced to be played with 7 human players but it is entirely possible to beat solo (Just brace yourself for an uphill battle). Playtime 1hr

Starting the game after downloading this mod

Multiplayer

Host a casual game, then click game mode and select scenario, scroll down until you find ZOMBIEVILL REBORNV1. Select it and click open.

Before starting this game make sure players 1-7 are all set to team 1, with player 8, a Computer AI being set to team 2. Starting age: Exploration Age (For Balance), Difficulty: does not matter, Starting Resources: Standard. Please note, if you cannot fill all 7 survivor slots with Human players, Computer players can be used, but will be removed at the start of the game.

Singleplayer

On the main menu select Skirmish, Then select Map, at the top select Custom Scenario, and scroll down until you find ZOMBIEVILL REBORNV1. Select it and click open.

Before starting this game make sure players 1-7 are all set to team 1 with player 8 a Computer AI being set to team 2. Starting age: Exploration Age (For Balance), Difficulty: does not matter, Starting Resources: Standard.

Gameplay

At the beginning of the game, you will start out with a Survivor, an outpost wagon, and 3 food. Food represents the number of lives your explorer has left. You will have 30 seconds to prepare before the first round starts.

At the start of each round Zombies will emerge from the blue ice located on the map, defeat all of the zombies to progress to the next round. Each zombie you kill will reward you with coin, which varies based on the zombie type.

This coin allows you to buy units and upgrades to help your survivor defeat increasingly difficult rounds.

Some units and upgrades are locked off behind “pay walls”, to unlock these units and upgrades you must have the amount of coin required by the Depot, this will vary from 100-400 coin. Once you have the amount of coin required simply stand next to the depot to unlock the new area.

Some upgrades and units are hidden, be on the lookout for ways to unlock a hidden area!

 

Please reach out of you notice any bugs or crashes, or if you have balance suggestions

Thank you all for playing!

Special thanks to Joshua667746 for the AI help

New building concept: Wildlife Trade Market.

A trade building with the following powerful military and economic features:

- can train exclusive pets and upgrade any kind of pet.
- sends infinite shipments of wild huntable animals and shoals of fish.
- provides outstanding unique improvements for your exclusive pets.
- generates a small amount of resource and can be configured to produce different types of resources.
- attracts nearby wild huntable animals for bonus production of resources.
- supports 25 population.
- heals idle pet units.

A unique mercenary building that can train a number of random mythological creatures as mercenaries and heroes.

This collection contains 100 maps edited to vary the survival gameplay style, perfect for those who love maps with limited or no resources. Each map requires distinct strategies, so explore and try out new techniques. Immerse yourself in unique challenges and diverse strategies on each of these maps:

1. Alaska: Frozen terrain with tundra plains and glacial hazards.

2. Amazonia: Navigate through a dense tropical terrain with a river.

3. Andes: Survive the rugged mountain terrains.

4. Arcae Fortunae: Random resource crates appear across the map.

5. Arctic Territories: Frozen terrain with slick surfaces and biting winds.

6. Arena: Face off in a confined battlefield.

7. Argentaria: Capturable banks grant coins to fuel economy.

8. Bahia: Explore the coastal landscape and devise tactics.

9. Basinfall: Sudden drops and shallow waters that disrupt movement.

10. Basilica: Capturable basilicas provide experience points to boost progress.

11. Bayou: A maze of swamps and narrow channels that restrict movement and construction.

12. Bengal: Low, humid lands shaped by winding rivers and stretches of mangrove.

13. Borneo: Tight island layout with narrow paths and limited maneuvering space.

14. Caribbean: Group of islands with shallow waters.

15. Carapax: A turtle‑shaped archipelago with scattered island plates.

16. Cascade Range: Two large water sections split the map near its center.

17. Cattleland: Strategize in this open plain where cattle roam freely.

18. Central Asia: A vast crossroads of cultures and landscapes shaped by nomads and open steppes.

19. Ceylon: Explore the diverse landscapes of this island map.

20. Classic: Experience a traditional survival map with ever-changing lands.

21. Colorado: Mountainous landscape with high plateaus and elevation shifts.

22. Couloir de la Mort: A deadly corridor with confined terrain in the Alps, a perilous mountaineering passage famed for its danger.

23. Crate Battle: Fight to control the supply crates concentrated at the heart of the map.

24. Dakota: Expansive prairies with gentle hills and open horizons.

25. Deccan: A sunbaked plateau marked by rugged contours.

26. Desertum: A barren battlefield split by a single narrow passage.

27. Dojo: Capturable dojos grant military advantages.

28. Domus Tributii: Capturable houses generate trickle resources.

29. Eerie Marsh: Beware the abandoned buildings, beasts, crocodiles, and cadavers in this haunted marshland.

30. Equilux: Shifting light cycles alternate between day and night.

31. Ethiopia: A desert landscape with a central plateau challenges survival.

32. Fabricarium: Navigate through a chain of factories.

33. Fertile Crescent: A historic arc of rich lands where ancient civilizations first took root.

34. Florida: Adapt to the swamps, beaches, and tropical terrain of the Sunshine State.

35. Fluxus Aureus: Capturable mines generate coin income.

36. Fortress: Defend your stronghold with walls and towers against relentless foes.

37. Fraser River: Survive along the winding river with challenging terrain.

38. Frostland: Endure the harsh, cold environment.

39. Gibraltar: A landmass surrounded by water where control of key positions determines dominance.

40. Grand Canyon: Battle through deep ravines and towering plateaus.

41. Great Barrier Reef: Survive among scattered islands and coral reefs, where naval control is essential.

42. Great Lakes: Survive around the vast lake with challenging terrain.

43. Great Plains: Vast open terrain with little cover.

44. Guianas: Coastal lowlands with muddy terrain and scattered hills.

45. Hausaland: A land of settlements and ancient trade routes.

46. Hispaniola: A Caribbean island with coastal edges and varied interior elevation.

47. Hokkaido: Survive the diverse terrains of Japan's northern island.

48. Honshu Regicide: Protect your regent in this Japanese-themed map.

49. Indochina: Coastal lowlands with river deltas connecting to the sea.

50. Indonesia: A tropical archipelago of scattered islands separated by shallow channels.

51. Kamchatka: A peninsula with rugged terrain, uneven slopes, and harsh northern ground.

52. Korea: A broad plain with gentle rises and rocky coastlines that define natural pathways.

53. Low Countries: Adapt to flood-prone plains and complex waterways.

54. Manchuria: A land of broad plains and open northern terrain with gentle rises.

55. Mantarrago: A manta‑shaped archipelago with wide lateral paths.

56. Melanesia: Navigate tropical archipelagos, known for their diverse cultures and volcanic islands.

57. Mercennarius: A map designed exclusively for mercenary‑based civilizations.

58. Minas Gerais: Vast highlands with uneven plateaus and rolling hills, marked by rocky formations and traces of gold-bearing terrain.

59. Miniclash: A tiny map with extremely limited space for expansion.

60. Mississippi Delta: Alternating stretches of land and water.

61. Missouri: River crossings shape the flow of battle in this close terrain.

62. Molendinum: A vast plain with windmills on the horizon.

63. Natura Fallax: A land of illusions where every resource is a decoy.

64. Nemus Falsum: A forest filled only with trees that yield no wood when chopped.

65. Northwest Territory: Remote land with rivers and narrow passages.

66. Orinoco: Navigate the river and its challenging surroundings.

67. Panama: Control key positions along the canal as you adapt to limited resources.

68. Pampas: Flat plains with dry grass and long horizons.

69. Parallel Rivers: Strips of water separate the land, shaping distinct paths.

70. Patagonia: Expanses of land interrupted by bodies of water.

71. Peregrinus: Start with extra free villagers for a boosted early game.

72. Pirates' Bay: Take on the challenge of surviving in this pirate-infested bay.

73. Pluvia Sanatrix: Healing rain falls over select zones, restoring wounded units.

74. Plymouth: Relive the historical landing with survival challenges and restricted supplies.

75. Polynesia: Explore and survive among the islands of this tropical paradise.

76. Praetorium: Begin with a Command Post offering randomized technologies.

77. Punjab: A vast land shaped by ancient rivers and historic settlements.

78. Rail Canyon: A rugged canyon crossed by rail tracks, forcing battles along narrow paths.

79. Regentbane: A ruthless battleground where the fall of your Regent marks the end.

80. Revolution: All civilizations begin in Revolution mode, creating a unique and fast‑paced dynamic.

81. Rockies: Survive the unforgiving mountain range with elevation shifts.

82. Rocky Grotto: Explore this rugged cave with challenging paths.

83. Saguenay: A landscape with a lake at its edge that changes appearance with the seasons.

84. Siberia: Endless snowy expanse with permafrost and biting winds.

85. Silk Road: Traverse the ancient trade route with survival challenges along the way.

86. Sonora: Desert region with rocky surfaces and dry valleys.

87. Texas Frontier: Experience the wild west's harsh terrains.

88. Thesauri: Search for treasures in various challenging terrains.

89. Tormentum: Face the challenge of powerful fixed guns.

90. Tria Opum: Mango groves, mines, and cherry orchards spawn randomly.

91. Tumuli: Explore the ancient mounds filled with mystery.

92. Turrium: Survive in a landscape dominated by towers.

93. Twin Sisters: Mountainous terrain inspired by the twin peaks of Colorado.

94. Unknown: Each game is a new adventure with randomly generated terrain.

95. Valetudinarium: Capturable field hospitals heal nearby units and allow training of surgeons.

96. Via Commercii: Square trade routes at map center.

97. Vulcanus: Navigate this volcanic terrain fraught with natural hazards.

98. Yamal: Survive the freezing tundra and permafrost challenges.

99. Yellow River: Navigate the challenges of this river map.

100. Yucatan: A flat tropical expanse bordered by water on both sides, offering wide open spaces and uniform ground.

Arrow Knight (EXPERIMENTAL)

This is a unit of repute that has quite a bit of discussion on its capabilities, I am using it as a test of implementing possible ways for it maybe not be too extreme one way or another.

EXPERIMENTAL CHANGE - Arrow knight can now use SIEGE ATTACK against ARTILLERY (abusing an lesser known interaction where if you put long range siege infantry in melee mode, they will use the siege attack against ships instead of the normal attack, I have made it so that Arrow knights do the same against artillery, and since the siege attack has higher priority then the normal range attack, this should mean that it will always try to use siege attack against artillery)

Heavy infantry tag removed

Siege damage increased from 39 to 40, siege range increased from 30 to 34.

Range attack decreased to 6

HP increased to 215, range armour decreased from 40 to 20

Multipliers against artillery, Cav and Shock infantry Removed

The idea here is to allow it to do its 2 roles more seperately and cleanly rather then leading to the situation where buffing it against artillery makes it clear units too easily. Noted that with its current siege damage, it actually has less then half DPS against artillery then non-patch ( due range attack multiplier and 1.5 ROF) but has longer range to compensate

Increased HP to allow survivalbility against splash damage (even if artillery deals 0.5 damage, the splash can offset that) and melee

If this is a decent idea ,could be a good change for other high siege units.

Tech Changes

Big Button changes - A bunch of big button have been merged with the temple support shipments in order to make these effects more accessible and decreased the amount of must cards in decks, also some changes.

War Song (Nobles' Hut) 

Now delivers 5 Arrow Knights as base and 1 every 3 mins (total 15) and gives the following

  • +20% damage (same as before)
  • 1 AOE to all range attacks
  • In melee mode, will prioritise attacking artillery ( think Italian Bersagliere Alpini card)

Now the unit does less damage but do some amount of small spread damage against units and can better focus against artillery)

Barometz (War Hut)

Now delivers 5 Puma Spearman as base and 1 every 3 mins (total 15) and gives the following

  • +25% Speed
  • +25% HP 

They can now keep with with coyote for a different complement unlike Jaguar

Cinteotl Worship (Farms)

Now delivers 5 Eagle Runner Knight as base and 1 every 3 mins (total 15) and gives the following

  • +6 range (for a total of 18) 

Cant think of anything much here, more range means they can stay further away, there are also other changes.

Coatlicue Worship (House)

Now delivers 5 Otontin Slingers as base and 1 every 3 mins (total 15) and gives the following:

  • +25% HP
  • +2 range and LoS

No changes to the effect here

Founder (Community Plaza)

Instead of increasing the War Chief XP bonus from x2 to x3, now increases general kill bounty by 50% ( It feels prety bad losing war chief with the x3 aura, this way under the aura, the increased XP bonus is still the same (1.5 x2 = 3) but if you lose the War Chief its not a complete loss.

AGE UP CHANGES

The Chief 

Now upgrades the Cuachic Ascension attack into a charge attack and improves the bonus it gives, which in addition to faster attack speed for the war chief also increase other unit speed and attack speed by 5% for 15 second) - Cooldown is 60 seconds irrc

In addition, instead of locking in the bonus HP and damage of the special attack with the age up, now gives scaled increase with every age up as a shadow tech 

Age 2 (+20% HP, 25% Special attack damage and +1 AOE)

Age 3 (+10% HP, +25% Special attack damage)

Age 4 (+10% HP, +25% Special attack damage and +0.25 AOE)

Age 5 (+10% HP, +25% Special attack damage and +0.75 AOE) 

In total you get an extra +50% HP, +100% special attack damage and +2 AOE (same as getting the age 5 version of the tech before) this was just a never used option and I want to experiment

The Elder

Now gives +30% stats to War Hut and Nobles' Hut with every version of the age up

Just want to try this one, can make this scale if needed

The Wise Woman

Now gives 20% gather rate to Farms, and Estates on every age up ( and also boost Fields, Rice Paddies, Mills, Hacienda (and the Hacienda autogather) on every age up cause again it feels bad to lose out on it if you decide to go for it earlier

Age 3 onwards sends Homestead Wagons, which can make both Farms and Estate instead of just Farms.

CARD CHANGES (Long list coming)

Cards Added

Native American Allies Aztec

Like Inca version, but unlocks Maya, Zaoptec and Carib. This gives Aztec a plethora of eco (Maya and Zapotec) and also combat upgrades (either +20% HP on basically everything or +20% hand infantry Damage or +10% hand infantry and foot archer damage and increased damage against vills on otontins and Eagle Runner Knights - this is also why Eagle Runner Knights are Foot Archers now)

10 Coalition Allies (500 Gold)

Sends 5 Zapotec Lightning Warrior and 5 Mayan Javalineer, Can be sent twice

24 Coalition Allies (1000 Gold)

Sends 12 Zapotec Lightning Warrior and 12 Mayan Javalineer, gives Elite, Can be sent twice

18 INF Coalition Allies (1000 Gold)

Sends 9 Zapotec Lightning Warrior and 9 Mayan Javalineer, gives Elite and Champion

Basically want to adjust the famous rush abit in a way that reduces the spam potential of sending multiple shipment but still keeping a bit of its power. In the late game version sending 9 of each makes it so that you can send quite a bit of them before hitting the unit limit cap

Cards Removed 

Temple of Centeotl Support (merged with Coatlicue Worship) 

Temple of Coatlicue Support (merged with War Song) 

Temple of Xochipilli Support (merged with Baromezt)

Temple of Tlaloc Support (Merged with Cinteotl Worship)

11 Mayan allies 

9 Zapotec allies

24 Mayan allies

24 Zapotec allies

17 inf Mayan allies

17 inf Zapotec allies

Stone Towers

Card Changed

Aztec Fortifications

Added effects of stone towers to make it more reflective of its backend name (extensivefortificationsaztec)

Tollocan Garrisons

Added effects of War Hut Training make it more like Haud Advanced War Hut

############# of Xolotl Support

Now buffs gather rate for Fields, Rice Paddies, Mills, Hacienda (and the Hacienda autogather) as well

Religious Unity

Now Reduce gold cost of all home city shipments by 40% (including infinite shipments) instead of 50% for non-infinite ones and 25% for infinite ones.

Old Ways

Like Haud version, Makes all Big button 0 cost and unlocks special big button (Inf 12 Jaguar Prowl Knights)

Agrarian Ways

Like Haud Version, moved to age 2 and now makes cost of farm and estates upgrades free but makes it slower.

Inf 600 Gold

Now sends additional crates with every age up after its starting age (+200/+200/+200) for total of 1200Gold in Imp

Inf 900 Gold

Now sends additional crates with every age up after its starting age (+200/+200) for total of 1300Gold in Imp

Inf 1300 Gold

Now sends additional crates with every age up after its starting age (+100) for total of 1400Gold in Imp

These changes again are more so that it feels less bad being locked into 1 of these 3 choices depending on the game plan or game mode. Lategame the Inf 1300 Gold is still the best for treaty purposes. May also consider doing the same to the 300 gold version but that one is shared amongst civs so harder to touch

Privat use only

IMPORTANT: You do not need any other versions of the legacy UI installed to play this. Enabling this mod causes a crash to desktop (CTD) when first returning to the main menu. This is expected, and your game should restart without issue.

If you run into an issue where you can't navigate to the mods page, locate your subscribed mods folder and manually delete the mod folder and entry in the .json file.

With the abandonment of developer support, it's time for the fans to take a crack at fixing some of the more obvious errors. My goal is to fix some of the most obviously broken balance issues, bugs, and inaccuracies so that the game is left in a more acceptable state and not just a buggy battle between bottomans. I'm also adding a some additional flavour to some of the more underpowered cards and making other miscelaneous improvements

Units & Buildings

Abus Gunner
- Removed light cavalry ranged multiplier
- Changed melee multiplier to all cavalry
Azap
- Reduced range by 2
Humbaraci
- Reduced range to 12
- Reduced speed to 4.25
- Reduced ranged attack to 17, melee attack to 26, siege attack to 42
- Reduced artillery multiplier to x1.5
Deli
- +5 food cost
Bashibozuk
- Reduced levy quantity to 5 (from 6)
- Increased ranged attack to 30, siege attack to 6
- Reduced siege RoF to 3
- Reduced villager multiplier to x0.25
- Reduced heavy infantry and mercenary multiplier to x1.25
- Increased siege unit multiplier to x1.75
- Increased health to 215
- Now affected by the Akinci card (increase villager multiplier to x1)
Chinaco
- Removed heavy cavalry multiplier
Sacred Cow
- Now uses Zebu model
Royal Musketeer
- Decreased melee resistance to 20%
Sebastopol Mortar
- +2 LoS in age 3 (32>37>42)
- Explosive Shot:
  - Increase damage to 75
  - Increase artillery multiplier to x1, infantry multiplier to x5
- Solid Shot:
  - Increase artillery multiplier to x0.3
Arrow Knight
- Changed tags from heavy infantry to ranged shock infantry
- Artillery multiplier vs Arrow Knights removed
- Base range reduced to 27 (+1 with each upgrade)
- RoF changed to 3
- Attack increased to 17
- Artillery multiplier increased to x7, cavalry multiplier removed
- Resistance changed to 20% siege, 20% ranged
- Health increased to 175
Black Rider
- Reduced ranged attack to 37 (from 40)
- Reduced melee attack to 17 (from 18)
Hand Mortar
- Added x0.5 multiplier against training ships
Monitor
- Now tagged as a training ship (prevents absurd multipliers for Culverins and Hand Mortars)
Cannon Boat
- Now tagged as a training ship (prevents absurd multipliers for Culverins and Hand Mortars)
Trading Post
- Build time increased by 25%
Blockhouse
- Chinese version now only trains Castle armies but now also contains Castle upgrades
Line Infantry
- Renamed to Whitecoat
- Stances reworked
  - Volley: 23 attack, 3 RoF, 13 range, resists 0.15 ranged
  - Stagger: 23 attack, 3 RoF, 13 range, resists 0.15 siege
  - Melee:13 attack, 1.2 RoF, resists 0.15 melee (default is 16 attack, 1.5 RoF)
  - Defend: 23 attack, 3.3 RoF, 13 range, resists 0.2 ranged & 0.1 melee
  - Stand Ground: 23 attack, 3.3 RoF, 16 range, resists 0.15 ranged
Royal Huntsman
- Renamed to Royal Warden
- Gains and additional 10% yield when gathering
- Can now also chop wood
- No longer transforms to Royal Hunters with the League of Armed Neutrality technology
Royal Hunter
- Removed access to this unit
Wild Boar
- Now has its own model instead of using the Warthog model (credit to InnateJaguar)

Cards & Techs

Acemi Oglan School (Ottomans)
- Cost reduction reduced to -15% (from -20%)
Topcu Corps (Ottomans)
- Increased Great Bombard train time by 5%
Akincis (Ottomans)
- Also increases Bashibozuk villager multiplier
Mosque Construction (Ottomans)
- Reduced discount for Palace Intrigue technologies to 35%
Grapeshot (Portuguese)
- +0.5 AoE to all artillery (except Culverins)
- +0.25 infantry multiplier to Organ Guns
- Removed effect on packing and RoF
Ordenanca (Portuguese)
- Training time reduction lowered to -15% (from 30%)
Jesuit Influence (Ethiopians)
- Moved to age 1
Prize Bull (Ethiopians)
- Big Benny now has 700 food
Great Temple of Quetzalcoatl Support (Aztecs)
- Now correctly sends 12 Jaguar Prowl Knights

  - Akan from alliances on the map no longer have their portrait updated

- Does not increase Royal Warden population because they already have a cost
12 Oldenburg Allies (Russia)

Civilization Balance

Russia
- Starting food crates consolidated to 1 large food crate (total remains 500)

Welcome to my fake civ, the Bauhemians!

This is my first attempt at modding Age of Empires 3 using additive modding strategies in 2025. There were some sources online that helped me learn, but I primarily learned how to do this from looking at the resource manager. That was the quickest way for me to understand what to change and how to change it. Hopefully anyone else who downloads this mod can do the same by looking at the files in their mods folder, in addition to the other mods out there. Everything in this mod is how I learned modding works in 2025. When making this civ, I jammed the things together I liked, regardless of balance, as I wanted testing to be really fun.

Now, to the civilization, rather, the town of Astoria, Oregon.

New Civ: Bauhemians (multicultural)
Homecity: Astoria, Oregon, US
Explorer: Enrico Nicholai DiGiovanni
Flag: Astoria, Oregon flag from the late 1800s

Bonuses:
- Always have access to advanced Arsenal technologies
- Build Banks to automatically produce coin
- Levy/Conscription can be used repeatedly but with a long global cooldown between each use
- Always have access to Mercenary Hand Canoneers at the Tavern
- Have access to 40 cards but CANNOT revolt (sorry Lion Heart, next civ will be more revolting, I promise!)

Royal Guard Units:
Pikeman (Tercio), Rodelero (Espadachin), Ruyter (Carabineer)

Unique Units:
Longbowmen, Dopplesoldner, Arquebusier, Missionary, Cossack, Ruyter, Leather Cannon, Organ Gun, Flamethrower, Morutaru, Canoe, Sloop, Fluyt, Fushwan (improper spelling because kept getting hashed out even though is the name of the unit??), Rocket

Unique Buildings:
Banks

CUSTOM CHURCH CARD (combination of existing church cards): 
Bohemian Bauhaus -> Grants access to the following techs
- Young Guard -> Middle Guard -> Old Guard
- Black Watch
- Wild Geese -> Waloon Guards

The Kingdom of Bauhemia began as an idea, a balancing of concepts that would revolutionize the known world. Being a cultural melting pot of estranged peoples, the Bauhemians are known for their archaic tactics and extensive use of mercenaries and natives. They also have many ways of sustaining themselves for the long term. The culture is multilingual, so you will hear different units speak different languages (by design, I think it sounds really cool, like a real melting pot civ).

modName: The India companies : Semi-versus world map
description:
A semi-versus scenario for two, three or four players.

Play a European civilization and claim the most trading posts around the world. But beware, the natives are defending their land.

Rules : 

Each player must choose one of these civilizations: British, Spanish, French or Dutch. And no two players can take the same civilization.

Each player starts with a city in Europe that is indestructible. You can't build houses, but each trading post gives you 10 population.

Ships cannot attack. They are only used for exploration, training and transport.

The game lasts 45 minutes. At the end, the player with the highest score wins the game. 
(In practice, the game ends in defeat for all players. Just consider yourself the winner if you have the highest score).

Game settings in multiplayer :

-Check the "Free for all" option box. But you can make teams if you want.

-The fifth player (Indians) must be an AI. He represents the natives. No matter what team he is, he will be enemies with the players (this was coded in the scenario).

-If you are playing with fewer than four players, fill the empty player lines with AIs from civs that are not being played (British, Spanish, French or Dutch).

-If you set the difficulty to Hard or higher, the game will last 1 hour instead of 45 minutes.

Multiplayer bugs :

-If the button to change civilization bugs. Click on ‘Ready’ and then ‘Not ready’. This should unlock it.

-If you have desynchronization issues at the start of the game, ask all players to restart Age of Empires 3.

Singleplayer :

You can play this scenario alone in a Skirmish game (click "Custom scenario" when selecting a map) or by clicking on "Load" (and "Custom scenario") in the main menu.

Siege of Caffa (1345)

 

Caffa (modern-day Feodosia) was a thriving port city on the Crimean Peninsula, established by Genoese merchants in 1266 by a purchase agreement with the Khan of the Golden Horde. The city was strategically situated on the Black Sea, serving as a critical link in the trade routes between Europe and Asia. Caffa's prosperity made it a coveted target for various powers, including the Mongol Empire. The Mongols, under the leadership of the Golden Horde, sought to control this lucrative trading post, which was vital for the flow of goods such as spices, silk, and precious metals.

The relationship between the Genoese and the Mongol Golden Horde was complex, marked by periods of cooperation and conflict. The Genoese had initially secured their trading rights through treaties with the Mongols, but tensions arose due to competing interests and the volatile political landscape of the region. The escalation of these tensions ultimately led to the siege of Caffa in 1346.

The siege of Caffa was characterized by intense military tactics from both sides. After several years of siege, the armies of the Horde were forced to withdraw. The siege is famous for a story recounted by Italian notary Gabriel de Mussis, which attributed the subsequent spread of the Black Death to plague-infested corpses having been launched over the walls at the end of the siege.

 

Scenario Features -  

  • Manage a dying population during siege and plague . Use healers to constantly keep your troops healthy. Dont worry your enemies also suffer from plague. 
  • Use italian naval tactic to deal with much larger enemy navy.

modName: 尝试为所有人带来低保附加MOD
description:
显然，之前的船运强化的肉身兼容性不够理想。

于是我研究了一番，夺舍了一个兼容性更好的附加数据mod。

参考对象是 卢薪丞  的 大清TC结盟科技MOD。

事实上我这个MOD可以跟他的兼容（笑）。

主要修改：到达时代2之后，城镇中心/市场可以研究亚洲盟友科技。

研究后会获得一个原住民使馆马拉车，可以获得 腾格里盟友，少林盟友以及4个低保科技。

分别是：

火热的生产：显著提高生产效率。极大增加船只的生命值。并提供些微船运细流和船运加速。

火炮术：提高火炮单位伤害，极大提高建筑物射程，生命与攻击伤害，顺带提高一下诸葛弩伤害。任意建筑均会提供2人口上限。

骑兵与炮兵操练：显著增加骑兵与炮兵的生存能力和移动速度。所有单位获得少量闲置时生命回复。每次船运额外为所有单位提供少量攻击与生命加成。提供些微船运细流。

高级火力：所有单位获得大幅属性加成，并且所有单位额外获得 雇佣兵 与 土著战士 标签。（标签越复杂，吃的加成越多）（虽然也会吃间谍高额追加，但是电脑正常不会满人口间谍）获得：铁人军，掷环兵，软剑兵与瑞士长枪兵的生产权限。提供些微船运细流。

This mod is a historical oriented mod, set against the backdrop of the line infantry combat mode period from the mid to late 18th century.

In terms of changes, significant modifications have been made to the original camp:

In Europe, the "German" was changed to Austria, and the "Italian" was changed to Prussia, with a total of 10 European camps, namely, Great Britain, France, Prussia, Austria, Russia, Spain, Portugal, the Netherlands, Sweden, and Ottoman;

In Asia, China was changed from a mix of multiple dynasties to only the Qing Dynasty, and India was changed from a mix of Mughal, Maratha, and colonial eras to only the Maratha Alliance;

The American countries retained the alliance between the United States (during the War of Independence) and the Haudenosoni, while the 'Lakota' were renamed as the Dzungar Khanate, a regime that historically belonged to Asia and opposed the Qing Dynasty in China. The 'Incas' planned to be renamed as the Mapuche power, a force that had been fighting against Spanish colonial rule in South America for many years. The' Mexicans' were renamed as the Polish Lithuanian Commonwealth, a major European power that cannot be ignored. The 'Aztecs' planned to be renamed as the Persian Afshar Empire in the future, so players are advised to use this camp with caution;

It is particularly noteworthy that the original faction 'Malta' has been designed as a new concept faction in this mod: Pirates. This faction can choose sub factions after entering the game, and there are three sets of cards that are suitable for each sub faction. Players need to customize the card set themselves. Pirates will cause the game to crash in preloading mode, so turn off preloading mode and use it again.

There are significant differences between this mod and the original game in terms of game settings:

Due to the significantly shortened timeline compared to the original game, the upgrade of troops has been cancelled, and the gameplay is also aimed at saving more resources for combat use;

Remake the mercenary system, significantly reducing the types and data of mercenaries;

The common military branches of various countries have unique appearances;

Added a large number of active and passive skills, unit characteristics, and damage characteristics for different units, such as camel units intimidating cavalry, poisoned units having a certain probability of fainting, and new tactical modes such as archer's rocket mode, artillery's shotgun mode, etc;

The indigenous peoples on the overall map have been replaced, and the indigenous peoples on the Asian map have been changed from religious indigenous peoples to traditional secular regimes similar to those in Europe, such as North Korea, Vietnam, Afghanistan, etc. Voice has been configured for these new indigenous units as much as possible. The types of indigenous peoples on the European map have also been changed.

At present, the mod is still in the stage of continued development and there are still many problems. In addition to some factions being unable to use it, there are also some unit models that do not conform to history, lack of unit avatars, lack of corresponding avatars and models, some units lack voice, new indigenous people lack architecture and unit models, and some factions have AI problems due to significant changes (such as Prussia and Pirates). These shortcomings will be gradually improved in the future. Please forgive me, players. 

I am deeply grateful for the help of many players and other mod producers during the production process of this mod. I would like to express my special thanks to Skytraveler and Atlas▪Stardust for their tremendous assistance in mod design, data and balance, historical setting, and promotion. Without their support, this mod would not have been possible; Module review, Haiwen, Caiyi, and their support for the production theory and promotion of this mod; Thanks to Scaring the Bull, Lawrence's help in creating model textures, and authorized use of model resources; Thanks to the production team of Beilai and their authorized use of the Middle Ages 2 Total War mod Asian Dynasty model resources; In addition, I would like to express my special thanks to my friends at Changchun Mingshan Archery Club. They have contributed a lot to the improvement and internal testing of the mod, especially to club member Yujia and club owner Jiaming. Yujia has redrawn a large number of portraits of Chinese Qing Dynasty units for me, which has been of great help; Mr. Jiaming has made significant contributions to the promotion of the mod and has also assisted in its development and testing. We would like to express our sincerest gratitude to these friends for their help and welcome everyone to come and learn from us offline. And finally, thank you to all players of the Age of Empires and enthusiasts of military history games for your support and assistance. Wishing everyone a great time!

modName: A map where you dont control the villagers
description:
A map where you don't control the villagers (2 players)

(Now also available in solo mode for China !)

Gameplay:

You are a chinese lord defending your country against japanese pirats raids. There are 4 different villages living on your land: Hunters, Miners, Farmers and Fishermen. Each of them collect different resources. In order to build armies, you have to raise taxes in those villages by sending a tax collector. But be careful as if you tax a village too much so that they don't have resources for themselves, they might revolt. The villagers will surender only if they gather enough resources for them, or if you raze their village to the ground.

You must resist long enough until the emperor army arrives. With this help, you should be able to destory the 4 pirat boats and win the game. You can also try to destory some pirat ships before the emperor army arrives. Each ship destroyed means there will be less pirats arriving.

The other player controls the japanese pirats. He has to break through chinese defenses and destroy the 2 castles to win. He can also try to raid on the villages, so that they won't be able to produce enough food for the chinese lord.


BEFORE YOU PLAY ON 1V1:

 - make sure Japan is in team 1 and chineses are in team 2

 - set the beginning age to age 3

 - make sure all other players are China

BEFORE YOU PLAY ON SOLO:

 - open scenario editor

 - load this scenario

 - go to "Scenario", "Play Test Scenario", and play it as Player 2, with Extreme difficulty.

That's it ! Don't hesitate to bring feedbacks

This mod adds a completely custom European civilization: the Swiss. It comes with brand new buildings, units, textures, voicelines, and more. The Swiss have a heavy infantry-focused playstyle, with strong mercenary support but very weak cavalry options. They also feature a unique militia system, new age-up politicians, and a bunch of unique units that set them apart from any other civ. Everything’s made to give them a distinct identity and fresh gameplay. I am a solo dev with a small budget, but I'm happy to look into any suggestions and I'm happy to recieve constructive feedback.

Join our new Discord to find games and discuss the mod: https://discord.gg/PJtmdrM9

Huge thanks to:

-Ahriman204 for the original mod

-Gwentmaster for the custom unit models

A Scenario Map where you are a spy in the city of Florence.

It is an infiltration map. You have to take down your target, without being killed by guards.

There is a lot of traffic in the city, guards patrol in all streets. Guards will try to kill you when they notice you. Civilians won't attack you, except if they are threatened. But if they see you, the will reveal your position to guards nearby.

Civilians and guards will usually notice you are the spy only if they are very close to you. However, some special civilians can notice you even from a distance (towers, the envoy, the dog). When you are noticed, you have to find a place where no one see you to hide your identity again.

There are basically 3 steps on this map. Once you have taken down the target, you have to free a prisonner. Then you will have to defend the city against invasion.

Overall the 2 first missions are very hard (even for me). It is a map where you have to try several times to succeed. Good luck.

How to play ?

Just download the map, and place it in your Scenario file. Then launch the game, and launch this Scenario Editor map.

Horror Map (1v1 or solo)

A map of Hide and Seek:
Some travellers got lost in a very dark and dense forest. But they are not alone in this forest...
Look for survivors and books that will help you find an exit on this map. Hide well from the monsters.

This map is also playable for 2 players: player 1 plays the monsters and try to find and kill each lost traveller. He can detect the presence of player 2 by seeing wood being chopped, or if player 2 go through a glade (for example for rescuing survivors). Player 2 tries to hide and survive long enough, while completing the different missions (saving survivors, collecting books, ...).

Advice:
If you play this map for the first time, try the solo version. If you already know well this map, try it in multiplayer. It should be balanced for players if they both know well the map.

How to play

Solo:
Download the map, and put the ..._VS_AI version in your scenario file. Then don't load it directly (or you will be playing the monsters). Instead, go in scenario editor, load this map, and test it as player 2 with extreme difficulty.

1v1:
Put the 1v1 version in your scenarios. Then host a multiplayer game with this map. Player 1 (host) will be the hunters, and player 2 (guest) will be the hiders. Note that sometimes when player 2 joins, it messes with the teams, so check it so that players 1,4,5 are in team1, players 2,6 are in team2, and the others are in team3.

There is a lot of traffic in the towns, guards patrol in all streets. Town guards will try to take you down when they notice you. Civilians won't attack you, but if they see you, the will reveal your position to guards nearby.


Civilians and guards will only notice you are the spy if they are very close to you. Opposite to the first map (in Florence), towers will not notice you. Dogs can recognise you from a distance. When you are noticed, you have to find a spot where no one see you to hide your identity again.


There is a main quest for all the scenario. After some time, you can also find the side quests all around the map.


Overall, the main quest can be quite hard, so it is adviced to save your game from time to time. Good luck.

  • New Name for the Maaya revolution
  • Updated Names for Maaya units
  • Chan Santa Cruz was a late 19th-century indigenous Maaya State in the modern-day Federal State of Quintana Roo. It was also the name of a shrine that served as the center of the Maya Cruzo’ob Uprising conformed of Native Maaya people and the mixture of Spanish/Mexican blood. 


The “Rite of ########## Cross”

Cruzo'ob cames from the Spanish word Cross and o’ob from the Yucateco Maaya, beside of been a non oficial army but a rebellion on its own conformed of Rebels and Guerrilleros, they adquiere a Religious favor in the rites of adoration for the “Talking Cross”. José María Barrera, found a religious cross on a cenote, creating and promoting the movement for the indigenous people to fight as true Christians against the “duzles” (Spaniards or Europeans/whites)

The uprising took the city (today known as Felipe Carrillo) as it was built and started the uprising that ended in favor of Mexico Federal Army in charge of the President Porfirio Diaz in 1901.


General Changes

  • REV Native Village now is named “Maaya Wíinik” (The name Maaya People gave to themselves. The words translates as “Maaya” or “Maaya People”
  • NEW ICON for the “Maaya Wíinik” for better UI color recognition based on some traditional Maaya clothing and body painting.
  • Cruzoob Infantry becomes Crozo’ob Rebel
  • Cruzoob Infantry Avenger Crozo’ob Guerrilla

NOTE: In reference that the Maaya State do not consider themselves as part of the Mexican Federation, and was in fact, recognized as an independent state by the British Empire and others. The “Imperial/Presidential” status has been changed to “Legendary,” as they consider themselves as a Native Culture.


Buildings

Because the capital city of Chan santa cruz was previously a colonial city of the Federal State of Yucatan, the Fort, TC, Dock and Houses will retain their original “Spanish” or “Mediterranean” architecture. BUT! Stables will retain the Maaya Architecture. This is a reference of the city's original past but mixed cultures. 

NOTE: The Yucateco Maaya culture of this period is a mixture of Spanish, Mexican and other European cultures as well as the Maaya blood and has “no architecture of their own”. The Mayan as an Empire, disappeared almost completely even before the Spanish arrived. This mod ONLY refers to the Mayas in the Yucatan Peninsula, not the complete empire.

ADDITIONAL NOTE: For those who love the Mayan Architecture change upon age-up, just delete the “BUILDINGS” folder inside the “ART” folder. Love you guys.


Artillery

  • Light Cannon renamed as “Cruzo’ob Cannon”
  • FIeld Cannons NEW ICON

NOTE: Many of the firearms the rebels gather where from outside sources, the artillery rename here is just for UI consistency and theme unification.


Cards

Chan Santa Cruz - NEW ICON

The Chan Santa Cruz card was upgraded with the emblem of the “Talking Cross” and added the background color of the Chan Santa Cruz Flag. Same effect, just new names:

  • Yucateco Insurgente becomes “Legendary Cruzo’ob insurgentes
  • Cruzoob Infantry becomes “Legendary Cruzo’ob Rebel”
  • Cruzoob Infantry Avenger “Legendary Cruzo’ob Guerrilla”

 

British Weapon Trade - NEW ICON

In the 1850s, the United Kingdom recognized the Maya state because of the value of its trade with British Honduras (present-day Belize) and provided arms to the rebels at the beginning of the insurgency. In representation of this event, a NEW ICON with the British Honduras Flag replaces the generic British Rockets. Also, added the RedCoats and Rocket portrait for better visual recognition of what the card delivers.

RedCoat renamed as “British Honduran RedCoat” for the Historical reference
Rockets renamed as “Congreve Rockets” for the Historical reference
NOTE: The card keeps its original effects on the Cruzo’ob light Cannon


Some other minor changes could be found here and there. I will leave you to explore and enjoy the Mod. 

Happy Maaya Uprisung! 

The military upgrades were inspired in some of the Mexican Army and Mexican-American War. NOTE: Some artistic liberties were taken to fit the original visual concept of the base game.

PARAGUAYAN REVOLUTION (Available for the Spanish)
-Ships a General, 2 Falconets and 10 Paraguayan Militia (Veteran Insurgentes equipped with Muskets).
-Your Settlers get +25% hitpoints instead of becoming Revolutionaries.

NEW CARDS
-Plunder: Heavy Infantry do more damage to buildings. Enemy buildings you destroy will now also reward you with Food, Wood, and Coin (half the XP bounty for each resource).
-Ybycuí Foundry: Ships 1 Arsenal Wagon and enables Advanced Arsenal improvements. Docks and Military Buildings work faster when an Arsenal is nearby.
-Paraguayan Army: Increases the attack and hitpoints of the General, Heavy Infantry and all Cavalry. Allows you to research the Guard upgrade for Insurgentes (Paraguayan Militia).
-Tacuary: Ships 1 Steamer and allows them to be produced in the Docks replacing the Galleons. Steamers get +1 build limit, +25% hit points and attack, +2 Line of Sight and range.
-Paraguayan Defenses: Enables the Explorer, General and Musketeers to build Forts and Outposts. Forts cost 30% less to construct, are built 30% faster and two more may be constructed.
-Homeland Estancias: Mills generate a small trickle of Coin, and Estates generate a small trickle of Food, +1 Homestead Wagon.
-Peasant Rally: Ships 20 Insurgentes. Insurgente speed increased.

OTHER CARDS: Advanced Mill, Advanced Estates, Pioneers, Unction, Hand Infantry Damage Team, Hand Infantry Hitpoints, Hand Infantry Combat, Cavalry Combat, Peninsular Guerrillas (renamed as "Guerrillas"), Flying Battery, 16 Musketeers INF, 8 Hussars, 2 Heavy Cannons, Factory, 2 Covered Wagons, Naval Combat, 2 Horse Artillery INF.

...

SOME HISTORICAL CONTEXT
-This mod is mainly inspired by the independence of Paraguay and the War of the Triple Alliance (also known as "Paraguayan War", 1864-1870) where Paraguay fought against the Empire of Brazil, Argentina and Uruguay. This war was characterized by massive looting, naval battles on rivers, the use and capture of fortifications, guerrilla warfare, and a combination of regular and irregular forces, among other aspects. Early in the war, Paraguay achieved great and daring victories, but was ultimately defeated by the superior forces of its adversaries, suffering enormous losses.
-“Tacuary” refers to the steamer Tacuary, a name that honors the Battle of Tacuarí (1811), whose name derives from the fact that it took place near the Tacuary River. This steamer was imported from England (1854) and carried between 8 and 10 steam engines in its holds to assemble other ships in Paraguayan shipyards. The Paraguayans manufactured several other steamers using this technology, hence the design of this card.
-In the city of Ybycuí there is an iron casting foundry as well as the repository of arms Minas Cue, named as La Rosada, where arms and military tools were made, besides navy ships components of the Paraguayan navy. During the Paraguayan War, bullets, guns and bladed weapons were made at Minas Cue, but near the end of the war (1869) the allied troops took it and destroyed it. Now at La Rosada, there is a museum where tools used at the time are shown, as well as guns which were manufactured there.

...

December 2, 1805

[EN] The Battle of Austerlitz took place during the Third Coalition, an alliance formed by the United Kingdom, Austria, Russia, Sweden and Naples against Napoleonic France. After the German campaign, Napoleon lured the enemy armies into a position he considered favorable.

Napoleon feigned weakness to encourage the allies to attack his right flank. Once their army was committed, he launched a decisive offensive in the center, notably on the Pratzen plateau, which he had voluntarily abandoned. This move trapped the Allied army, leading to its defeat.

[FR] La bataille d’Austerlitz a lieu pendant la Troisième Coalition, une alliance formée par le Royaume-Uni, l’Autriche, la Russie, la Suède et Naples contre la France napoléonienne. Après la campagne d'Allemagne, Napoléon attire les armées ennemies dans une position qu’il juge favorable.

Napoléon feint la faiblesse pour inciter les alliés à attaquer son flanc droit. Une fois leur armée engagée, il lance une offensive décisive au centre, notamment sur le plateau de Pratzen, qu’il avait volontairement abandonné. Ce mouvement piège l’armée alliée, provoquant sa déroute.

modName: Extra Large Map of North America (Vanilla-Nomad-Conquest)
description:
A pack of four large maps of North America with different rules. For singleplayer, multiplayer, PvP, Coop or PvE.

1-Vanilla map (with town center), for 8 players and variant maps for playing against AIs (4 ais, 5 ais, 6 ais, 7 ais).

2-Vanilla map (nomad), for 8 players. Start at the edge of the map with a caravel.

3-Conquest (natives), for 5 players. Explore and conquer a North America defended by natives. Build trading posts to increase your population and your economy.

4-Conquest (empires), for 4 players. Play an empire already present on the continent after the European colonization: Haudenosaunee, Lakota, Aztec, British, Spanish, French, United States or Mexicans (Please note, two players cannot take the same civilization, this would cause a bug), or a foreign nation (nomad). Conquer trading posts to increase your population and your economy.

The conquest versions are a bit dense with many units on the map. Beware of performance issues, especially in multiplayer.

You can play these scenarios in multiplayer (select Scenario when you create a multiplayer game) or alone in a Skirmish game (click "Custom scenario" when selecting a map) or by clicking on "Load" (and "Custom scenario") in the main menu.
